```tsx
import { useColorScheme } from '@haklex/rich-editor'
import { presentDialog } from '@haklex/rich-editor-ui'
import { usePortalTheme } from '@haklex/rich-style-token'
import { Maximize2, ScanSearch, X, ZoomIn, ZoomOut } from 'lucide-react'
import { Component, type FC, type ReactNode } from 'react'
import { useCallback, useEffect, useRef, useState } from 'react'

import * as css from './styles.css'
import { useExcalidrawData } from './useExcalidrawData'

export interface ExcalidrawStaticRendererProps {
  snapshot: string
}

export const ExcalidrawDisplayRenderer: FC<ExcalidrawStaticRendererProps> = ({
  snapshot,
}) => {
  const theme = useColorScheme()
  const containerRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null)
  const [hasEnteredView, setHasEnteredView] = useState(false)

  useEffect(() => {
    if (hasEnteredView) return
    const target = containerRef.current
    if (!target) return

    const observer = new IntersectionObserver(
      (entries) => {
        if (entries.some((e) => e.isIntersecting)) {
          setHasEnteredView(true)
          observer.disconnect()
        }
      },
      { rootMargin: '200px 0px', threshold: 0.1 },
    )
    observer.observe(target)
    return () => observer.disconnect()
  }, [hasEnteredView])

  if (!hasEnteredView) {
    return (
      <div ref={containerRef} className={css.excalidrawStaticContainer}>
        <div className={css.excalidrawLoading}>Loading excalidraw...</div>
      </div>
    )
  }

  return (
    <div ref={containerRef}>
      <ExcalidrawStaticCanvas snapshot={snapshot} theme={theme} />
    </div>
  )
}

// --- Fullscreen readonly content ---

const ExcalidrawExpandContent: FC<{
  dismiss: () => void
  ExcalidrawComponent: FC<any>
  data: Record<string, any>
  theme: 'light' | 'dark'
}> = ({ dismiss, ExcalidrawComponent, data, theme }) => {
  const apiRef = useRef<any>(null)

  return (
    <>
      <div className={css.excalidrawDialogHeader}>
        <span className={css.excalidrawDialogTitle}>Whiteboard</span>
        <span className={css.excalidrawDialogMeta}>excalidraw</span>
        <button type="button" className={css.excalidrawHeaderClose} onClick={dismiss}>
          <X size={18} />
        </button>
      </div>
      <div className={css.excalidrawDialogCanvas}>
        <ExcalidrawComponent
          initialData={data}
          viewModeEnabled
          theme={theme}
          excalidrawAPI={(api: any) => {
            apiRef.current = api
            setTimeout(() => api.scrollToContent(), 100)
          }}
        />
      </div>
    </>
  )
}

// --- Main Canvas ---

const ExcalidrawStaticCanvas: FC<{
  snapshot: string
  theme: 'light' | 'dark'
}> = ({ snapshot, theme }) => {
  const {
    snapshot: data,
    loading: dataLoading,
    error: dataError,
  } = useExcalidrawData(snapshot)
  const [ExcalidrawComponent, setExcalidrawComponent] = useState<FC<any> | null>(null)
  const [libLoading, setLibLoading] = useState(true)
  const apiRef = useRef<any>(null)
  const { className: portalClassName } = usePortalTheme()

  useEffect(() => {
    import('@excalidraw/excalidraw')
      .then((mod) => {
        setExcalidrawComponent(() => mod.Excalidraw)
        setLibLoading(false)
      })
      .catch(() => setLibLoading(false))
  }, [])

  const handleExpand = useCallback(() => {
    if (!ExcalidrawComponent || !data) return
    presentDialog({
      content: ({ dismiss }) => (
        <ExcalidrawExpandContent
          dismiss={dismiss}
          ExcalidrawComponent={ExcalidrawComponent}
          data={data}
          theme={theme}
        />
      ),
      className: css.excalidrawFullscreenPopup,
      portalClassName,
      theme,
      showCloseButton: false,
      clickOutsideToDismiss: true,
    })
  }, [ExcalidrawComponent, data, theme, portalClassName])

  const loading = dataLoading || libLoading

  if (loading) {
    return (
      <div className={css.excalidrawStaticContainer}>
        <div className={css.excalidrawLoading}>Loading excalidraw...</div>
      </div>
    )
  }

  if (dataError || !data) {
    return (
      <div className={css.excalidrawStaticContainer}>
        <div className={css.excalidrawError}>{dataError || 'No data'}</div>
      </div>
    )
  }

  if (!ExcalidrawComponent) {
    return (
      <div className={css.excalidrawStaticContainer}>
        <div className={css.excalidrawError}>Failed to load excalidraw</div>
      </div>
    )
  }

  return (
    <div
      className={css.excalidrawStaticContainer}
      data-theme={theme}
      data-color-scheme={theme}
      suppressHydrationWarning
    >
      <ExcalidrawErrorBoundary
        fallback={
          <div className={css.excalidrawError}>Failed to render excalidraw</div>
        }
      >
        <ExcalidrawComponent
          initialData={data}
          viewModeEnabled
          theme={theme}
          excalidrawAPI={(api: any) => {
            apiRef.current = api
            setTimeout(() => api.scrollToContent(), 100)
          }}
        />
      </ExcalidrawErrorBoundary>

      <div className={css.excalidrawActionGroup}>
        <button
          type="button"
          className={css.excalidrawActionButton}
          title="Zoom In"
          onClick={() => {
            const api = apiRef.current
            if (!api) return
            const zoom = api.getAppState().zoom.value
            api.updateScene({ appState: { zoom: { value: zoom * 1.25 } } })
          }}
        >
          <ZoomIn size={20} />
        </button>
        <button
          type="button"
          className={css.excalidrawActionButton}
          title="Zoom Out"
          onClick={() => {
            const api = apiRef.current
            if (!api) return
            const zoom = api.getAppState().zoom.value
            api.updateScene({ appState: { zoom: { value: zoom / 1.25 } } })
          }}
        >
          <ZoomOut size={20} />
        </button>
        <button
          type="button"
          className={css.excalidrawActionButton}
          title="Fit to Content"
          onClick={() => apiRef.current?.scrollToContent()}
        >
          <ScanSearch size={20} />
        </button>
        <button
          type="button"
          className={css.excalidrawActionButton}
          title="Expand"
          onClick={handleExpand}
        >
          <Maximize2 size={20} />
        </button>
      </div>
    </div>
  )
}

class ExcalidrawErrorBoundary extends Component<
  { fallback: ReactNode; children: ReactNode },
  { hasError: boolean }
> {
  state = { hasError: false }
  static getDerivedStateFromError() {
    return { hasError: true }
  }
  render() {
    return this.state.hasError ? this.props.fallback : this.props.children
  }
}

```

```tsx
import { useColorScheme } from '@haklex/rich-editor'
import { presentDialog, SegmentedControl } from '@haklex/rich-editor-ui'
import { usePortalTheme } from '@haklex/rich-style-token'
import { Clipboard, Download, Pencil, Upload, X } from 'lucide-react'
import type { FC } from 'react'
import { useCallback, useEffect, useRef, useState } from 'react'

import * as css from './styles.css'
import { useExcalidrawConfig } from './ExcalidrawConfigContext'
import { useExcalidrawData } from './useExcalidrawData'

export interface ExcalidrawEditRendererProps {
  snapshot: string
  onSnapshotChange: (snapshot: string) => void
}

const SAVE_DEBOUNCE_MS = 2000

// ── Dialog content (fullscreen editor) ──────────────────────────

const ExcalidrawEditorDialogContent: FC<{
  dismiss: () => void
  ExcalidrawComponent: FC<any>
  initialData: Record<string, any> | undefined
  initialSnapshot: string
  theme: 'light' | 'dark'
  onSave: (ref: string) => void
  saveSnapshot?: (snapshot: object) => Promise<string>
  onClose: (doc?: Record<string, any>) => void
}> = ({
  dismiss,
  ExcalidrawComponent,
  initialData,
  initialSnapshot,
  theme,
  onSave,
  saveSnapshot,
  onClose,
}) => {
  const { className: portalClassName } = usePortalTheme()
  const apiRef = useRef<any>(null)
  const saveTimerRef = useRef<ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>>(undefined)
  const [isDirty, setIsDirty] = useState(false)
  const isDirtyRef = useRef(false)
  const [storageMode, setStorageMode] = useState<'inline' | 'remote'>(() => {
    if (!saveSnapshot || !initialSnapshot || !initialSnapshot.trim())
      return 'inline'
    try {
      JSON.parse(initialSnapshot)
      return 'inline'
    } catch {
      return 'remote'
    }
  })
  const storageModeRef = useRef(storageMode)
  const [savedRef, setSavedRef] = useState(() => {
    if (!saveSnapshot || !initialSnapshot || !initialSnapshot.trim()) return ''
    try {
      JSON.parse(initialSnapshot)
      return ''
    } catch {
      return initialSnapshot
    }
  })
  const [isSaving, setIsSaving] = useState(false)

  useEffect(() => {
    return () => {
      if (saveTimerRef.current) clearTimeout(saveTimerRef.current)
    }
  }, [])

  const getSnapshot = useCallback(() => {
    const api = apiRef.current
    if (!api) return undefined
    return {
      elements: api.getSceneElements(),
      appState: api.getAppState(),
      files: api.getFiles(),
    }
  }, [])

  const performSave = useCallback((): Promise<void> => {
    if (saveTimerRef.current) clearTimeout(saveTimerRef.current)
    const doc = getSnapshot()
    if (!doc) return Promise.resolve()
    try {
      if (storageModeRef.current === 'remote' && saveSnapshot) {
        setIsSaving(true)
        return saveSnapshot(doc).then(
          (ref) => {
            onSave(ref)
            isDirtyRef.current = false
            setIsDirty(false)
            setIsSaving(false)
            setSavedRef(ref)
          },
          () => {
            setIsSaving(false)
          },
        )
      } else {
        onSave(JSON.stringify(doc))
        isDirtyRef.current = false
        setIsDirty(false)
      }
    } catch {
      // ignore
    }
    return Promise.resolve()
  }, [onSave, saveSnapshot, getSnapshot])

  const handleChange = useCallback(() => {
    if (!isDirtyRef.current) {
      isDirtyRef.current = true
      setIsDirty(true)
    }
    if (saveTimerRef.current) clearTimeout(saveTimerRef.current)
    saveTimerRef.current = setTimeout(
      () => performSave(),
      SAVE_DEBOUNCE_MS,
    )
  }, [performSave])

  const handleSaveAndClose = useCallback(() => {
    void performSave().then(() => {
      const doc = getSnapshot()
      onClose(doc)
      dismiss()
    })
  }, [performSave, getSnapshot, onClose, dismiss])

  const handleDiscard = useCallback(() => {
    if (saveTimerRef.current) clearTimeout(saveTimerRef.current)
    dismiss()
  }, [dismiss])

  const showConfirmDialog = useCallback(() => {
    presentDialog({
      title: 'Unsaved Changes',
      description:
        'You have unsaved changes that will be lost if you close now.',
      content: ({ dismiss: dismissConfirm }) => (
        <div className={css.excalidrawConfirmActions}>
          <button
            type="button"
            className={css.excalidrawConfirmBtn}
            onClick={dismissConfirm}
          >
            Continue Editing
          </button>
          <button
            type="button"
            className={css.excalidrawConfirmBtnDanger}
            onClick={() => {
              dismissConfirm()
              handleDiscard()
            }}
          >
            Discard
          </button>
          <button
            type="button"
            className={css.excalidrawConfirmBtnPrimary}
            onClick={() => {
              dismissConfirm()
              handleSaveAndClose()
            }}
          >
            Save & Close
          </button>
        </div>
      ),
      portalClassName,
      theme,
      showCloseButton: false,
      clickOutsideToDismiss: false,
    })
  }, [handleDiscard, handleSaveAndClose, portalClassName, theme])

  // Intercept ESC in capture phase to prevent Base UI auto-dismiss
  useEffect(() => {
    const handleKeyDown = (e: KeyboardEvent) => {
      if (e.key === 'Escape') {
        e.preventDefault()
        e.stopImmediatePropagation()
        if (isDirtyRef.current) {
          showConfirmDialog()
        } else {
          dismiss()
        }
      }
    }
    document.addEventListener('keydown', handleKeyDown, true)
    return () => document.removeEventListener('keydown', handleKeyDown, true)
  }, [dismiss, showConfirmDialog])

  const attemptClose = useCallback(() => {
    if (isDirtyRef.current) {
      showConfirmDialog()
    } else {
      dismiss()
    }
  }, [dismiss, showConfirmDialog])

  const handleManualUpload = useCallback(() => {
    performSave()
  }, [performSave])

  const handleExportJson = useCallback(() => {
    const doc = getSnapshot()
    if (!doc) return
    const blob = new Blob([JSON.stringify(doc, null, 2)], {
      type: 'application/json',
    })
    const url = URL.createObjectURL(blob)
    const a = document.createElement('a')
    a.href = url
    a.download = 'excalidraw-snapshot.json'
    a.click()
    URL.revokeObjectURL(url)
  }, [getSnapshot])

  const handleCopyJson = useCallback(() => {
    const doc = getSnapshot()
    if (!doc) return
    void navigator.clipboard.writeText(JSON.stringify(doc))
  }, [getSnapshot])

  const handleCopyRef = useCallback(() => {
    if (savedRef) void navigator.clipboard.writeText(savedRef)
  }, [savedRef])

  const handleStorageModeChange = useCallback(
    (newMode: 'inline' | 'remote') => {
      setStorageMode(newMode)
      storageModeRef.current = newMode
      if (newMode === 'inline') setSavedRef('')
      performSave()
    },
    [performSave],
  )

  return (
    <>
      {/* Header */}
      <div className={css.excalidrawDialogHeader}>
        <span
          className={css.excalidrawStatusDot}
          style={{ backgroundColor: isDirty ? '#f59e0b' : '#22c55e' }}
        />
        <span className={css.excalidrawDialogTitle}>Canvas Editor</span>
        <span className={css.excalidrawDialogMeta}>excalidraw</span>

        <div className={css.excalidrawHeaderActions}>
          {saveSnapshot && (
            <>
              <SegmentedControl
                items={[
                  { value: 'inline', label: 'Inline' },
                  { value: 'remote', label: 'Remote' },
                ]}
                value={storageMode}
                onChange={handleStorageModeChange}
              />
              <div className={css.excalidrawActionBarSep} />
              <button
                type="button"
                className={css.excalidrawActionBarBtn}
                onClick={handleManualUpload}
                disabled={isSaving}
              >
                <Upload size={14} />
                <span>{isSaving ? 'Saving...' : 'Save'}</span>
              </button>
              <div className={css.excalidrawActionBarSep} />
            </>
          )}
          <button
            type="button"
            className={css.excalidrawActionBarBtn}
            onClick={handleExportJson}
          >
            <Download size={14} />
            JSON
          </button>
          <button
            type="button"
            className={css.excalidrawActionBarBtn}
            onClick={handleCopyJson}
          >
            <Clipboard size={14} />
            Copy
          </button>
          {storageMode === 'remote' && savedRef && (
            <>
              <div className={css.excalidrawActionBarSep} />
              <button
                type="button"
                className={css.excalidrawActionBarUrl}
                onClick={handleCopyRef}
                title={savedRef}
              >
                {savedRef}
              </button>
            </>
          )}
        </div>

        <button
          type="button"
          className={css.excalidrawHeaderClose}
          onClick={attemptClose}
        >
          <X size={18} />
        </button>
      </div>

      {/* Canvas */}
      <div className={css.excalidrawDialogCanvas}>
        <ExcalidrawComponent
          initialData={initialData}
          theme={theme}
          onChange={handleChange}
          excalidrawAPI={(api: any) => {
            apiRef.current = api
          }}
        />
      </div>
    </>
  )
}

// ── Main component ──────────────────────────────────────────────

export const ExcalidrawEditRenderer: FC<ExcalidrawEditRendererProps> = ({
  snapshot,
  onSnapshotChange,
}) => {
  const theme = useColorScheme()
  const { saveSnapshot } = useExcalidrawConfig()
  const {
    snapshot: initialData,
    loading: dataLoading,
    error: dataError,
  } = useExcalidrawData(snapshot)
  const [ExcalidrawComponent, setExcalidrawComponent] = useState<FC<any> | null>(null)
  const [libLoading, setLibLoading] = useState(true)
  const previewApiRef = useRef<any>(null)
  const initialDataRef = useRef<Record<string, any> | undefined>(undefined)
  const onSnapshotChangeRef = useRef(onSnapshotChange)
  onSnapshotChangeRef.current = onSnapshotChange
  const saveSnapshotRef = useRef(saveSnapshot)
  saveSnapshotRef.current = saveSnapshot
  const snapshotRef = useRef(snapshot)
  snapshotRef.current = snapshot
  const { className: portalClassName } = usePortalTheme()

  useEffect(() => {
    if (initialData && !initialDataRef.current) {
      initialDataRef.current = initialData
    }
  }, [initialData])

  useEffect(() => {
    import('@excalidraw/excalidraw')
      .then((mod) => {
        setExcalidrawComponent(() => mod.Excalidraw)
        setLibLoading(false)
      })
      .catch(() => setLibLoading(false))
  }, [])

  const handleOpenEditor = useCallback(() => {
    if (!ExcalidrawComponent) return
    presentDialog({
      content: ({ dismiss }) => (
        <ExcalidrawEditorDialogContent
          dismiss={dismiss}
          ExcalidrawComponent={ExcalidrawComponent}
          initialData={initialDataRef.current}
          initialSnapshot={snapshotRef.current}
          theme={theme}
          onSave={(ref) => onSnapshotChangeRef.current(ref)}
          saveSnapshot={saveSnapshotRef.current}
          onClose={(doc) => {
            if (doc) initialDataRef.current = doc
          }}
        />
      ),
      className: css.excalidrawFullscreenPopup,
      portalClassName,
      theme,
      showCloseButton: false,
      clickOutsideToDismiss: false,
    })
  }, [ExcalidrawComponent, theme, portalClassName])

  const loading = dataLoading || libLoading

  if (loading || !ExcalidrawComponent) {
    return (
      <div className={css.excalidrawEditorContainer}>
        <div className={css.excalidrawLoading}>Loading excalidraw editor...</div>
      </div>
    )
  }

  if (dataError) {
    return (
      <div className={css.excalidrawEditorContainer}>
        <div className={css.excalidrawError}>{dataError}</div>
      </div>
    )
  }

  return (
    <div className={css.excalidrawEditorContainer}>
      <ExcalidrawComponent
        initialData={initialDataRef.current}
        viewModeEnabled
        theme={theme}
        excalidrawAPI={(api: any) => {
          previewApiRef.current = api
          setTimeout(() => api.scrollToContent(), 100)
        }}
      />

      <button
        type="button"
        className={css.excalidrawEditOverlay}
        onClick={handleOpenEditor}
      >
        <span className={css.excalidrawEditLabel}>
          <Pencil size={16} />
          Edit Whiteboard
        </span>
      </button>
    </div>
  )
}

```

```typescript
import { vars } from '@haklex/rich-style-token'
import { globalStyle, keyframes, style } from '@vanilla-extract/css'

const ASPECT_RATIO = '16 / 10'

export const excalidrawStaticContainer = style({
  position: 'relative',
  width: '100%',
  aspectRatio: ASPECT_RATIO,
  margin: '1rem 0',
  borderRadius: '0.5rem',
  overflow: 'hidden',
})

export const excalidrawEditorContainer = style({
  position: 'relative',
  width: '100%',
  aspectRatio: ASPECT_RATIO,
  margin: '1rem 0',
  borderRadius: '0.5rem',
  overflow: 'hidden',
  border: `1px solid ${vars.color.border}`,
})

export const excalidrawLoading = style({
  display: 'flex',
  alignItems: 'center',
  justifyContent: 'center',
  position: 'absolute',
  inset: 0,
  color: vars.color.textSecondary,
  fontSize: vars.typography.fontSizeMd,
})

const spin = keyframes({
  to: { transform: 'rotate(360deg)' },
})

globalStyle(`${excalidrawLoading}::after`, {
  content: '""',
  display: 'inline-block',
  width: 16,
  height: 16,
  marginLeft: 8,
  border: '2px solid currentColor',
  borderRightColor: 'transparent',
  borderRadius: '50%',
  animation: `${spin} 0.6s linear infinite`,
})

export const excalidrawError = style({
  display: 'flex',
  alignItems: 'center',
  justifyContent: 'center',
  position: 'absolute',
  inset: 0,
  padding: '0.75rem 1rem',
  backgroundColor: `color-mix(in srgb, ${vars.color.alertCaution} 10%, transparent)`,
  color: vars.color.alertCaution,
  fontSize: vars.typography.fontSizeMd,
})

export const excalidrawActionGroup = style({
  position: 'absolute',
  bottom: 8,
  right: 8,
  zIndex: 10,
  display: 'flex',
  gap: 4,
  borderRadius: 6,
  padding: 2,
  backgroundColor: 'color-mix(in srgb, currentColor 8%, transparent)',
  backdropFilter: 'blur(8px)',
})

export const excalidrawActionButton = style({
  display: 'flex',
  alignItems: 'center',
  justifyContent: 'center',
  width: 28,
  height: 28,
  borderRadius: 4,
  border: 'none',
  background: 'transparent',
  color: vars.color.textSecondary,
  cursor: 'pointer',
  transition: 'background 0.15s, color 0.15s',
  ':hover': {
    background: 'color-mix(in srgb, currentColor 12%, transparent)',
    color: vars.color.text,
  },
})

globalStyle(`${excalidrawActionButton} svg`, {
  width: 16,
  height: 16,
})

// ── Fullscreen popup (overrides editor-ui dialog popup) ─────────

const _excalidrawFullscreenPopup = style({})
globalStyle(`${_excalidrawFullscreenPopup}${_excalidrawFullscreenPopup}`, {
  position: 'fixed',
  inset: 0,
  top: 0,
  left: 0,
  transform: 'none',
  width: '100vw',
  height: '100vh',
  maxWidth: '100vw',
  maxHeight: '100vh',
  margin: 0,
  padding: 0,
  gap: 0,
  borderRadius: 0,
  border: 'none',
  display: 'flex',
  flexDirection: 'column',
  background: vars.color.bg,
  overflow: 'hidden',
})
globalStyle(`${_excalidrawFullscreenPopup}${_excalidrawFullscreenPopup}[data-open]`, {
  animation: 'none',
})
globalStyle(`${_excalidrawFullscreenPopup}${_excalidrawFullscreenPopup}[data-closed]`, {
  animation: 'none',
})
export { _excalidrawFullscreenPopup as excalidrawFullscreenPopup }

// ── Dialog header ───────────────────────────────────────────────

export const excalidrawDialogHeader = style({
  display: 'flex',
  alignItems: 'center',
  height: 48,
  flexShrink: 0,
  padding: '0 12px 0 16px',
  borderBottom: `1px solid ${vars.color.border}`,
  gap: 10,
})

export const excalidrawStatusDot = style({
  width: 8,
  height: 8,
  borderRadius: '50%',
  flexShrink: 0,
  transition: 'background-color 0.3s',
})

export const excalidrawDialogTitle = style({
  fontSize: vars.typography.fontSizeMd,
  fontWeight: 600,
  color: vars.color.text,
  lineHeight: 1,
})

export const excalidrawDialogMeta = style({
  fontSize: vars.typography.fontSizeSm,
  color: vars.color.textSecondary,
  lineHeight: 1,
  paddingLeft: 10,
  borderLeft: `1px solid ${vars.color.border}`,
})

export const excalidrawHeaderActions = style({
  marginLeft: 'auto',
  display: 'flex',
  alignItems: 'center',
  gap: 2,
})

export const excalidrawHeaderClose = style({
  marginLeft: 8,
  flexShrink: 0,

  cursor: 'pointer',
  transition: 'background 0.15s, color 0.15s',

    background: 'color-mix(in srgb, currentColor 8%, transparent)',
    color: vars.color.text,
  },
})

// ── Header action buttons ────────────────────────────────────────

export const excalidrawActionBarBtn = style({
  display: 'inline-flex',
  alignItems: 'center',
  gap: 4,
  padding: '4px 10px',
  borderRadius: 4,
  border: 'none',
  background: 'transparent',

    color: vars.color.text,

    '&:disabled': {
      opacity: 0.4,
      pointerEvents: 'none',
    },
  },
})

  height: 14,
  flexShrink: 0,
})

export const excalidrawActionBarSep = style({

})

export const excalidrawActionBarUrl = style({

// ── Dialog canvas area ──────────────────────────────────────────

export const excalidrawDialogCanvas = style({

})

// ── Unsaved-changes confirmation (used inside presentDialog) ────

export const excalidrawConfirmActions = style({

  justifyContent: 'flex-end',
  gap: 8,
})

export const excalidrawConfirmBtn = style({
  padding: '6px 14px',
  borderRadius: 6,
  border: `1px solid ${vars.color.border}`,
  background: 'transparent',

    background: `color-mix(in srgb, ${vars.color.alertCaution} 10%, transparent)`,

// ── Edit overlay ────────────────────────────────────────────────
```

```typescript
export type { ExcalidrawConfig } from './ExcalidrawConfigContext'
```

```text
    25→  "packages/rich-renderer-katex"

    27→  "packages/rich-renderer-mention"
    28→  "packages/rich-renderer-mermaid"

    30→  "packages/rich-ext-code-snippet"

    32→  "packages/rich-ext-gallery"

    40→  "packages/rich-kit-shiro"

    42→)
    43→
    44→get_pkg_name() {
    45→  jq -r '.name' "$1/package.json"
    46→}
    47→
    48→publish_cmd() {
    49→  local dir="$1"
    50→  if [ "$DRY_RUN" = true ]; then
    51→    echo "[DRY-RUN] pnpm publish $dir --access public --no-git-checks"
    52→  else
    53→    (cd "$dir" && pnpm publish --access public --no-git-checks)
    54→  fi
    55→}
    56→
    57→if [ "$SKIP_BUILD" = false ]; then
    58→  echo "Building packages..."
    59→  for pkg in "${PACKAGES[@]}"; do
    60→    if [ -f "$pkg/package.json" ]; then
    61→      name=$(get_pkg_name "$pkg")
    62→      echo "Building $name..."
    63→      pnpm --filter "$name" build
    64→    fi
    65→  done
    66→fi
    67→
    68→echo "Publishing..."
    69→for pkg in "${PACKAGES[@]}"; do
    70→  if [ -f "$pkg/package.json" ]; then
    71→    publish_cmd "$pkg"
    72→  fi
    73→done
    74→
```

<summary>
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user asked to implement a detailed plan to replace `tldraw` with `@excalidraw/excalidraw` across a Lexical-based rich editor monorepo (`haklex/` packages). The plan involves:
   - Creating a new `@haklex/rich-ext-excalidraw` package (copying skeleton from old tldraw package, rewriting renderers to use excalidraw API)
   - Updating all dependent packages: `rich-headless`, `rich-renderers`, `rich-renderers-edit`, `rich-kit-shiro`, `rich-editor-demo`
   - Deleting the old `@haklex/rich-ext-tldraw` package
   - Installing dependencies and verifying builds

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Lexical editor framework with DecoratorNode pattern
   - Static/Edit node split for bundle optimization (tree-shaking)
   - Excalidraw API: `<Excalidraw initialData={data} viewModeEnabled theme={theme} excalidrawAPI={setApi} onChange={cb} />` (props-driven) vs tldraw's imperative `onMount` callback pattern
   - Excalidraw data model: `{ elements, appState, files }` vs tldraw's `store.getSnapshot()`
   - Excalidraw zoom: `api.getAppState().zoom.value * 1.25` and `api.updateScene({ appState: { zoom: { value: n } } })`
   - Excalidraw fit: `api.scrollToContent()` vs tldraw's `editor.zoomToFit()`
   - Vanilla Extract CSS-in-TS for styling
   - pnpm workspace monorepo with Turborepo
   - Vite library mode builds

3. Files and Code Sections:

   **CREATED - New Package `haklex/rich-ext-excalidraw/`:**
   
   - `package.json` - Dependencies changed from `tldraw: ^4.4.0` to `@excalidraw/excalidraw: ^0.18.0`. Exports use `rich-ext-excalidraw` naming.
   
   - `tsconfig.json` - Identical to old package
   
   - `vite.config.ts` - Identical to old package (entry: index + static)
   
   - `src/ExcalidrawNode.ts` - Base node class, type `'excalidraw'`, DOM class `'rich-excalidraw-wrapper'`, exports `$createExcalidrawNode`, `$isExcalidrawNode`
   
   - `src/ExcalidrawEditNode.ts` - Edit variant with lazy imports, slash menu keywords `['excalidraw', 'whiteboard', 'draw', 'canvas']`
   
   - `src/ExcalidrawDisplayRenderer.tsx` - **Core rewrite**: Uses `excalidrawAPI` prop callback instead of `onMount`, `viewModeEnabled` prop instead of imperative `editor.updateInstanceState({ isReadonly: true })`, theme via prop instead of `editor.user.updateUserPreferences`, zoom via `api.getAppState().zoom.value * 1.25` / `/ 1.25`
   
   - `src/ExcalidrawEditRenderer.tsx` - **Most complex rewrite**: Uses `onChange` prop instead of `editor.store.listen()`, snapshot via `{ elements: api.getSceneElements(), appState: api.getAppState(), files: api.getFiles() }` instead of `editor.store.getSnapshot()`, debounced dirty tracking with `isDirtyRef` optimization
   
   - `src/ExcalidrawSSRRenderer.tsx` - Element count via `Array.isArray(data.elements) ? data.elements.length : 0` instead of `shape:*` key counting
   
   - `src/ExcalidrawPlugin.tsx` - `INSERT_EXCALIDRAW_COMMAND`
   
   - `src/ExcalidrawConfigContext.tsx` - `ExcalidrawConfig`, `ExcalidrawConfigProvider`, `useExcalidrawConfig`
   
   - `src/useExcalidrawData.ts` - Identical logic to useTldrawData, renamed types
   
   - `src/styles.css.ts` - All CSS class names `tldraw*` → `excalidraw*`, style values unchanged
   
   - `src/index.ts` - Main entry re-exporting all public APIs
   
   - `src/static.ts` - Static-only entry (no edit deps)
   
   - `src/transformer.ts` - Re-exports `EXCALIDRAW_BLOCK_TRANSFORMER` from `@haklex/rich-headless/transformers`
   
   - `src/ExcalidrawRenderer.tsx` - Backward compat re-export

   **MODIFIED - `haklex/rich-headless/src/index.ts`:**
   - `TldrawNode` → `ExcalidrawNode` (headlessDecorator with type `'excalidraw'`)
   - `customHeadlessNodes` array: `TldrawNode` → `ExcalidrawNode`

   **MODIFIED - `haklex/rich-headless/src/transformers.ts`:**
   - `TLDRAW_BLOCK_TRANSFORMER` → `EXCALIDRAW_BLOCK_TRANSFORMER`
   - Node type check: `'tldraw'` → `'excalidraw'`
   - HTML tags: `<tldraw>` → `<excalidraw>`
   - `allHeadlessTransformers` array updated

   **CREATED - `haklex/rich-renderers/src/excalidraw.ts`:**

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./haklex/rich-ext-excalidraw/src/ExcalidrawDisplayRenderer.tsx`

```text
     1→import { useColorScheme } from '@haklex/rich-editor'
     2→import { presentDialog } from '@haklex/rich-editor-ui'
     3→import { usePortalTheme } from '@haklex/rich-style-token'
     4→import { Maximize2, ScanSearch, X, ZoomIn, ZoomOut } from 'lucide-react'
     5→import { Component, type FC, type ReactNode } from 'react'
     6→import { useCallback, useEffect, useRef, useState } from 'react'
     7→
     8→import * as css from './styles.css'
     9→import { useExcalidrawData } from './useExcalidrawData'
    10→
    11→export interface ExcalidrawStaticRendererProps {
    12→  snapshot: string
    13→}
    14→
    15→export const ExcalidrawDisplayRenderer: FC<ExcalidrawStaticRendererProps> = ({
    16→  snapshot,
    17→}) => {
    18→  const theme = useColorScheme()
    19→  const containerRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null)
    20→  const [hasEnteredView, setHasEnteredView] = useState(false)
    21→
    22→  useEffect(() => {
    23→    if (hasEnteredView) return
    24→    const target = containerRef.current
    25→    if (!target) return
    26→
    27→    const observer = new IntersectionObserver(
    28→      (entries) => {
    29→        if (entries.some((e) => e.isIntersecting)) {
    30→          setHasEnteredView(true)
    31→          observer.disconnect()
    32→        }
    33→      },
    34→      { rootMargin: '200px 0px', threshold: 0.1 },
    35→    )
    36→    observer.observe(target)
    37→    return () => observer.disconnect()
    38→  }, [hasEnteredView])
    39→
    40→  if (!hasEnteredView) {
    41→    return (
    42→      <div ref={containerRef} className={css.excalidrawStaticContainer}>
    43→        <div className={css.excalidrawLoading}>Loading excalidraw...</div>
    44→      </div>
    45→    )
    46→  }
    47→
    48→  return (
    49→    <div ref={containerRef}>
    50→      <ExcalidrawStaticCanvas snapshot={snapshot} theme={theme} />
    51→    </div>
    52→  )
    53→}
    54→
    55→// --- Fullscreen readonly content ---
    56→
    57→const ExcalidrawExpandContent: FC<{
    58→  dismiss: () => void
    59→  ExcalidrawComponent: FC<any>
    60→  data: Record<string, any>
    61→  theme: 'light' | 'dark'
    62→}> = ({ dismiss, ExcalidrawComponent, data, theme }) => {
    63→  const apiRef = useRef<any>(null)
    64→
    65→  return (
    66→    <>
    67→      <div className={css.excalidrawDialogHeader}>
    68→        <span className={css.excalidrawDialogTitle}>Whiteboard</span>
    69→        <span className={css.excalidrawDialogMeta}>excalidraw</span>
    70→        <button type="button" className={css.excalidrawHeaderClose} onClick={dismiss}>
    71→          <X size={18} />
    72→        </button>
    73→      </div>
    74→      <div className={css.excalidrawDialogCanvas}>
    75→        <ExcalidrawComponent
    76→          initialData={data}
    77→          viewModeEnabled
    78→          theme={theme}
    79→          excalidrawAPI={(api: any) => {
    80→            apiRef.current = api
    81→            setTimeout(() => api.scrollToContent(), 100)
    82→          }}
    83→        />
    84→      </div>
    85→    </>
    86→  )
    87→}
    88→
    89→// --- Main Canvas ---
    90→
    91→const ExcalidrawStaticCanvas: FC<{
    92→  snapshot: string
    93→  theme: 'light' | 'dark'
    94→}> = ({ snapshot, theme }) => {
    95→  const {
    96→    snapshot: data,
    97→    loading: dataLoading,
    98→    error: dataError,
    99→  } = useExcalidrawData(snapshot)
   100→  const [ExcalidrawComponent, setExcalidrawComponent] = useState<FC<any> | null>(null)
   101→  const [libLoading, setLibLoading] = useState(true)
   102→  const apiRef = useRef<any>(null)
   103→  const { className: portalClassName } = usePortalTheme()
   104→
   105→  useEffect(() => {
   106→    import('@excalidraw/excalidraw')
   107→      .then((mod) => {
   108→        setExcalidrawComponent(() => mod.Excalidraw)
   109→        setLibLoading(false)
   110→      })
   111→      .catch(() => setLibLoading(false))
   112→  }, [])
   113→
   114→  const handleExpand = useCallback(() => {
   115→    if (!ExcalidrawComponent || !data) return
   116→    presentDialog({
   117→      content: ({ dismiss }) => (
   118→        <ExcalidrawExpandContent
   119→          dismiss={dismiss}
   120→          ExcalidrawComponent={ExcalidrawComponent}
   121→          data={data}
   122→          theme={theme}
   123→        />
   124→      ),
   125→      className: css.excalidrawFullscreenPopup,
   126→      portalClassName,
   127→      theme,
   128→      showCloseButton: false,
   129→      clickOutsideToDismiss: true,
   130→    })
   131→  }, [ExcalidrawComponent, data, theme, portalClassName])
   132→
   133→  const loading = dataLoading || libLoading
   134→
   135→  if (loading) {
   136→    return (
   137→      <div className={css.excalidrawStaticContainer}>
   138→        <div className={css.excalidrawLoading}>Loading excalidraw...</div>
   139→      </div>
   140→    )
   141→  }
   142→
   143→  if (dataError || !data) {
   144→    return (
   145→      <div className={css.excalidrawStaticContainer}>
   146→        <div className={css.excalidrawError}>{dataError || 'No data'}</div>
   147→      </div>
   148→    )
   149→  }
   150→
   151→  if (!ExcalidrawComponent) {
   152→    return (
   153→      <div className={css.excalidrawStaticContainer}>
   154→        <div className={css.excalidrawError}>Failed to load excalidraw</div>
   155→      </div>
   156→    )
   157→  }
   158→
   159→  return (
   160→    <div
   161→      className={css.excalidrawStaticContainer}
   162→      data-theme={theme}
   163→      data-color-scheme={theme}
   164→      suppressHydrationWarning
   165→    >
   166→      <ExcalidrawErrorBoundary
   167→        fallback={
   168→          <div className={css.excalidrawError}>Failed to render excalidraw</div>
   169→        }
   170→      >
   171→        <ExcalidrawComponent
   172→          initialData={data}
   173→          viewModeEnabled
   174→          theme={theme}
   175→          excalidrawAPI={(api: any) => {
   176→            apiRef.current = api
   177→            setTimeout(() => api.scrollToContent(), 100)
   178→          }}
   179→        />
   180→      </ExcalidrawErrorBoundary>
   181→
   182→      <div className={css.excalidrawActionGroup}>
   183→        <button
   184→          type="button"
   185→          className={css.excalidrawActionButton}
   186→          title="Zoom In"
   187→          onClick={() => {
   188→            const api = apiRef.current
   189→            if (!api) return
   190→            const zoom = api.getAppState().zoom.value
   191→            api.updateScene({ appState: { zoom: { value: zoom * 1.25 } } })
   192→          }}
   193→        >
   194→          <ZoomIn size={20} />
   195→        </button>
   196→        <button
   197→          type="button"
   198→          className={css.excalidrawActionButton}
   199→          title="Zoom Out"
   200→          onClick={() => {
   201→            const api = apiRef.current
   202→            if (!api) return
   203→            const zoom = api.getAppState().zoom.value
   204→            api.updateScene({ appState: { zoom: { value: zoom / 1.25 } } })
   205→          }}
   206→        >
   207→          <ZoomOut size={20} />
   208→        </button>
   209→        <button
   210→          type="button"
   211→          className={css.excalidrawActionButton}
   212→          title="Fit to Content"
   213→          onClick={() => apiRef.current?.scrollToContent()}
   214→        >
   215→          <ScanSearch size={20} />
   216→        </button>
   217→        <button
   218→          type="button"
   219→          className={css.excalidrawActionButton}
   220→          title="Expand"
   221→          onClick={handleExpand}
   222→        >
   223→          <Maximize2 size={20} />
   224→        </button>
   225→      </div>
   226→    </div>
   227→  )
   228→}
   229→
   230→class ExcalidrawErrorBoundary extends Component<
   231→  { fallback: ReactNode; children: ReactNode },
   232→  { hasError: boolean }
   233→> {
   234→  state = { hasError: false }
   235→  static getDerivedStateFromError() {
   236→    return { hasError: true }
   237→  }
   238→  render() {
   239→    return this.state.hasError ? this.props.fallback : this.props.children
   240→  }
   241→}
   242→
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./haklex/rich-ext-excalidraw/src/ExcalidrawEditRenderer.tsx`

```text
     1→import { useColorScheme } from '@haklex/rich-editor'
     2→import { presentDialog, SegmentedControl } from '@haklex/rich-editor-ui'
     3→import { usePortalTheme } from '@haklex/rich-style-token'
     4→import { Clipboard, Download, Pencil, Upload, X } from 'lucide-react'
     5→import type { FC } from 'react'
     6→import { useCallback, useEffect, useRef, useState } from 'react'
     7→
     8→import * as css from './styles.css'
     9→import { useExcalidrawConfig } from './ExcalidrawConfigContext'
    10→import { useExcalidrawData } from './useExcalidrawData'
    11→
    12→export interface ExcalidrawEditRendererProps {
    13→  snapshot: string
    14→  onSnapshotChange: (snapshot: string) => void
    15→}
    16→
    17→const SAVE_DEBOUNCE_MS = 2000
    18→
    19→// ── Dialog content (fullscreen editor) ──────────────────────────
    20→
    21→const ExcalidrawEditorDialogContent: FC<{
    22→  dismiss: () => void
    23→  ExcalidrawComponent: FC<any>
    24→  initialData: Record<string, any> | undefined
    25→  initialSnapshot: string
    26→  theme: 'light' | 'dark'
    27→  onSave: (ref: string) => void
    28→  saveSnapshot?: (snapshot: object) => Promise<string>
    29→  onClose: (doc?: Record<string, any>) => void
    30→}> = ({
    31→  dismiss,
    32→  ExcalidrawComponent,
    33→  initialData,
    34→  initialSnapshot,
    35→  theme,
    36→  onSave,
    37→  saveSnapshot,
    38→  onClose,
    39→}) => {
    40→  const { className: portalClassName } = usePortalTheme()
    41→  const apiRef = useRef<any>(null)
    42→  const saveTimerRef = useRef<ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>>(undefined)
    43→  const [isDirty, setIsDirty] = useState(false)
    44→  const isDirtyRef = useRef(false)
    45→  const [storageMode, setStorageMode] = useState<'inline' | 'remote'>(() => {
    46→    if (!saveSnapshot || !initialSnapshot || !initialSnapshot.trim())
    47→      return 'inline'
    48→    try {
    49→      JSON.parse(initialSnapshot)
    50→      return 'inline'
    51→    } catch {
    52→      return 'remote'
    53→    }
    54→  })
    55→  const storageModeRef = useRef(storageMode)
    56→  const [savedRef, setSavedRef] = useState(() => {
    57→    if (!saveSnapshot || !initialSnapshot || !initialSnapshot.trim()) return ''
    58→    try {
    59→      JSON.parse(initialSnapshot)
    60→      return ''
    61→    } catch {
    62→      return initialSnapshot
    63→    }
    64→  })
    65→  const [isSaving, setIsSaving] = useState(false)
    66→
    67→  useEffect(() => {
    68→    return () => {
    69→      if (saveTimerRef.current) clearTimeout(saveTimerRef.current)
    70→    }
    71→  }, [])
    72→
    73→  const getSnapshot = useCallback(() => {
    74→    const api = apiRef.current
    75→    if (!api) return undefined
    76→    return {
    77→      elements: api.getSceneElements(),
    78→      appState: api.getAppState(),
    79→      files: api.getFiles(),
    80→    }
    81→  }, [])
    82→
    83→  const performSave = useCallback((): Promise<void> => {
    84→    if (saveTimerRef.current) clearTimeout(saveTimerRef.current)
    85→    const doc = getSnapshot()
    86→    if (!doc) return Promise.resolve()
    87→    try {
    88→      if (storageModeRef.current === 'remote' && saveSnapshot) {
    89→        setIsSaving(true)
    90→        return saveSnapshot(doc).then(
    91→          (ref) => {
    92→            onSave(ref)
    93→            isDirtyRef.current = false
    94→            setIsDirty(false)
    95→            setIsSaving(false)
    96→            setSavedRef(ref)
    97→          },
    98→          () => {
    99→            setIsSaving(false)
   100→          },
   101→        )
   102→      } else {
   103→        onSave(JSON.stringify(doc))
   104→        isDirtyRef.current = false
   105→        setIsDirty(false)
   106→      }
   107→    } catch {
   108→      // ignore
   109→    }
   110→    return Promise.resolve()
   111→  }, [onSave, saveSnapshot, getSnapshot])
   112→
   113→  const handleChange = useCallback(() => {
   114→    if (!isDirtyRef.current) {
   115→      isDirtyRef.current = true
   116→      setIsDirty(true)
   117→    }
   118→    if (saveTimerRef.current) clearTimeout(saveTimerRef.current)
   119→    saveTimerRef.current = setTimeout(
   120→      () => performSave(),
   121→      SAVE_DEBOUNCE_MS,
   122→    )
   123→  }, [performSave])
   124→
   125→  const handleSaveAndClose = useCallback(() => {
   126→    void performSave().then(() => {
   127→      const doc = getSnapshot()
   128→      onClose(doc)
   129→      dismiss()
   130→    })
   131→  }, [performSave, getSnapshot, onClose, dismiss])
   132→
   133→  const handleDiscard = useCallback(() => {
   134→    if (saveTimerRef.current) clearTimeout(saveTimerRef.current)
   135→    dismiss()
   136→  }, [dismiss])
   137→
   138→  const showConfirmDialog = useCallback(() => {
   139→    presentDialog({
   140→      title: 'Unsaved Changes',
   141→      description:
   142→        'You have unsaved changes that will be lost if you close now.',
   143→      content: ({ dismiss: dismissConfirm }) => (
   144→        <div className={css.excalidrawConfirmActions}>
   145→          <button
   146→            type="button"
   147→            className={css.excalidrawConfirmBtn}
   148→            onClick={dismissConfirm}
   149→          >
   150→            Continue Editing
   151→          </button>
   152→          <button
   153→            type="button"
   154→            className={css.excalidrawConfirmBtnDanger}
   155→            onClick={() => {
   156→              dismissConfirm()
   157→              handleDiscard()
   158→            }}
   159→          >
   160→            Discard
   161→          </button>
   162→          <button
   163→            type="button"
   164→            className={css.excalidrawConfirmBtnPrimary}
   165→            onClick={() => {
   166→              dismissConfirm()
   167→              handleSaveAndClose()
   168→            }}
   169→          >
   170→            Save & Close
   171→          </button>
   172→        </div>
   173→      ),
   174→      portalClassName,
   175→      theme,
   176→      showCloseButton: false,
   177→      clickOutsideToDismiss: false,
   178→    })
   179→  }, [handleDiscard, handleSaveAndClose, portalClassName, theme])
   180→
   181→  // Intercept ESC in capture phase to prevent Base UI auto-dismiss
   182→  useEffect(() => {
   183→    const handleKeyDown = (e: KeyboardEvent) => {
   184→      if (e.key === 'Escape') {
   185→        e.preventDefault()
   186→        e.stopImmediatePropagation()
   187→        if (isDirtyRef.current) {
   188→          showConfirmDialog()
   189→        } else {
   190→          dismiss()
   191→        }
   192→      }
   193→    }
   194→    document.addEventListener('keydown', handleKeyDown, true)
   195→    return () => document.removeEventListener('keydown', handleKeyDown, true)
   196→  }, [dismiss, showConfirmDialog])
   197→
   198→  const attemptClose = useCallback(() => {
   199→    if (isDirtyRef.current) {
   200→      showConfirmDialog()
   201→    } else {
   202→      dismiss()
   203→    }
   204→  }, [dismiss, showConfirmDialog])
   205→
   206→  const handleManualUpload = useCallback(() => {
   207→    performSave()
   208→  }, [performSave])
   209→
   210→  const handleExportJson = useCallback(() => {
   211→    const doc = getSnapshot()
   212→    if (!doc) return
   213→    const blob = new Blob([JSON.stringify(doc, null, 2)], {
   214→      type: 'application/json',
   215→    })
   216→    const url = URL.createObjectURL(blob)
   217→    const a = document.createElement('a')
   218→    a.href = url
   219→    a.download = 'excalidraw-snapshot.json'
   220→    a.click()
   221→    URL.revokeObjectURL(url)
   222→  }, [getSnapshot])
   223→
   224→  const handleCopyJson = useCallback(() => {
   225→    const doc = getSnapshot()
   226→    if (!doc) return
   227→    void navigator.clipboard.writeText(JSON.stringify(doc))
   228→  }, [getSnapshot])
   229→
   230→  const handleCopyRef = useCallback(() => {
   231→    if (savedRef) void navigator.clipboard.writeText(savedRef)
   232→  }, [savedRef])
   233→
   234→  const handleStorageModeChange = useCallback(
   235→    (newMode: 'inline' | 'remote') => {
   236→      setStorageMode(newMode)
   237→      storageModeRef.current = newMode
   238→      if (newMode === 'inline') setSavedRef('')
   239→      performSave()
   240→    },
   241→    [performSave],
   242→  )
   243→
   244→  return (
   245→    <>
   246→      {/* Header */}
   247→      <div className={css.excalidrawDialogHeader}>
   248→        <span
   249→          className={css.excalidrawStatusDot}
   250→          style={{ backgroundColor: isDirty ? '#f59e0b' : '#22c55e' }}
   251→        />
   252→        <span className={css.excalidrawDialogTitle}>Canvas Editor</span>
   253→        <span className={css.excalidrawDialogMeta}>excalidraw</span>
   254→
   255→        <div className={css.excalidrawHeaderActions}>
   256→          {saveSnapshot && (
   257→            <>
   258→              <SegmentedControl
   259→                items={[
   260→                  { value: 'inline', label: 'Inline' },
   261→                  { value: 'remote', label: 'Remote' },
   262→                ]}
   263→                value={storageMode}
   264→                onChange={handleStorageModeChange}
   265→              />
   266→              <div className={css.excalidrawActionBarSep} />
   267→              <button
   268→                type="button"
   269→                className={css.excalidrawActionBarBtn}
   270→                onClick={handleManualUpload}
   271→                disabled={isSaving}
   272→              >
   273→                <Upload size={14} />
   274→                <span>{isSaving ? 'Saving...' : 'Save'}</span>
   275→              </button>
   276→              <div className={css.excalidrawActionBarSep} />
   277→            </>
   278→          )}
   279→          <button
   280→            type="button"
   281→            className={css.excalidrawActionBarBtn}
   282→            onClick={handleExportJson}
   283→          >
   284→            <Download size={14} />
   285→            JSON
   286→          </button>
   287→          <button
   288→            type="button"
   289→            className={css.excalidrawActionBarBtn}
   290→            onClick={handleCopyJson}
   291→          >
   292→            <Clipboard size={14} />
   293→            Copy
   294→          </button>
   295→          {storageMode === 'remote' && savedRef && (
   296→            <>
   297→              <div className={css.excalidrawActionBarSep} />
   298→              <button
   299→                type="button"
   300→                className={css.excalidrawActionBarUrl}
   301→                onClick={handleCopyRef}
   302→                title={savedRef}
   303→              >
   304→                {savedRef}
   305→              </button>
   306→            </>
   307→          )}
   308→        </div>
   309→
   310→        <button
   311→          type="button"
   312→          className={css.excalidrawHeaderClose}
   313→          onClick={attemptClose}
   314→        >
   315→          <X size={18} />
   316→        </button>
   317→      </div>
   318→
   319→      {/* Canvas */}
   320→      <div className={css.excalidrawDialogCanvas}>
   321→        <ExcalidrawComponent
   322→          initialData={initialData}
   323→          theme={theme}
   324→          onChange={handleChange}
   325→          excalidrawAPI={(api: any) => {
   326→            apiRef.current = api
   327→          }}
   328→        />
   329→      </div>
   330→    </>
   331→  )
   332→}
   333→
   334→// ── Main component ──────────────────────────────────────────────
   335→
   336→export const ExcalidrawEditRenderer: FC<ExcalidrawEditRendererProps> = ({
   337→  snapshot,
   338→  onSnapshotChange,
   339→}) => {
   340→  const theme = useColorScheme()
   341→  const { saveSnapshot } = useExcalidrawConfig()
   342→  const {
   343→    snapshot: initialData,
   344→    loading: dataLoading,
   345→    error: dataError,
   346→  } = useExcalidrawData(snapshot)
   347→  const [ExcalidrawComponent, setExcalidrawComponent] = useState<FC<any> | null>(null)
   348→  const [libLoading, setLibLoading] = useState(true)
   349→  const previewApiRef = useRef<any>(null)
   350→  const initialDataRef = useRef<Record<string, any> | undefined>(undefined)
   351→  const onSnapshotChangeRef = useRef(onSnapshotChange)
   352→  onSnapshotChangeRef.current = onSnapshotChange
   353→  const saveSnapshotRef = useRef(saveSnapshot)
   354→  saveSnapshotRef.current = saveSnapshot
   355→  const snapshotRef = useRef(snapshot)
   356→  snapshotRef.current = snapshot
   357→  const { className: portalClassName } = usePortalTheme()
   358→
   359→  useEffect(() => {
   360→    if (initialData && !initialDataRef.current) {
   361→      initialDataRef.current = initialData
   362→    }
   363→  }, [initialData])
   364→
   365→  useEffect(() => {
   366→    import('@excalidraw/excalidraw')
   367→      .then((mod) => {
   368→        setExcalidrawComponent(() => mod.Excalidraw)
   369→        setLibLoading(false)
   370→      })
   371→      .catch(() => setLibLoading(false))
   372→  }, [])
   373→
   374→  const handleOpenEditor = useCallback(() => {
   375→    if (!ExcalidrawComponent) return
   376→    presentDialog({
   377→      content: ({ dismiss }) => (
   378→        <ExcalidrawEditorDialogContent
   379→          dismiss={dismiss}
   380→          ExcalidrawComponent={ExcalidrawComponent}
   381→          initialData={initialDataRef.current}
   382→          initialSnapshot={snapshotRef.current}
   383→          theme={theme}
   384→          onSave={(ref) => onSnapshotChangeRef.current(ref)}
   385→          saveSnapshot={saveSnapshotRef.current}
   386→          onClose={(doc) => {
   387→            if (doc) initialDataRef.current = doc
   388→          }}
   389→        />
   390→      ),
   391→      className: css.excalidrawFullscreenPopup,
   392→      portalClassName,
   393→      theme,
   394→      showCloseButton: false,
   395→      clickOutsideToDismiss: false,
   396→    })
   397→  }, [ExcalidrawComponent, theme, portalClassName])
   398→
   399→  const loading = dataLoading || libLoading
   400→
   401→  if (loading || !ExcalidrawComponent) {
   402→    return (
   403→      <div className={css.excalidrawEditorContainer}>
   404→        <div className={css.excalidrawLoading}>Loading excalidraw editor...</div>
   405→      </div>
   406→    )
   407→  }
   408→
   409→  if (dataError) {
   410→    return (
   411→      <div className={css.excalidrawEditorContainer}>
   412→        <div className={css.excalidrawError}>{dataError}</div>
   413→      </div>
   414→    )
   415→  }
   416→
   417→  return (
   418→    <div className={css.excalidrawEditorContainer}>
   419→      <ExcalidrawComponent
   420→        initialData={initialDataRef.current}
   421→        viewModeEnabled
   422→        theme={theme}
   423→        excalidrawAPI={(api: any) => {
   424→          previewApiRef.current = api
   425→          setTimeout(() => api.scrollToContent(), 100)
   426→        }}
   427→      />
   428→
   429→      <button
   430→        type="button"
   431→        className={css.excalidrawEditOverlay}
   432→        onClick={handleOpenEditor}
   433→      >
   434→        <span className={css.excalidrawEditLabel}>
   435→          <Pencil size={16} />
   436→          Edit Whiteboard
   437→        </span>
   438→      </button>
   439→    </div>
   440→  )
   441→}
   442→
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-02 11:27:43Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The user wants two things:

1. **SSR Renderer should use Suspense**: The `ExcalidrawSSRRenderer` should be a Suspense fallback that then loads the actual Excalidraw canvas on the client. Currently it's just a static placeholder div with text.

2. **Blog post about incremental data storage**: The WebFetch didn't get the content (it's a client-rendered page). Let me try to understand what they might mean. The concept is likely about storing excalidraw data incrementally (only changes/diffs) rather than full snapshots each time.

Wait, let me re-read the user's message more carefully:


So the user wants the SSR renderer to display the current placeholder as a Suspense fallback, then hydrate with the actual Excalidraw canvas on the client side, similar to how the display renderer works but from the static/SSR entry point. They're also referencing a blog post about incremental data storage concepts for Excalidraw.

The challenge here is that the SSR renderer is specifically designed for server-side rendering where Excalidraw can't load since it's a client-only library. The display renderer already handles client-side rendering with lazy loading, so I need to think about whether the SSR renderer should be enhanced to support client-side hydration or if they're asking for something different. Looking at the static entry structure, both renderers are exported separately, so maybe the intent is to make the SSR renderer smarter about detecting when it's on the client and switching to full rendering.

As for the incremental storage concept, I'm thinking this refers to storing only the changes or operations to the Excalidraw state rather than the entire JSON blob each time—this would significantly reduce storage overhead for canvases with many edits. The user might want me to apply this pattern to how the canvas state is persisted or managed. Writing the Suspense wrapper...

I'm realizing there's a dependency problem—the display renderer relies on edit-only packages like `useColorScheme` and `presentDialog`, but it's being exported from the static entry point which shouldn't have those dependencies. The static bundle is already exporting the display renderer, which creates a circular dependency issue with the rich-editor packages.

Actually, I think the solution is simpler: the SSR renderer can render a placeholder on the server, then on the client side lazy-load the real display renderer which already has its own intersection observer and dynamic import logic built in. The SSR renderer just needs to wrap it with Suspense to handle the initial hydration properly.

But there's a key constraint I'm realizing—in a true SSR context like the static renderer, we might not have access to React client features at all. Let me trace through how the node's `decorate` method determines which renderer gets used and check the ExcalidrawNode implementation to see where the SSR renderer actually fits in.
</details></think>

 `./haklex/rich-ext-excalidraw/src/ExcalidrawNode.ts`

```text
     1→import type {
     2→  EditorConfig,
     3→  LexicalEditor,
     4→  LexicalNode,
     5→  NodeKey,
     6→  SerializedLexicalNode,
     7→  Spread,
     8→} from 'lexical'
     9→import { DecoratorNode } from 'lexical'
    10→import type { ReactElement } from 'react'
    11→import { createElement } from 'react'
    12→
    13→import { ExcalidrawSSRRenderer } from './ExcalidrawSSRRenderer'
    14→
    15→export type SerializedExcalidrawNode = Spread<
    16→  { snapshot: string },
    17→  SerializedLexicalNode
    18→>
    19→
    20→export class ExcalidrawNode extends DecoratorNode<ReactElement> {
    21→  __snapshot: string
    22→
    23→  static getType(): string {
    24→    return 'excalidraw'
    25→  }
    26→
    27→  static clone(node: ExcalidrawNode): ExcalidrawNode {
    28→    return new ExcalidrawNode(node.__snapshot, node.__key)
    29→  }
    30→
    31→  constructor(snapshot: string, key?: NodeKey) {
    32→    super(key)
    33→    this.__snapshot = snapshot
    34→  }
    35→
    36→  createDOM(_config: EditorConfig): HTMLElement {
    37→    const div = document.createElement('div')
    38→    div.className = 'rich-excalidraw-wrapper'
    39→    return div
    40→  }
    41→
    42→  updateDOM(): boolean {
    43→    return false
    44→  }
    45→
    46→  isInline(): boolean {
    47→    return false
    48→  }
    49→
    50→  static importJSON(serializedNode: SerializedExcalidrawNode): ExcalidrawNode {
    51→    return $createExcalidrawNode(serializedNode.snapshot)
    52→  }
    53→
    54→  exportJSON(): SerializedExcalidrawNode {
    55→    return {
    56→      ...super.exportJSON(),
    57→      type: 'excalidraw',
    58→      snapshot: this.__snapshot,
    59→      version: 1,
    60→    }
    61→  }
    62→
    63→  getSnapshot(): string {
    64→    return this.__snapshot
    65→  }
    66→
    67→  setSnapshot(snapshot: string): void {
    68→    const writable = this.getWritable()
    69→    writable.__snapshot = snapshot
    70→  }
    71→
    72→  decorate(_editor: LexicalEditor, _config: EditorConfig): ReactElement {
    73→    return createElement(ExcalidrawSSRRenderer, {
    74→      snapshot: this.__snapshot,
    75→    })
    76→  }
    77→}
    78→
    79→export function $createExcalidrawNode(snapshot: string): ExcalidrawNode {
    80→  return new ExcalidrawNode(snapshot)
    81→}
    82→
    83→export function $isExcalidrawNode(
    84→  node: LexicalNode | null | undefined,
    85→): node is ExcalidrawNode {
    86→  return node instanceof ExcalidrawNode
    87→}
    88→
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-02 11:27:59Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now I understand the architecture:

1. `ExcalidrawNode.decorate()` → renders `ExcalidrawSSRRenderer` (lightweight placeholder)
2. `ExcalidrawEditNode.decorate()` → renders `ExcalidrawEditRenderer` (full editor)
3. The `ExcalidrawDisplayRenderer` is a separate component that loads excalidraw on the client with IntersectionObserver

The user wants:
1. **SSR Renderer to use Suspense**: The SSR renderer should show the placeholder on server, then lazy-load the `ExcalidrawDisplayRenderer` on the client with Suspense fallback


2. **Incremental storage concept**: Reference from their blog

I need to understand how other renderers like tldraw or mermaid handle this SSR/client pattern. The key insight is that `ExcalidrawNode.decorate()` returns the static placeholder, but when a custom renderer is configured via `RendererConfig`, it maps to `ExcalidrawDisplayRenderer` instead—the SSR renderer only acts as a fallback when no custom renderer is set up.

The user's goal is to make the SSR renderer itself smarter by using Suspense to show the placeholder server-side while loading the actual canvas on the client, so the excalidraw canvas renders properly even without the `RendererConfig` override. But I'm noticing that `ExcalidrawNode` doesn't use `RendererWrapper` like other nodes do—it directly renders `ExcalidrawSSRRenderer` in `decorate()`, and the custom renderer injection happens at a different layer. I need to check how the renderers config registers `ExcalidrawDisplayRenderer` to understand the full flow.
</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-02 11:28:21Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now I see - the `ExcalidrawNode.decorate()` returns `ExcalidrawSSRRenderer` directly. The `ExcalidrawDisplayRenderer` is not injected via `RendererConfig` (there's no `Excalidraw` key in the config - unlike Alert, Banner, etc.). The `ExcalidrawNode` doesn't use `RendererWrapper` like other nodes.

So the flow is:
- Server: `ExcalidrawNode.decorate()` → `ExcalidrawSSRRenderer` → static placeholder
- Client (static): Same - just a placeholder


- Client (editor): `ExcalidrawEditNode.decorate()` → `ExcalidrawEditRenderer` → full editor

The solution is to wrap the SSR renderer in Suspense so it renders the placeholder on the server, then lazy-loads the display renderer on the client to show the actual canvas. This way the static display path automatically upgrades to the full canvas without needing to inject a separate renderer config.

Now I'm thinking through the incremental storage concept—

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-02 11:29:41Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now I have the full blog post content. Let me understand the key concepts:

## Incremental Data Storage Concept

### Problem
- Excalidraw JSON data is very large for complex diagrams (32K+ compressed)
- Embedding directly in Markdown makes documents huge (hundreds of KB with multiple diagrams)
- AlgoliaSearch refuses to index documents with too many characters

### Solution: Three storage modes

1. **Inline (embedded)**: Full JSON data inline in the markdown code block
   ```excalidraw
   {"type":"excalidraw","version":2,...}
   ```

2. **Referenced (remote)**: URL or ref to an external file
   ```excalidraw
   https://gist.githubusercontent.com/.../test.excalidraw
   ```
   or
   ```excalidraw
   ref:file/koi3m8unhivw47rwan.excalidraw
   ```

3. **Incremental (delta)**: Base reference plus a JSON patch containing only the changes, where the first line points to the base data and subsequent lines contain the diff operations.

The system uses `jsondiffpatch` to compute and apply these deltas, with the base data stored on the server and the delta kept inline as a compact JSON object. When rendering, I fetch the base data and apply the patch to reconstruct the final state.

The parsing logic checks whether the snapshot is valid JSON first, then determines if it's a reference URL by looking for `http` or `ref:` prefixes, and if additional lines follow, treats them as a delta patch to apply after fetching the base data.

Handling the transformation in useMemo... I'm parsing the incoming data by splitting it into a reference URL and a patch delta—if the first line is an HTTP URL or a ref identifier, I extract that as the reference, and any remaining lines get parsed as a JSON diff patch to apply incrementally.

Now I'm examining the `useExcalidrawData` hook to understand how it currently handles the data and where I need to integrate this incremental storage pattern.
</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-02 11:29:41Z)**_

博文内容已获。核心理念:

1. **Inline**: 全量 JSON 内嵌
2. **Referenced**: URL/`ref:file/xxx` 引用远程文件
3. **Incremental**: 基础引用 + `jsondiffpatch` delta diff，第一行引用地址，第二行增量 JSON

现在读 `useExcalidrawData` 看当前实现状态。

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-02 11:29:42Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./haklex/rich-ext-excalidraw/src/useExcalidrawData.ts`

```text
     1→import { useEffect, useMemo, useState } from 'react'
     2→
     3→import { useExcalidrawConfig } from './ExcalidrawConfigContext'
     4→
     5→interface ExcalidrawDataState {
     6→  snapshot: Record<string, any> | undefined
     7→  loading: boolean
     8→  error: string
     9→}
    10→
    11→export function useExcalidrawData(data: string): ExcalidrawDataState {
    12→  const { apiUrl } = useExcalidrawConfig()
    13→
    14→  const parsed = useMemo(() => {
    15→    if (!data) return { type: 'empty' as const }
    16→
    17→    try {
    18→      const json = JSON.parse(data)
    19→      if (json && typeof json === 'object') {
    20→        return {
    21→          type: 'inline' as const,
    22→          snapshot: json as Record<string, any>,
    23→        }
    24→      }
    25→    } catch {
    26→      // not JSON
    27→    }
    28→
    29→    const firstLine = data.split('\n')[0].trim()
    30→    let fetchUrl: string | undefined
    31→
    32→    if (firstLine.startsWith('http')) {
    33→      fetchUrl = firstLine
    34→    } else if (firstLine.startsWith('ref:') && apiUrl) {
    35→      fetchUrl = `${apiUrl}/objects/${firstLine.slice(4)}`
    36→    }
    37→
    38→    if (fetchUrl) {
    39→      return { type: 'remote' as const, fetchUrl }
    40→    }
    41→
    42→    return {
    43→      type: 'error' as const,
    44→      error: firstLine.startsWith('ref:')
    45→        ? 'Missing apiUrl for ref resolution'
    46→        : 'Unrecognized snapshot format',
    47→    }
    48→  }, [data, apiUrl])
    49→
    50→  const fetchUrl = parsed.type === 'remote' ? parsed.fetchUrl : undefined
    51→
    52→  const [remoteSnapshot, setRemoteSnapshot] = useState<
    53→    Record<string, any> | undefined
    54→  >()
    55→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(!!fetchUrl)
    56→  const [error, setError] = useState('')
    57→
    58→  useEffect(() => {
    59→    if (!fetchUrl) return
    60→
    61→    let cancelled = false
    62→    setLoading(true)
    63→    setError('')
    64→
    65→    fetch(fetchUrl)
    66→      .then((res) => {
    67→        if (!res.ok) throw new Error(`HTTP ${res.status}`)
    68→        return res.json()
    69→      })
    70→      .then((json) => {
    71→        if (!cancelled) {
    72→          setRemoteSnapshot(json)
    73→          setLoading(false)
    74→        }
    75→      })
    76→      .catch((err) => {
    77→        if (!cancelled) {
    78→          setError(err instanceof Error ? err.message : 'Failed to load')
    79→          setLoading(false)
    80→        }
    81→      })
    82→
    83→    return () => {
    84→      cancelled = true
    85→    }
    86→  }, [fetchUrl])
    87→
    88→  if (parsed.type === 'inline') {
    89→    return { snapshot: parsed.snapshot, loading: false, error: '' }
    90→  }
    91→
    92→  if (parsed.type === 'remote') {
    93→    return { snapshot: remoteSnapshot, loading, error }
    94→  }
    95→
    96→  if (parsed.type === 'error') {
    97→    return { snapshot: undefined, loading: false, error: parsed.error }
    98→  }
    99→
   100→  return { snapshot: undefined, loading: false, error: '' }
   101→}
   102→
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
     4→export interface ExcalidrawConfig {

     6→  saveSnapshot?: (snapshot: object) => Promise<string>
     7→}
     8→
     9→const ExcalidrawConfigContext = createContext<ExcalidrawConfig>({})
    10→
    11→export function ExcalidrawConfigProvider({
    12→  apiUrl,
    13→  saveSnapshot,
    14→  children,
    15→}: ExcalidrawConfig & { children: ReactNode }) {
    16→  const value = useMemo(
    17→    () => ({ apiUrl, saveSnapshot }),
    18→    [apiUrl, saveSnapshot],
    19→  )
    20→  return (
    21→    <ExcalidrawConfigContext.Provider value={value}>
    22→      {children}
    23→    </ExcalidrawConfigContext.Provider>
    24→  )
    25→}
    26→
    27→export function useExcalidrawConfig(): ExcalidrawConfig {
    28→  return use(ExcalidrawConfigContext)
    29→}
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-02 11:30:52Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now I understand the full picture. Let me look at what needs to be done:

### 1. SSR Renderer with Suspense

The `ExcalidrawSSRRenderer` is used by `ExcalidrawNode.decorate()`. It should:
- Render the placeholder on server (current behavior)
- On client, lazy-load `ExcalidrawDisplayRenderer` via Suspense

The issue: `ExcalidrawSSRRenderer` is in the static entry and should not have heavy deps. But `ExcalidrawDisplayRenderer` is ALSO in the static entry already. However, it uses `useColorScheme` from `@haklex/rich-editor` and `presentDialog` from `@haklex/rich-editor-ui`.
 
So `ExcalidrawDisplayRenderer` is already exported from the static bundle, but the real problem is that the SSR renderer needs to provide a lightweight placeholder that works on the server, while the display renderer handles the actual interactive canvas on the client side.

The solution is to lazy-load the display renderer with a Suspense boundary using the static placeholder as a fallback. The display renderer depends on `useColorScheme` and `ColorSchemeContext`, but since `ExcalidrawNode` always runs within a Lexical editor context that provides this, that dependency should be satisfied. The SSR renderer is specifically for static rendering paths like `@haklex/rich-static-renderer`.

Setting up the component... I'm realizing that `React.lazy` with dynamic imports should work fine in a Node.js server context since it's just loading a module—the real issue would only arise if the module itself accesses browser APIs at the top level, which `ExcalidrawDisplayRenderer` doesn't do. The key insight is that by making `ExcalidrawSSRRenderer` lazy-import `ExcalidrawDisplayRenderer`, the code splitting happens correctly even though `ExcalidrawNode.ts` directly imports the SSR renderer, because the lazy import creates a separate chunk.

In Vite's library mode, code splitting for lazy imports works differently—the dynamic import is preserved in the output and the consumer's bundler handles the actual splitting. Since the static entry exports both renderers separately and only `ExcalidrawNode` imports the SSR version, the tree-shaking should work out correctly with the lazy import being handled downstream by Next.js's bundler.

Now I need to tackle the incremental storage case. The current `useExcalidrawData` handles inline JSON and remote URLs, but it's missing support for the delta diff pattern where the first line is a URL pointing to base data and subsequent lines contain JSON diffs that need to be patched together. I'll need to detect this scenario, fetch the base data, parse the delta, and apply the patch to reconstruct the final data. I'm realizing the user wants me to reference the incremental storage concept from their blog post when implementing the `useExcalidrawData` hook—so I need to support the delta format where a base URL and JSON diff are stored together, not just implement SSR with Suspense.

To make this work, I'll need to add `jsondiffpatch` as a dependency since I'll need its `patch` function to apply deltas when displaying data and its `diff` function when editing to compute the delta between the base and current state.

For the display flow with incremental updates, the first line would be a URL or ref pointing to base data, then subsequent lines would contain delta objects that get applied on top. The `parsed` useMemo would need to extract both the base data and delta, then apply the patch after fetching.

For saving in the edit renderer, if the initial data came from a ref plus delta, I'd compute a new delta by diffing the base data against the current state, then save that delta instead of the full snapshot.

Looking at the current implementation, `useExcalidrawData` only reads the first line and ignores the rest, so I need to extract the delta from the remaining lines and apply it after fetching the base data.

Now I'm building the SSR renderer with Suspense support... I'm realizing there's a dependency issue here—the lazy-loaded renderer pulls in heavy imports like color scheme hooks, dialog utilities, and icon libraries that should stay on the client side only. The SSR placeholder needs to stay lightweight, but I'm hitting a problem where the base `ExcalidrawNode` might not have the necessary context providers set up for the renderer to work properly.

Actually, looking at how the hooks handle missing context—they return sensible defaults like 'light' mode—this should work fine even without the full provider chain. And since `decorate()` always runs within a Lexical editor context, the providers should be available anyway. Let me trace through how other renderers like Mermaid handle this same pattern to see if there's a precedent I'm missing.

Wait, I think I've been looking at this wrong. The SSR placeholder was always just a placeholder, and the actual `ExcalidrawDisplayRenderer` gets injected through the `RendererConfig` mechanism. But checking the config, there's no Excalidraw entry there, which means the base node just renders the placeholder even on the client side. The `ExcalidrawDisplayRenderer` only shows up when `ExcalidrawEditNode` overrides `decorate()` to use it, or when a custom renderer config explicitly provides it. Let me look at what `ExcalidrawEditNode` is actually doing with its `decorate()` method to get the full picture.
</details></think>

 `./haklex/rich-ext-excalidraw/src/ExcalidrawEditNode.ts`

```text
     1→import type { SlashMenuItemConfig } from '@haklex/rich-editor'
     2→import type { EditorConfig, LexicalEditor, LexicalNode, NodeKey } from 'lexical'
     3→import { $getNodeByKey, $insertNodes } from 'lexical'
     4→import { PenTool } from 'lucide-react'
     5→import type { ReactElement } from 'react'
     6→import { createElement, lazy, Suspense } from 'react'
     7→
     8→import { type SerializedExcalidrawNode, ExcalidrawNode } from './ExcalidrawNode'
     9→
    10→const LazyDisplayRenderer = lazy(() =>
    11→  import('./ExcalidrawDisplayRenderer').then((m) => ({
    12→    default: m.ExcalidrawDisplayRenderer,
    13→  })),
    14→)
    15→const LazyEditRenderer = lazy(() =>
    16→  import('./ExcalidrawEditRenderer').then((m) => ({
    17→    default: m.ExcalidrawEditRenderer,
    18→  })),
    19→)
    20→
    21→export class ExcalidrawEditNode extends ExcalidrawNode {
    22→  static slashMenuItems: SlashMenuItemConfig[] = [
    23→    {
    24→      title: 'Whiteboard',
    25→      icon: createElement(PenTool, { size: 20 }),
    26→      description: 'Excalidraw whiteboard canvas',
    27→      keywords: ['excalidraw', 'whiteboard', 'draw', 'canvas'],
    28→      section: 'MEDIA',
    29→      onSelect: (editor) => {
    30→        editor.update(() => {
    31→          $insertNodes([$createExcalidrawEditNode('{}')])
    32→        })
    33→      },
    34→    },
    35→  ]
    36→
    37→  static clone(node: ExcalidrawEditNode): ExcalidrawEditNode {
    38→    return new ExcalidrawEditNode(node.__snapshot, node.__key)
    39→  }
    40→
    41→  constructor(snapshot: string, key?: NodeKey) {
    42→    super(snapshot, key)
    43→  }
    44→
    45→  static importJSON(serializedNode: SerializedExcalidrawNode): ExcalidrawEditNode {
    46→    return new ExcalidrawEditNode(serializedNode.snapshot)
    47→  }
    48→
    49→  decorate(editor: LexicalEditor, _config: EditorConfig): ReactElement {
    50→    const nodeKey = this.__key
    51→    const isEditable = editor.isEditable()
    52→
    53→    const fallback = createElement('div', {
    54→      className: 'rich-excalidraw-loading',
    55→      style: {
    56→        position: 'relative',
    57→        width: '100%',
    58→        aspectRatio: '16 / 10',
    59→        margin: '1rem 0',
    60→      },
    61→    })
    62→
    63→    if (isEditable) {
    64→      return createElement(
    65→        Suspense,
    66→        { fallback },
    67→        createElement(LazyEditRenderer, {
    68→          snapshot: this.__snapshot,
    69→          onSnapshotChange: (newSnapshot: string) => {
    70→            editor.update(() => {
    71→              const node = $getNodeByKey(nodeKey) as ExcalidrawNode | null
    72→              if (node) {
    73→                node.setSnapshot(newSnapshot)
    74→              }
    75→            })
    76→          },
    77→        }),
    78→      )
    79→    }
    80→
    81→    return createElement(
    82→      Suspense,
    83→      { fallback },
    84→      createElement(LazyDisplayRenderer, { snapshot: this.__snapshot }),
    85→    )
    86→  }
    87→}
    88→
    89→export function $createExcalidrawEditNode(snapshot: string): ExcalidrawEditNode {
    90→  return new ExcalidrawEditNode(snapshot)
    91→}
    92→
    93→export function $isExcalidrawEditNode(
    94→  node: LexicalNode | null | undefined,
    95→): node is ExcalidrawEditNode {
    96→  return node instanceof ExcalidrawEditNode
    97→}
    98→
```
