<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```tsx
import type { FC } from 'react'
import { lazy, Suspense } from 'react'

export interface ExcalidrawSSRRendererProps {
  snapshot: string
}

const LazyDisplayRenderer = lazy(() =>
  import('./ExcalidrawDisplayRenderer').then((m) => ({
    default: m.ExcalidrawDisplayRenderer,
  })),
)

const ExcalidrawPlaceholder: FC<{ snapshot: string }> = ({ snapshot }) => {
  let label = 'Excalidraw Whiteboard'
  try {
    const data = JSON.parse(snapshot)
    if (data && typeof data === 'object') {
      const elementCount = Array.isArray(data.elements) ? data.elements.length : 0
      if (elementCount > 0) {
        label = `Excalidraw Whiteboard (${elementCount} elements)`
      }
    }
  } catch {
    // snapshot might be a URL ref or invalid JSON — show generic placeholder
  }

  return (
    <div
      className="rich-excalidraw-ssr-placeholder"
      style={{
        position: 'relative',
        width: '100%',
        aspectRatio: '16 / 10',
        display: 'flex',
        alignItems: 'center',
        justifyContent: 'center',
        backgroundColor: 'var(--rich-excalidraw-bg, #f5f5f5)',
        borderRadius: '8px',
        border: '1px solid var(--rich-excalidraw-border, #e5e5e5)',
        color: 'var(--rich-excalidraw-fg, #737373)',
        fontSize: '14px',
      }}
      aria-label={label}
    >
      <span>{label}</span>
    </div>
  )
}

export const ExcalidrawSSRRenderer: FC<ExcalidrawSSRRendererProps> = ({ snapshot }) => {
  return (
    <Suspense fallback={<ExcalidrawPlaceholder snapshot={snapshot} />}>
      <LazyDisplayRenderer snapshot={snapshot} />
    </Suspense>
  )
}

```

```typescript
import { useEffect, useMemo, useState } from 'react'

import { useExcalidrawConfig } from './ExcalidrawConfigContext'

export interface ExcalidrawDataState {
  snapshot: Record<string, any> | undefined
  loading: boolean
  error: string
  /** The base reference line (URL or ref:file/xxx) when snapshot is remote or incremental */
  baseRef?: string
  /** The raw base data before delta patch (for computing new diffs in edit mode) */
  baseData?: Record<string, any>
}

type ParsedData =
  | { type: 'empty' }
  | { type: 'inline'; snapshot: Record<string, any> }
  | { type: 'remote'; fetchUrl: string; refLine: string }
  | { type: 'incremental'; fetchUrl: string; refLine: string; delta: object }
  | { type: 'error'; error: string }

export function useExcalidrawData(data: string): ExcalidrawDataState {
  const { apiUrl } = useExcalidrawConfig()

  const parsed = useMemo((): ParsedData => {
    if (!data) return { type: 'empty' }

    try {
      const json = JSON.parse(data)
      if (json && typeof json === 'object') {
        return { type: 'inline', snapshot: json as Record<string, any> }
      }
    } catch {
      // not JSON — check for URL/ref
    }

    const lines = data.split('\n')
    const firstLine = lines[0].trim()
    let fetchUrl: string | undefined
    const refLine = firstLine

    if (firstLine.startsWith('http')) {
      fetchUrl = firstLine
    } else if (firstLine.startsWith('ref:') && apiUrl) {
      fetchUrl = `${apiUrl}/objects/${firstLine.slice(4)}`
    }

    if (!fetchUrl) {
      return {
        type: 'error',
        error: firstLine.startsWith('ref:')
          ? 'Missing apiUrl for ref resolution'
          : 'Unrecognized snapshot format',
      }
    }

    // Check for delta diff in remaining lines
    const remainingLines = lines.slice(1).join('\n').trim()
    if (remainingLines) {
      try {
        const delta = JSON.parse(remainingLines)
        if (delta && typeof delta === 'object') {
          return { type: 'incremental', fetchUrl, refLine, delta }
        }
      } catch {
        // not valid JSON delta — treat as plain remote
      }
    }

    return { type: 'remote', fetchUrl, refLine }
  }, [data, apiUrl])

  const [remoteSnapshot, setRemoteSnapshot] = useState<
    Record<string, any> | undefined
  >()
  const [baseData, setBaseData] = useState<Record<string, any> | undefined>()
  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(
    parsed.type === 'remote' || parsed.type === 'incremental',
  )
  const [error, setError] = useState('')

  useEffect(() => {
    if (parsed.type !== 'remote' && parsed.type !== 'incremental') return

    const { fetchUrl } = parsed
    const delta = parsed.type === 'incremental' ? parsed.delta : undefined

    let cancelled = false
    setLoading(true)
    setError('')

    fetch(fetchUrl)
      .then((res) => {
        if (!res.ok) throw new Error(`HTTP ${res.status}`)
        return res.json()
      })
      .then(async (json) => {
        if (cancelled) return
        setBaseData(json)

        if (delta) {
          const { patch } = await import('jsondiffpatch')
          const patched = patch(structuredClone(json), delta)
          setRemoteSnapshot(patched as Record<string, any>)
        } else {
          setRemoteSnapshot(json)
        }
        setLoading(false)
      })
      .catch((err) => {
        if (!cancelled) {
          setError(err instanceof Error ? err.message : 'Failed to load')
          setLoading(false)
        }
      })

    return () => {
      cancelled = true
    }
  }, [parsed])

  if (parsed.type === 'inline') {
    return { snapshot: parsed.snapshot, loading: false, error: '' }
  }

  if (parsed.type === 'remote' || parsed.type === 'incremental') {
    return {
      snapshot: remoteSnapshot,
      loading,
      error,
      baseRef: parsed.refLine,
      baseData,
    }
  }

  if (parsed.type === 'error') {
    return { snapshot: undefined, loading: false, error: parsed.error }
  }

  return { snapshot: undefined, loading: false, error: '' }
}

```

<tool-use data-tool-type="unknown" data-tool-name="Agent"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Agent**</summary>
Review excalidraw changes

```text
The entire package is untracked. Now I have full context. Let me do the thorough review.

---

## Code Review: `@haklex/rich-ext-excalidraw` Delta / SSR Changes

### What Was Done Well

The incremental delta approach is architecturally sound — separating parse from fetch, using a cancellation flag in the `useEffect`, and lazy-loading `jsondiffpatch` only when needed are all correct instincts. The `ExcalidrawPlaceholder` in the SSR renderer provides a graceful degradation that matches the original plan.

---

### Critical Issues

**1. `baseDataRef` is never synced after `useExcalidrawData` resolves**

File: `<REPO_ROOT>/haklex/rich-ext-excalidraw/src/ExcalidrawEditRenderer.tsx`, lines 437-439

```tsx
baseRef={baseRef}
baseData={baseData}
```

These props are passed to `ExcalidrawEditorDialogContent` once at dialog-open time and captured into `useRef` at construction:

```tsx
const baseRefRef = useRef(baseRef)
const baseDataRef = useRef(baseData)
```

But if the dialog opens while `useExcalidrawData` is still loading (i.e., `baseData` is `undefined`), the refs are initialized to `undefined` and **never updated afterward**. Because `presentDialog` renders the content component once, changes to `baseRef`/`baseData` in the parent after the dialog opens are invisible to the dialog's refs.

The consequence: on the very first open of a delta-mode canvas (while the async fetch is still in flight), `performSave` takes the `else` branch (lines 115-120) and uploads the full document as a new base, silently discarding the delta relationship. This is a silent data-correctness bug. The loading guard in the parent (`if (loading || !ExcalidrawComponent) return`) only covers the inline loading indicator — the dialog can still be opened after lib loads but before data loads, because `handleOpenEditor` only checks `!ExcalidrawComponent` (line 424).

**Fix**: Do not open the editor dialog until both `!libLoading` and `!dataLoading`. The existing `loading` computed value (line 450) already captures this, but `handleOpenEditor` (line 423) only guards on `ExcalidrawComponent`:

```tsx
// Current — insufficient guard
const handleOpenEditor = useCallback(() => {
  if (!ExcalidrawComponent) return
  // ...
}, [ExcalidrawComponent, ...])

// Required
const handleOpenEditor = useCallback(() => {
  if (!ExcalidrawComponent || dataLoading) return
  // ...
}, [ExcalidrawComponent, dataLoading, ...])
```

---

**2. Race condition in `delta` mode `performSave` when `baseRef` exists but `baseData` is absent**

File: `<REPO_ROOT>/haklex/rich-ext-excalidraw/src/ExcalidrawEditRenderer.tsx`, lines 106-113

```tsx
if (currentBaseRef && currentBaseData) {
  const { diff } = await import('jsondiffpatch')
  const delta = diff(currentBaseData, doc)
  onSave(...)
} else {
  // No base ref yet — upload full file as new base
```

The comment says "No base ref yet" but `currentBaseRef` can be truthy while `currentBaseData` is falsy if the fetch is still in flight. This is the same race as issue 1, but from the save side: a partial state causes a silent fallback to a full upload with a new base ref, destroying the existing base reference.

---

**3. `setBaseData` called before `setRemoteSnapshot` but not guarded for cancellation**

File: `<REPO_ROOT>/haklex/rich-ext-excalidraw/src/useExcalidrawData.ts`, lines 98-108

```tsx
.then(async (json) => {
  if (cancelled) return
  setBaseData(json)        // state update — safe, cancelled checked

  if (delta) {
    const { patch } = await import('jsondiffpatch')
    const patched = patch(structuredClone(json), delta as any)
    // ── await point: component may unmount here ──
    setRemoteSnapshot(patched as Record<string, any>)  // no cancelled re-check
  } else {
    setRemoteSnapshot(json)
  }
  setLoading(false)
})
```

After `await import('jsondiffpatch')`, the component may have unmounted or `data` may have changed (causing `parsed` to change and the effect to re-run). The `cancelled` flag is not re-checked after the `await`. This causes a state update on an unmounted or stale component.

**Fix**:

```tsx
.then(async (json) => {
  if (cancelled) return
  setBaseData(json)

  if (delta) {
    const { patch } = await import('jsondiffpatch')
    if (cancelled) return   // re-check after await
    const patched = patch(structuredClone(json), delta as any)
    setRemoteSnapshot(patched as Record<string, any>)
  } else {
    setRemoteSnapshot(json)
  }
  setLoading(false)
})
```

---

### Important Issues

**4. `patch` mutates even with `structuredClone` — but `diff` receives live `doc` object**

File: `<REPO_ROOT>/haklex/rich-ext-excalidraw/src/ExcalidrawEditRenderer.tsx`, line 108

```tsx
const delta = diff(currentBaseData, doc)
```

`doc` is `{ elements: api.getSceneElements(), ... }` — references to Excalidraw's internal mutable arrays. If `jsondiffpatch.diff` performs deep traversal and Excalidraw mutates the arrays concurrently (possible given the async `import('jsondiffpatch')` await), the diff may be computed on a partially-mutated snapshot. The save call in `useExcalidrawData` already uses `structuredClone` for patching, but `performSave` does not snapshot `doc` before the `await`:

```tsx
const doc = getSnapshot()     // live reference
if (!doc) return Promise.resolve()
// ...
return (async () => {
  // ...
  const { diff } = await import('jsondiffpatch')   // await — doc references may change
  const delta = diff(currentBaseData, doc)          // doc may be stale
})()
```

**Fix**: clone `doc` before the async block:

```tsx
const doc = getSnapshot()
if (!doc) return Promise.resolve()
const frozenDoc = structuredClone(doc)   // snapshot before any await
return (async () => {
  const { diff } = await import('jsondiffpatch')
  const delta = diff(currentBaseData, frozenDoc)
  // ...
})()
```

---

**5. `storageModeRef` is not updated when `storageMode` state changes independently**

File: `<REPO_ROOT>/haklex/rich-ext-excalidraw/src/ExcalidrawEditRenderer.tsx`, lines 65, 280-288

`storageModeRef.current = storageMode` is only set in `handleStorageModeChange`. If `storageMode` is initialized to `'delta'` via the `useState` initializer and `performSave` is called before any user interaction, `storageModeRef.current` is already correct at construction. However, `handleStorageModeChange` calls `setStorageMode` and then immediately calls `performSave()`, but `storageModeRef.current` is updated synchronously before `performSave`, so that path is correct. The pattern is safe but fragile: `storageModeRef` is a manual mirror of `storageMode` state and could drift if `setStorageMode` is called anywhere else. Consider consolidating by removing the ref mirror and relying only on `storageModeRef` for the mutable read path, as is done for `isDirtyRef`.

---

**6. SSR Suspense with lazy-loaded `ExcalidrawDisplayRenderer` — hooks compatibility**

File: `<REPO_ROOT>/haklex/rich-ext-excalidraw/src/ExcalidrawSSRRenderer.tsx`, lines 8-12

The plan (section 1.7) described the SSR renderer as a static placeholder with element count. The implementation went further by wrapping the real `ExcalidrawDisplayRenderer` in `Suspense` + `lazy`. This is architecturally reasonable for client-side lazy loading, but `ExcalidrawDisplayRenderer` internally calls `useColorScheme()` and `usePortalTheme()` — hooks that depend on context providers. If this component is rendered in an SSR context (as the name implies) before those providers are mounted, it will throw.

The `Suspense` + `lazy` pattern prevents the component from being rendered during SSR (the lazy import will suspend), so the fallback placeholder is shown on the server — which is correct. But this only works if Next.js's RSC/SSR environment never resolves the `lazy()` promise synchronously. This is currently safe in Next.js, but represents an implicit assumption that should be documented.

Additionally, `ExcalidrawDisplayRenderer` uses `IntersectionObserver` which does not exist in SSR environments. That component already relies on the lazy-load barrier, but there is no explicit `typeof window !== 'undefined'` guard, making the safety transitive and fragile.

---

**7. `ExcalidrawPlaceholder` silently fails on delta snapshot format**

File: `<REPO_ROOT>/haklex/rich-ext-excalidraw/src/ExcalidrawSSRRenderer.tsx`, lines 14-26

```tsx
try {
  const data = JSON.parse(snapshot)
  // ...
} catch {
  // snapshot might be a URL ref or invalid JSON — show generic placeholder
}
```

For a delta snapshot (URL + newline + JSON delta), `JSON.parse(snapshot)` will throw (multi-line string starting with a URL is not valid JSON). The catch silently degrades to "Excalidraw Whiteboard" with no element count. The original plan said element count should come from `data.elements` for inline snapshots, which is implemented. But for the delta case, the base data is not available at SSR time, so no count is possible — this is acceptable. The comment should say "delta format" explicitly, not just "URL ref", to aid future maintainers.

---

**8. `initialDataRef` captures only the first resolved value**

File: `<REPO_ROOT>/haklex/rich-ext-excalidraw/src/ExcalidrawEditRenderer.tsx`, lines 408-412

```tsx
useEffect(() => {
  if (initialData && !initialDataRef.current) {
    initialDataRef.current = initialData
  }
}, [initialData])
```

This intentionally locks on the first value (to avoid re-initializing the Excalidraw canvas). However, if the parent re-renders with a new `snapshot` prop (e.g., after an external save), `initialDataRef.current` remains stale. The dialog will be opened with outdated initial data. This is an edge case but can cause confusion in scenarios where the snapshot is updated externally and the editor is reopened.

---

### Suggestions

**9. `handleStorageModeChange` calls `performSave()` without awaiting it**

File: `<REPO_ROOT>/haklex/rich-ext-excalidraw/src/ExcalidrawEditRenderer.tsx`, line 285

```tsx
const handleStorageModeChange = useCallback(
  (newMode: 'inline' | 'remote' | 'delta') => {
    setStorageMode(newMode)
    storageModeRef.current = newMode
    if (newMode === 'inline') setSavedRef('')
    performSave()   // fire-and-forget — no void, no await
  },
  [performSave],
)
```

The return value (a `Promise<void>`) is discarded without `void`. While intentional, convention in this codebase uses `void` explicitly for discarded promises (see `handleSaveAndClose`). Also, switching mode while a previous debounced save is in flight could interleave two concurrent saves. The existing `clearTimeout(saveTimerRef.current)` at the top of `performSave` handles the debounce timer, but two concurrent async `performSave` calls (e.g., from mode switch and a debounce expiry) could both proceed. A save-in-flight guard (like `isSaving` already present) should block the second call.

**10. `setTimeout(() => api.scrollToContent(), 100)` is used in three places**

Files: `ExcalidrawDisplayRenderer.tsx` lines 81, 177 and `ExcalidrawEditRenderer.tsx` line 476.

The magic `100ms` delay is fragile across different render speeds. A more robust approach would be to use a `useEffect` on the API ref being set, or respond to Excalidraw's `onReady` callback if available. At minimum, extract the constant.

---

### Plan Alignment

The plan (section 1.10) specified that `useExcalidrawData.ts` should have unchanged "JSON parse/fetch logic". The incremental delta support is an undocumented addition beyond the plan scope. The plan (section 1.6) specified `'inline' | 'remote'` as the two storage modes; `'delta'` is a third unplanned mode. These are improvements rather than regressions, but they significantly increase complexity and introduce the bugs documented above (issues 1-4). The plan's "Gotcha" section should be updated to document the delta format (`URL\ndelta-json`), the jsondiffpatch dependency, and the `baseRef`/`baseData` lifecycle constraints.

---

   179→## Gotchas
   180→
   181→1. **容器尺寸**: excalidraw 需容器有明确 width/height。当前 `aspectRatio: '16/10'` + `width: 100%` 应足够，但需验证全屏 dialog 中 `flex: 1` 能否正确撑开
   182→2. **`initialData` 仅读一次**: 与 tldraw `snapshot` prop 行为一致。预览更新需 key 变化触发 remount
   183→3. **`onChange` 高频触发**: 每次指针移动都会触发，debounce 已有，但需优化 `setIsDirty` 避免冗余 re-render
   184→4. **字体加载**: excalidraw 从 CDN 加载 Virgil/Cascadia 字体，离线环境可能降级
   185→5. **`files` 含二进制**: 画布上粘贴图片会存 base64，inline 存储模式下 snapshot 可能很大

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./haklex/rich-ext-excalidraw/src/ExcalidrawSSRRenderer.tsx`

```text
     1→import type { FC } from 'react'
     2→import { lazy, Suspense } from 'react'
     3→
     4→export interface ExcalidrawSSRRendererProps {
     5→  snapshot: string
     6→}
     7→
     8→const LazyDisplayRenderer = lazy(() =>
     9→  import('./ExcalidrawDisplayRenderer').then((m) => ({
    10→    default: m.ExcalidrawDisplayRenderer,
    11→  })),
    12→)
    13→
    14→const ExcalidrawPlaceholder: FC<{ snapshot: string }> = ({ snapshot }) => {
    15→  let label = 'Excalidraw Whiteboard'
    16→  try {
    17→    const data = JSON.parse(snapshot)
    18→    if (data && typeof data === 'object') {
    19→      const elementCount = Array.isArray(data.elements) ? data.elements.length : 0
    20→      if (elementCount > 0) {
    21→        label = `Excalidraw Whiteboard (${elementCount} elements)`
    22→      }
    23→    }
    24→  } catch {
    25→    // snapshot might be a URL ref or invalid JSON — show generic placeholder
    26→  }
    27→
    28→  return (
    29→    <div
    30→      className="rich-excalidraw-ssr-placeholder"
    31→      style={{
    32→        position: 'relative',
    33→        width: '100%',
    34→        aspectRatio: '16 / 10',
    35→        display: 'flex',
    36→        alignItems: 'center',
    37→        justifyContent: 'center',
    38→        backgroundColor: 'var(--rich-excalidraw-bg, #f5f5f5)',
    39→        borderRadius: '8px',
    40→        border: '1px solid var(--rich-excalidraw-border, #e5e5e5)',
    41→        color: 'var(--rich-excalidraw-fg, #737373)',
    42→        fontSize: '14px',
    43→      }}
    44→      aria-label={label}
    45→    >
    46→      <span>{label}</span>
    47→    </div>
    48→  )
    49→}
    50→
    51→export const ExcalidrawSSRRenderer: FC<ExcalidrawSSRRendererProps> = ({ snapshot }) => {
    52→  return (
    53→    <Suspense fallback={<ExcalidrawPlaceholder snapshot={snapshot} />}>
    54→      <LazyDisplayRenderer snapshot={snapshot} />
    55→    </Suspense>
    56→  )
    57→}
    58→
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./haklex/rich-ext-excalidraw/src/useExcalidrawData.ts`

```text
     1→import { useEffect, useMemo, useState } from 'react'
     2→
     3→import { useExcalidrawConfig } from './ExcalidrawConfigContext'
     4→
     5→export interface ExcalidrawDataState {
     6→  snapshot: Record<string, any> | undefined
     7→  loading: boolean
     8→  error: string
     9→  /** The base reference line (URL or ref:file/xxx) when snapshot is remote or incremental */
    10→  baseRef?: string
    11→  /** The raw base data before delta patch (for computing new diffs in edit mode) */
    12→  baseData?: Record<string, any>
    13→}
    14→
    15→type ParsedData =
    16→  | { type: 'empty' }
    17→  | { type: 'inline'; snapshot: Record<string, any> }
    18→  | { type: 'remote'; fetchUrl: string; refLine: string }
    19→  | { type: 'incremental'; fetchUrl: string; refLine: string; delta: object }
    20→  | { type: 'error'; error: string }
    21→
    22→export function useExcalidrawData(data: string): ExcalidrawDataState {
    23→  const { apiUrl } = useExcalidrawConfig()
    24→
    25→  const parsed = useMemo((): ParsedData => {
    26→    if (!data) return { type: 'empty' }
    27→
    28→    try {
    29→      const json = JSON.parse(data)
    30→      if (json && typeof json === 'object') {
    31→        return { type: 'inline', snapshot: json as Record<string, any> }
    32→      }
    33→    } catch {
    34→      // not JSON — check for URL/ref
    35→    }
    36→
    37→    const lines = data.split('\n')
    38→    const firstLine = lines[0].trim()
    39→    let fetchUrl: string | undefined
    40→    const refLine = firstLine
    41→
    42→    if (firstLine.startsWith('http')) {
    43→      fetchUrl = firstLine
    44→    } else if (firstLine.startsWith('ref:') && apiUrl) {
    45→      fetchUrl = `${apiUrl}/objects/${firstLine.slice(4)}`
    46→    }
    47→
    48→    if (!fetchUrl) {
    49→      return {
    50→        type: 'error',
    51→        error: firstLine.startsWith('ref:')
    52→          ? 'Missing apiUrl for ref resolution'
    53→          : 'Unrecognized snapshot format',
    54→      }
    55→    }
    56→
    57→    // Check for delta diff in remaining lines
    58→    const remainingLines = lines.slice(1).join('\n').trim()
    59→    if (remainingLines) {
    60→      try {
    61→        const delta = JSON.parse(remainingLines)
    62→        if (delta && typeof delta === 'object') {
    63→          return { type: 'incremental', fetchUrl, refLine, delta }
    64→        }
    65→      } catch {
    66→        // not valid JSON delta — treat as plain remote
    67→      }
    68→    }
    69→
    70→    return { type: 'remote', fetchUrl, refLine }
    71→  }, [data, apiUrl])
    72→
    73→  const [remoteSnapshot, setRemoteSnapshot] = useState<
    74→    Record<string, any> | undefined
    75→  >()
    76→  const [baseData, setBaseData] = useState<Record<string, any> | undefined>()
    77→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(
    78→    parsed.type === 'remote' || parsed.type === 'incremental',
    79→  )
    80→  const [error, setError] = useState('')
    81→
    82→  useEffect(() => {
    83→    if (parsed.type !== 'remote' && parsed.type !== 'incremental') return
    84→
    85→    const { fetchUrl } = parsed
    86→    const delta = parsed.type === 'incremental' ? parsed.delta : undefined
    87→
    88→    let cancelled = false
    89→    setLoading(true)
    90→    setError('')
    91→
    92→    fetch(fetchUrl)
    93→      .then((res) => {
    94→        if (!res.ok) throw new Error(`HTTP ${res.status}`)
    95→        return res.json()
    96→      })
    97→      .then(async (json) => {
    98→        if (cancelled) return
    99→        setBaseData(json)
   100→
   101→        if (delta) {
   102→          const { patch } = await import('jsondiffpatch')
   103→          const patched = patch(structuredClone(json), delta as any)
   104→          setRemoteSnapshot(patched as Record<string, any>)
   105→        } else {
   106→          setRemoteSnapshot(json)
   107→        }
   108→        setLoading(false)
   109→      })
   110→      .catch((err) => {
   111→        if (!cancelled) {
   112→          setError(err instanceof Error ? err.message : 'Failed to load')
   113→          setLoading(false)
   114→        }
   115→      })
   116→
   117→    return () => {
   118→      cancelled = true
   119→    }
   120→  }, [parsed])
   121→
   122→  if (parsed.type === 'inline') {
   123→    return { snapshot: parsed.snapshot, loading: false, error: '' }
   124→  }
   125→
   126→  if (parsed.type === 'remote' || parsed.type === 'incremental') {
   127→    return {
   128→      snapshot: remoteSnapshot,
   129→      loading,
   130→      error,
   131→      baseRef: parsed.refLine,
   132→      baseData,
   133→    }
   134→  }
   135→
   136→  if (parsed.type === 'error') {
   137→    return { snapshot: undefined, loading: false, error: parsed.error }
   138→  }
   139→
   140→  return { snapshot: undefined, loading: false, error: '' }
   141→}
   142→
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./haklex/rich-ext-excalidraw/src/ExcalidrawEditRenderer.tsx`

```text
     1→import { useColorScheme } from '@haklex/rich-editor'
     2→import { presentDialog, SegmentedControl } from '@haklex/rich-editor-ui'
     3→import { usePortalTheme } from '@haklex/rich-style-token'
     4→import { Clipboard, Download, Pencil, Upload, X } from 'lucide-react'
     5→import type { FC } from 'react'
     6→import { useCallback, useEffect, useRef, useState } from 'react'
     7→
     8→import * as css from './styles.css'
     9→import { useExcalidrawConfig } from './ExcalidrawConfigContext'
    10→import { useExcalidrawData } from './useExcalidrawData'
    11→
    12→export interface ExcalidrawEditRendererProps {
    13→  snapshot: string
    14→  onSnapshotChange: (snapshot: string) => void
    15→}
    16→
    17→const SAVE_DEBOUNCE_MS = 2000
    18→
    19→// ── Dialog content (fullscreen editor) ──────────────────────────
    20→
    21→const ExcalidrawEditorDialogContent: FC<{
    22→  dismiss: () => void
    23→  ExcalidrawComponent: FC<any>
    24→  initialData: Record<string, any> | undefined
    25→  initialSnapshot: string
    26→  theme: 'light' | 'dark'
    27→  onSave: (ref: string) => void
    28→  saveSnapshot?: (snapshot: object) => Promise<string>
    29→  onClose: (doc?: Record<string, any>) => void
    30→  baseRef?: string
    31→  baseData?: Record<string, any>
    32→}> = ({
    33→  dismiss,
    34→  ExcalidrawComponent,
    35→  initialData,
    36→  initialSnapshot,
    37→  theme,
    38→  onSave,
    39→  saveSnapshot,
    40→  onClose,
    41→  baseRef,
    42→  baseData,
    43→}) => {
    44→  const { className: portalClassName } = usePortalTheme()
    45→  const apiRef = useRef<any>(null)
    46→  const saveTimerRef = useRef<ReturnType<typeof setTimeout>>(undefined)
    47→  const [isDirty, setIsDirty] = useState(false)
    48→  const isDirtyRef = useRef(false)
    49→  const baseRefRef = useRef(baseRef)
    50→  const baseDataRef = useRef(baseData)
    51→  const [storageMode, setStorageMode] = useState<'inline' | 'remote' | 'delta'>(() => {
    52→    if (!saveSnapshot || !initialSnapshot || !initialSnapshot.trim())
    53→      return 'inline'
    54→    try {
    55→      JSON.parse(initialSnapshot)
    56→      return 'inline'
    57→    } catch {
    58→      const lines = initialSnapshot.split('\n')
    59→      if (lines.length > 1 && lines.slice(1).join('\n').trim()) {
    60→        return 'delta'
    61→      }
    62→      return 'remote'
    63→    }
    64→  })
    65→  const storageModeRef = useRef(storageMode)
    66→  const [savedRef, setSavedRef] = useState(() => {
    67→    if (!saveSnapshot || !initialSnapshot || !initialSnapshot.trim()) return ''
    68→    try {
    69→      JSON.parse(initialSnapshot)
    70→      return ''
    71→    } catch {
    72→      return initialSnapshot.split('\n')[0].trim()
    73→    }
    74→  })
    75→  const [isSaving, setIsSaving] = useState(false)
    76→
    77→  useEffect(() => {
    78→    return () => {
    79→      if (saveTimerRef.current) clearTimeout(saveTimerRef.current)
    80→    }
    81→  }, [])
    82→
    83→  const getSnapshot = useCallback(() => {
    84→    const api = apiRef.current
    85→    if (!api) return undefined
    86→    return {
    87→      elements: api.getSceneElements(),
    88→      appState: api.getAppState(),
    89→      files: api.getFiles(),
    90→    }
    91→  }, [])
    92→
    93→  const performSave = useCallback((): Promise<void> => {
    94→    if (saveTimerRef.current) clearTimeout(saveTimerRef.current)
    95→    const doc = getSnapshot()
    96→    if (!doc) return Promise.resolve()
    97→    const mode = storageModeRef.current
    98→
    99→    if (mode === 'delta' && saveSnapshot) {
   100→      setIsSaving(true)
   101→      return (async () => {
   102→        try {
   103→          const currentBaseRef = baseRefRef.current
   104→          const currentBaseData = baseDataRef.current
   105→
   106→          if (currentBaseRef && currentBaseData) {
   107→            const { diff } = await import('jsondiffpatch')
   108→            const delta = diff(currentBaseData, doc)
   109→            onSave(
   110→              delta
   111→                ? [currentBaseRef, JSON.stringify(delta)].join('\n')
   112→                : currentBaseRef,
   113→            )
   114→          } else {
   115→            // No base ref yet — upload full file as new base
   116→            const ref = await saveSnapshot(doc)
   117→            baseRefRef.current = ref
   118→            baseDataRef.current = doc as Record<string, any>
   119→            onSave(ref)
   120→            setSavedRef(ref)
   121→          }
   122→          isDirtyRef.current = false
   123→          setIsDirty(false)
   124→        } finally {
   125→          setIsSaving(false)
   126→        }
   127→      })()
   128→    }
   129→
   130→    if (mode === 'remote' && saveSnapshot) {
   131→      setIsSaving(true)
   132→      return saveSnapshot(doc).then(
   133→        (ref) => {
   134→          onSave(ref)
   135→          baseRefRef.current = ref
   136→          baseDataRef.current = doc as Record<string, any>
   137→          isDirtyRef.current = false
   138→          setIsDirty(false)
   139→          setIsSaving(false)
   140→          setSavedRef(ref)
   141→        },
   142→        () => {
   143→          setIsSaving(false)
   144→        },
   145→      )
   146→    }
   147→
   148→    // inline
   149→    try {
   150→      onSave(JSON.stringify(doc))
   151→      isDirtyRef.current = false
   152→      setIsDirty(false)
   153→    } catch {
   154→      // ignore
   155→    }
   156→    return Promise.resolve()
   157→  }, [onSave, saveSnapshot, getSnapshot])
   158→
   159→  const handleChange = useCallback(() => {
   160→    if (!isDirtyRef.current) {
   161→      isDirtyRef.current = true
   162→      setIsDirty(true)
   163→    }
   164→    if (saveTimerRef.current) clearTimeout(saveTimerRef.current)
   165→    saveTimerRef.current = setTimeout(
   166→      () => performSave(),
   167→      SAVE_DEBOUNCE_MS,
   168→    )
   169→  }, [performSave])
   170→
   171→  const handleSaveAndClose = useCallback(() => {
   172→    void performSave().then(() => {
   173→      const doc = getSnapshot()
   174→      onClose(doc)
   175→      dismiss()
   176→    })
   177→  }, [performSave, getSnapshot, onClose, dismiss])
   178→
   179→  const handleDiscard = useCallback(() => {
   180→    if (saveTimerRef.current) clearTimeout(saveTimerRef.current)
   181→    dismiss()
   182→  }, [dismiss])
   183→
   184→  const showConfirmDialog = useCallback(() => {
   185→    presentDialog({
   186→      title: 'Unsaved Changes',
   187→      description:
   188→        'You have unsaved changes that will be lost if you close now.',
   189→      content: ({ dismiss: dismissConfirm }) => (
   190→        <div className={css.excalidrawConfirmActions}>
   191→          <button
   192→            type="button"
   193→            className={css.excalidrawConfirmBtn}
   194→            onClick={dismissConfirm}
   195→          >
   196→            Continue Editing
   197→          </button>
   198→          <button
   199→            type="button"
   200→            className={css.excalidrawConfirmBtnDanger}
   201→            onClick={() => {
   202→              dismissConfirm()
   203→              handleDiscard()
   204→            }}
   205→          >
   206→            Discard
   207→          </button>
   208→          <button
   209→            type="button"
   210→            className={css.excalidrawConfirmBtnPrimary}
   211→            onClick={() => {
   212→              dismissConfirm()
   213→              handleSaveAndClose()
   214→            }}
   215→          >
   216→            Save & Close
   217→          </button>
   218→        </div>
   219→      ),
   220→      portalClassName,
   221→      theme,
   222→      showCloseButton: false,
   223→      clickOutsideToDismiss: false,
   224→    })
   225→  }, [handleDiscard, handleSaveAndClose, portalClassName, theme])
   226→
   227→  // Intercept ESC in capture phase to prevent Base UI auto-dismiss
   228→  useEffect(() => {
   229→    const handleKeyDown = (e: KeyboardEvent) => {
   230→      if (e.key === 'Escape') {
   231→        e.preventDefault()
   232→        e.stopImmediatePropagation()
   233→        if (isDirtyRef.current) {
   234→          showConfirmDialog()
   235→        } else {
   236→          dismiss()
   237→        }
   238→      }
   239→    }
   240→    document.addEventListener('keydown', handleKeyDown, true)
   241→    return () => document.removeEventListener('keydown', handleKeyDown, true)
   242→  }, [dismiss, showConfirmDialog])
   243→
   244→  const attemptClose = useCallback(() => {
   245→    if (isDirtyRef.current) {
   246→      showConfirmDialog()
   247→    } else {
   248→      dismiss()
   249→    }
   250→  }, [dismiss, showConfirmDialog])
   251→
   252→  const handleManualUpload = useCallback(() => {
   253→    performSave()
   254→  }, [performSave])
   255→
   256→  const handleExportJson = useCallback(() => {
   257→    const doc = getSnapshot()
   258→    if (!doc) return
   259→    const blob = new Blob([JSON.stringify(doc, null, 2)], {
   260→      type: 'application/json',
   261→    })
   262→    const url = URL.createObjectURL(blob)
   263→    const a = document.createElement('a')
   264→    a.href = url
   265→    a.download = 'excalidraw-snapshot.json'
   266→    a.click()
   267→    URL.revokeObjectURL(url)
   268→  }, [getSnapshot])
   269→
   270→  const handleCopyJson = useCallback(() => {
   271→    const doc = getSnapshot()
   272→    if (!doc) return
   273→    void navigator.clipboard.writeText(JSON.stringify(doc))
   274→  }, [getSnapshot])
   275→
   276→  const handleCopyRef = useCallback(() => {
   277→    if (savedRef) void navigator.clipboard.writeText(savedRef)
   278→  }, [savedRef])
   279→
   280→  const handleStorageModeChange = useCallback(
   281→    (newMode: 'inline' | 'remote' | 'delta') => {
   282→      setStorageMode(newMode)
   283→      storageModeRef.current = newMode
   284→      if (newMode === 'inline') setSavedRef('')
   285→      performSave()
   286→    },
   287→    [performSave],
   288→  )
   289→
   290→  return (
   291→    <>
   292→      {/* Header */}
   293→      <div className={css.excalidrawDialogHeader}>
   294→        <span
   295→          className={css.excalidrawStatusDot}
   296→          style={{ backgroundColor: isDirty ? '#f59e0b' : '#22c55e' }}
   297→        />
   298→        <span className={css.excalidrawDialogTitle}>Canvas Editor</span>
   299→        <span className={css.excalidrawDialogMeta}>excalidraw</span>
   300→
   301→        <div className={css.excalidrawHeaderActions}>
   302→          {saveSnapshot && (
   303→            <>
   304→              <SegmentedControl
   305→                items={[
   306→                  { value: 'inline', label: 'Inline' },
   307→                  { value: 'remote', label: 'Remote' },
   308→                  { value: 'delta', label: 'Delta' },
   309→                ]}
   310→                value={storageMode}
   311→                onChange={handleStorageModeChange}
   312→              />
   313→              <div className={css.excalidrawActionBarSep} />
   314→              <button
   315→                type="button"
   316→                className={css.excalidrawActionBarBtn}
   317→                onClick={handleManualUpload}
   318→                disabled={isSaving}
   319→              >
   320→                <Upload size={14} />
   321→                <span>{isSaving ? 'Saving...' : 'Save'}</span>
   322→              </button>
   323→              <div className={css.excalidrawActionBarSep} />
   324→            </>
   325→          )}
   326→          <button
   327→            type="button"
   328→            className={css.excalidrawActionBarBtn}
   329→            onClick={handleExportJson}
   330→          >
   331→            <Download size={14} />
   332→            JSON
   333→          </button>
   334→          <button
   335→            type="button"
   336→            className={css.excalidrawActionBarBtn}
   337→            onClick={handleCopyJson}
   338→          >
   339→            <Clipboard size={14} />
   340→            Copy
   341→          </button>
   342→          {storageMode === 'remote' && savedRef && (
   343→            <>
   344→              <div className={css.excalidrawActionBarSep} />
   345→              <button
   346→                type="button"
   347→                className={css.excalidrawActionBarUrl}
   348→                onClick={handleCopyRef}
   349→                title={savedRef}
   350→              >
   351→                {savedRef}
   352→              </button>
   353→            </>
   354→          )}
   355→        </div>
   356→
   357→        <button
   358→          type="button"
   359→          className={css.excalidrawHeaderClose}
   360→          onClick={attemptClose}
   361→        >
   362→          <X size={18} />
   363→        </button>
   364→      </div>
   365→
   366→      {/* Canvas */}
   367→      <div className={css.excalidrawDialogCanvas}>
   368→        <ExcalidrawComponent
   369→          initialData={initialData}
   370→          theme={theme}
   371→          onChange={handleChange}
   372→          excalidrawAPI={(api: any) => {
   373→            apiRef.current = api
   374→          }}
   375→        />
   376→      </div>
   377→    </>
   378→  )
   379→}
   380→
   381→// ── Main component ──────────────────────────────────────────────
   382→
   383→export const ExcalidrawEditRenderer: FC<ExcalidrawEditRendererProps> = ({
   384→  snapshot,
   385→  onSnapshotChange,
   386→}) => {
   387→  const theme = useColorScheme()
   388→  const { saveSnapshot } = useExcalidrawConfig()
   389→  const {
   390→    snapshot: initialData,
   391→    loading: dataLoading,
   392→    error: dataError,
   393→    baseRef,
   394→    baseData,
   395→  } = useExcalidrawData(snapshot)
   396→  const [ExcalidrawComponent, setExcalidrawComponent] = useState<FC<any> | null>(null)

   398→  const previewApiRef = useRef<any>(null)
   399→  const initialDataRef = useRef<Record<string, any> | undefined>(undefined)
   400→  const onSnapshotChangeRef = useRef(onSnapshotChange)
   401→  onSnapshotChangeRef.current = onSnapshotChange
   402→  const saveSnapshotRef = useRef(saveSnapshot)
   403→  saveSnapshotRef.current = saveSnapshot
   404→  const snapshotRef = useRef(snapshot)
   405→  snapshotRef.current = snapshot
   406→  const { className: portalClassName } = usePortalTheme()
   407→
   408→  useEffect(() => {
   409→    if (initialData && !initialDataRef.current) {
   410→      initialDataRef.current = initialData
   411→    }
   412→  }, [initialData])
   413→
   414→  useEffect(() => {
   415→    import('@excalidraw/excalidraw')
   416→      .then((mod) => {
   417→        setExcalidrawComponent(() => mod.Excalidraw)
   418→        setLibLoading(false)
   419→      })
   420→      .catch(() => setLibLoading(false))
   421→  }, [])

   423→  const handleOpenEditor = useCallback(() => {

   437→          }}
   438→          baseRef={baseRef}
   439→          baseData={baseData}
   440→        />
   441→      ),

   444→      theme,
   445→      showCloseButton: false,
   446→      clickOutsideToDismiss: false,
   447→    })

   450→  const loading = dataLoading || libLoading

   452→  if (loading || !ExcalidrawComponent) {

   454→      <div className={css.excalidrawEditorContainer}>

   456→      </div>
   457→    )
   458→  }
   459→
   460→  if (dataError) {

   464→      </div>
   465→    )
   466→  }
   467→
   468→  return (
   469→    <div className={css.excalidrawEditorContainer}>
   470→      <ExcalidrawComponent
   471→        initialData={initialDataRef.current}
   472→        viewModeEnabled
   473→        theme={theme}
   474→        excalidrawAPI={(api: any) => {
   475→          previewApiRef.current = api
   476→          setTimeout(() => api.scrollToContent(), 100)
   477→        }}
   478→      />
   479→
   480→      <button
   481→        type="button"
   482→        className={css.excalidrawEditOverlay}
   483→        onClick={handleOpenEditor}
   484→      >
   485→        <span className={css.excalidrawEditLabel}>
   486→          <Pencil size={16} />
   487→          Edit Whiteboard
   488→        </span>
   489→      </button>
   490→    </div>
   491→  )
   492→}

     5→  apiUrl?: string

    11→export function ExcalidrawConfigProvider({
    12→  apiUrl,
    13→  saveSnapshot,

    20→  return (

    22→      {children}
    23→    </ExcalidrawConfigContext.Provider>
    24→  )
    25→}
    26→
    27→export function useExcalidrawConfig(): ExcalidrawConfig {
    28→  return use(ExcalidrawConfigContext)
    29→}
    30→

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-03-02 11:39:19Z)**_

The entire package is untracked. Now I have full context. Let me do the thorough review.

---

## Code Review: `@haklex/rich-ext-excalidraw` Delta / SSR Changes

### What Was Done Well

The incremental delta approach is architecturally sound — separating parse from fetch, using a cancellation flag in the `useEffect`, and lazy-loading `jsondiffpatch` only when needed are all correct instincts. The `ExcalidrawPlaceholder` in the SSR renderer provides a graceful degradation that matches the original plan.

---

### Critical Issues

**1. `baseDataRef` is never synced after `useExcalidrawData` resolves**

File: `<REPO_ROOT>/haklex/rich-ext-excalidraw/src/ExcalidrawEditRenderer.tsx`, lines 437-439

```tsx
baseRef={baseRef}
baseData={baseData}
```

These props are passed to `ExcalidrawEditorDialogContent` once at dialog-open time and captured into `useRef` at construction:

```tsx
const baseRefRef = useRef(baseRef)
const baseDataRef = useRef(baseData)
```

But if the dialog opens while `useExcalidrawData` is still loading (i.e., `baseData` is `undefined`), the refs are initialized to `undefined` and **never updated afterward**. Because `presentDialog` renders the content component once, changes to `baseRef`/`baseData` in the parent after the dialog opens are invisible to the dialog's refs.

The consequence: on the very first open of a delta-mode canvas (while the async fetch is still in flight), `performSave` takes the `else` branch (lines 115-120) and uploads the full document as a new base, silently discarding the delta relationship. This is a silent data-correctness bug. The loading guard in the parent (`if (loading || !ExcalidrawComponent) return`) only covers the inline loading indicator — the dialog can still be opened after lib loads but before data loads, because `handleOpenEditor` only checks `!ExcalidrawComponent` (line 424).

**Fix**: Do not open the editor dialog until both `!libLoading` and `!dataLoading`. The existing `loading` computed value (line 450) already captures this, but `handleOpenEditor` (line 423) only guards on `ExcalidrawComponent`:

```tsx
// Current — insufficient guard
const handleOpenEditor = useCallback(() => {
  if (!ExcalidrawComponent) return
  // ...
}, [ExcalidrawComponent, ...])

// Required
const handleOpenEditor = useCallback(() => {
  if (!ExcalidrawComponent || dataLoading) return
  // ...
}, [ExcalidrawComponent, dataLoading, ...])
```

---

**2. Race condition in `delta` mode `performSave` when `baseRef` exists but `baseData` is absent**

File: `<REPO_ROOT>/haklex/rich-ext-excalidraw/src/ExcalidrawEditRenderer.tsx`, lines 106-113

```tsx
if (currentBaseRef && currentBaseData) {
  const { diff } = await import('jsondiffpatch')
  const delta = diff(currentBaseData, doc)
  onSave(...)
} else {
  // No base ref yet — upload full file as new base
```

The comment says "No base ref yet" but `currentBaseRef` can be truthy while `currentBaseData` is falsy if the fetch is still in flight. This is the same race as issue 1, but from the save side: a partial state causes a silent fallback to a full upload with a new base ref, destroying the existing base reference.

---

**3. `setBaseData` called before `setRemoteSnapshot` but not guarded for cancellation**

File: `<REPO_ROOT>/haklex/rich-ext-excalidraw/src/useExcalidrawData.ts`, lines 98-108

```tsx
.then(async (json) => {
  if (cancelled) return
  setBaseData(json)        // state update — safe, cancelled checked

  if (delta) {
    const { patch } = await import('jsondiffpatch')
    const patched = patch(structuredClone(json), delta as any)
    // ── await point: component may unmount here ──
    setRemoteSnapshot(patched as Record<string, any>)  // no cancelled re-check
  } else {
    setRemoteSnapshot(json)
  }
  setLoading(false)
})
```

After `await import('jsondiffpatch')`, the component may have unmounted or `data` may have changed (causing `parsed` to change and the effect to re-run). The `cancelled` flag is not re-checked after the `await`. This causes a state update on an unmounted or stale component.

**Fix**:

```tsx
.then(async (json) => {
  if (cancelled) return
  setBaseData(json)

  if (delta) {
    const { patch } = await import('jsondiffpatch')
    if (cancelled) return   // re-check after await
    const patched = patch(structuredClone(json), delta as any)
    setRemoteSnapshot(patched as Record<string, any>)
  } else {
    setRemoteSnapshot(json)
  }
  setLoading(false)
})
```

---

### Important Issues

**4. `patch` mutates even with `structuredClone` — but `diff` receives live `doc` object**

File: `<REPO_ROOT>/haklex/rich-ext-excalidraw/src/ExcalidrawEditRenderer.tsx`, line 108

```tsx
const delta = diff(currentBaseData, doc)
```

`doc` is `{ elements: api.getSceneElements(), ... }` — references to Excalidraw's internal mutable arrays. If `jsondiffpatch.diff` performs deep traversal and Excalidraw mutates the arrays concurrently (possible given the async `import('jsondiffpatch')` await), the diff may be computed on a partially-mutated snapshot. The save call in `useExcalidrawData` already uses `structuredClone` for patching, but `performSave` does not snapshot `doc` before the `await`:

```tsx
const doc = getSnapshot()     // live reference
if (!doc) return Promise.resolve()
// ...
return (async () => {
  // ...
  const { diff } = await import('jsondiffpatch')   // await — doc references may change
  const delta = diff(currentBaseData, doc)          // doc may be stale
})()
```

**Fix**: clone `doc` before the async block:

```tsx
const doc = getSnapshot()
if (!doc) return Promise.resolve()
const frozenDoc = structuredClone(doc)   // snapshot before any await
return (async () => {
  const { diff } = await import('jsondiffpatch')
  const delta = diff(currentBaseData, frozenDoc)
  // ...
})()
```

---

**5. `storageModeRef` is not updated when `storageMode` state changes independently**

File: `<REPO_ROOT>/haklex/rich-ext-excalidraw/src/ExcalidrawEditRenderer.tsx`, lines 65, 280-288

`storageModeRef.current = storageMode` is only set in `handleStorageModeChange`. If `storageMode` is initialized to `'delta'` via the `useState` initializer and `performSave` is called before any user interaction, `storageModeRef.current` is already correct at construction. However, `handleStorageModeChange` calls `setStorageMode` and then immediately calls `performSave()`, but `storageModeRef.current` is updated synchronously before `performSave`, so that path is correct. The pattern is safe but fragile: `storageModeRef` is a manual mirror of `storageMode` state and could drift if `setStorageMode` is called anywhere else. Consider consolidating by removing the ref mirror and relying only on `storageModeRef` for the mutable read path, as is done for `isDirtyRef`.

---

**6. SSR Suspense with lazy-loaded `ExcalidrawDisplayRenderer` — hooks compatibility**

File: `<REPO_ROOT>/haklex/rich-ext-excalidraw/src/ExcalidrawSSRRenderer.tsx`, lines 8-12

The plan (section 1.7) described the SSR renderer as a static placeholder with element count. The implementation went further by wrapping the real `ExcalidrawDisplayRenderer` in `Suspense` + `lazy`. This is architecturally reasonable for client-side lazy loading, but `ExcalidrawDisplayRenderer` internally calls `useColorScheme()` and `usePortalTheme()` — hooks that depend on context providers. If this component is rendered in an SSR context (as the name implies) before those providers are mounted, it will throw.

The `Suspense` + `lazy` pattern prevents the component from being rendered during SSR (the lazy import will suspend), so the fallback placeholder is shown on the server — which is correct. But this only works if Next.js's RSC/SSR environment never resolves the `lazy()` promise synchronously. This is currently safe in Next.js, but represents an implicit assumption that should be documented.

Additionally, `ExcalidrawDisplayRenderer` uses `IntersectionObserver` which does not exist in SSR environments. That component already relies on the lazy-load barrier, but there is no explicit `typeof window !== 'undefined'` guard, making the safety transitive and fragile.

---

**7. `ExcalidrawPlaceholder` silently fails on delta snapshot format**

File: `<REPO_ROOT>/haklex/rich-ext-excalidraw/src/ExcalidrawSSRRenderer.tsx`, lines 14-26

```tsx
try {
  const data = JSON.parse(snapshot)
  // ...
} catch {
  // snapshot might be a URL ref or invalid JSON — show generic placeholder
}
```

For a delta snapshot (URL + newline + JSON delta), `JSON.parse(snapshot)` will throw (multi-line string starting with a URL is not valid JSON). The catch silently degrades to "Excalidraw Whiteboard" with no element count. The original plan said element count should come from `data.elements` for inline snapshots, which is implemented. But for the delta case, the base data is not available at SSR time, so no count is possible — this is acceptable. The comment should say "delta format" explicitly, not just "URL ref", to aid future maintainers.

---

**8. `initialDataRef` captures only the first resolved value**

File: `<REPO_ROOT>/haklex/rich-ext-excalidraw/src/ExcalidrawEditRenderer.tsx`, lines 408-412

```tsx
useEffect(() => {
  if (initialData && !initialDataRef.current) {
    initialDataRef.current = initialData
  }
}, [initialData])
```

This intentionally locks on the first value (to avoid re-initializing the Excalidraw canvas). However, if the parent re-renders with a new `snapshot` prop (e.g., after an external save), `initialDataRef.current` remains stale. The dialog will be opened with outdated initial data. This is an edge case but can cause confusion in scenarios where the snapshot is updated externally and the editor is reopened.

---

### Suggestions

**9. `handleStorageModeChange` calls `performSave()` without awaiting it**

File: `<REPO_ROOT>/haklex/rich-ext-excalidraw/src/ExcalidrawEditRenderer.tsx`, line 285

```tsx
const handleStorageModeChange = useCallback(
  (newMode: 'inline' | 'remote' | 'delta') => {
    setStorageMode(newMode)
    storageModeRef.current = newMode
    if (newMode === 'inline') setSavedRef('')
    performSave()   // fire-and-forget — no void, no await
  },
  [performSave],
)
```

The return value (a `Promise<void>`) is discarded without `void`. While intentional, convention in this codebase uses `void` explicitly for discarded promises (see `handleSaveAndClose`). Also, switching mode while a previous debounced save is in flight could interleave two concurrent saves. The existing `clearTimeout(saveTimerRef.current)` at the top of `performSave` handles the debounce timer, but two concurrent async `performSave` calls (e.g., from mode switch and a debounce expiry) could both proceed. A save-in-flight guard (like `isSaving` already present) should block the second call.

**10. `setTimeout(() => api.scrollToContent(), 100)` is used in three places**

Files: `ExcalidrawDisplayRenderer.tsx` lines 81, 177 and `ExcalidrawEditRenderer.tsx` line 476.

The magic `100ms` delay is fragile across different render speeds. A more robust approach would be to use a `useEffect` on the API ref being set, or respond to Excalidraw's `onReady` callback if available. At minimum, extract the constant.

---

### Plan Alignment

The plan (section 1.10) specified that `useExcalidrawData.ts` should have unchanged "JSON parse/fetch logic". The incremental delta support is an undocumented addition beyond the plan scope. The plan (section 1.6) specified `'inline' | 'remote'` as the two storage modes; `'delta'` is a third unplanned mode. These are improvements rather than regressions, but they significantly increase complexity and introduce the bugs documented above (issues 1-4). The plan's "Gotcha" section should be updated to document the delta format (`URL\ndelta-json`), the jsondiffpatch dependency, and the `baseRef`/`baseData` lifecycle constraints.

---

### Summary Table

| # | Severity | Location | Issue |
|---|----------|----------|-------|
| 1 | Critical | `ExcalidrawEditRenderer.tsx:424` | Dialog opens before data loads, `baseDataRef` is `undefined`, silent full-upload fallback |
| 2 | Critical | `ExcalidrawEditRenderer.tsx:106` | `baseRef` truthy + `baseData` falsy race destroys delta relationship |
| 3 | Critical | `useExcalidrawData.ts:102` | Missing `cancelled` re-check after `await import('jsondiffpatch')` |
| 4 | Important | `ExcalidrawEditRenderer.tsx:108` | Live `doc` reference passed to async `diff()` without pre-cloning |
| 5 | Important | `ExcalidrawEditRenderer.tsx:65,280` | `storageModeRef` manual mirror is fragile |
| 6 | Important | `ExcalidrawSSRRenderer.tsx:8` | SSR safety relies on implicit lazy-load barrier, not explicit guard |
| 7 | Suggestion | `ExcalidrawSSRRenderer.tsx:24` | Placeholder comment should mention delta format |
| 8 | Suggestion | `ExcalidrawEditRenderer.tsx:409` | `initialDataRef` stale on external snapshot update |
| 9 | Suggestion | `ExcalidrawEditRenderer.tsx:285` | Mode-switch save: missing `void`, concurrent save risk |
| 10 | Suggestion | Multiple | Magic `100ms` scroll delay duplicated 3 times |

构建通过，无 TS 错误。

已修复全部关键问题:

1. **`useExcalidrawData`**: `await import('jsondiffpatch')` 后增加 `cancelled` 重检，防止卸载后状态更新
2. **`handleOpenEditor`**: 增加 `dataLoading` 守卫，确保 `baseData` 就绪后才开对话框；`baseRefRef`/`baseDataRefOuter` 随异步数据同步更新
3. **`performSave` delta 模式**: `structuredClone(doc)` 冻结快照再送入异步 `diff()`，防止引用漂移
4. **SSR placeholder 注释**: 补充 delta 格式说明

**变更总结**:

| 文件 | 改动 |
|------|------|
| `ExcalidrawSSRRenderer.tsx` | Suspense + lazy DisplayRenderer，placeholder 作 fallback |
| `useExcalidrawData.ts` | 支持增量格式 `URL\ndelta-json`，fetch 基础数据后 `patch()` 合成 |
| `ExcalidrawEditRenderer.tsx` | 三模式存储 (inline/remote/delta)，delta 保存时 `diff()` 计算增量 |
| `package.json` | 加 `jsondiffpatch: ^0.6.0` |

Both 0.17.0 and 0.18.0 export `Excalidraw` as a named export. The type signatures are similar:
- 0.17.0: `export declare const Excalidraw: React.MemoExoticComponent<(props: ExcalidrawProps) => JSX.Element>`
- 0.18.0: `export declare const Excalidraw: React.MemoExoticComponent<(props: ExcalidrawProps) => import("react/jsx-runtime").JSX.Element>`

So the `Excalidraw` component export exists in both versions. 

The props we're using (`initialData`, `viewModeEnabled`, `theme`, `excalidrawAPI`, `onChange`) should work with both versions. There's also a React version mismatch: 0.17.0 only supports React 17 and 18, not 19, which could be causing runtime errors. Let me double-check what 0.17.0's entry point actually exports.

     1→import { Excalidraw as Board, exportToBlob } from '@excalidraw/excalidraw'
     2→import type { ExcalidrawElement } from '@excalidraw/excalidraw/types/element/types'
     3→import type {
     4→  AppState,
     5→  BinaryFiles,
     6→  ExcalidrawImperativeAPI,
     7→} from '@excalidraw/excalidraw/types/types'
     8→import { useQuery } from '@tanstack/react-query'
     9→import type { Delta } from 'jsondiffpatch'
    10→import { patch } from 'jsondiffpatch'
    11→import * as React from 'react'
    12→import {
    13→  useEffect,
    14→  useImperativeHandle,
    15→  useMemo,
    16→  useRef,
    17→  useState,
    18→} from 'react'
    19→
    20→import { useIsMobile } from '~/atoms/hooks/viewport'
    21→import { API_URL } from '~/constants/env'
    22→import { useIsDark } from '~/hooks/common/use-is-dark'
    23→import { stopPropagation } from '~/lib/dom'
    24→import { clsxm, safeJsonParse } from '~/lib/helper'
    25→import { cloneDeep } from '~/lib/lodash'
    26→import { toast } from '~/lib/toast'
    27→
    28→import { MotionButtonBase } from '../button'
    29→import { useModalStack } from '../modal'
    30→
    31→export interface ExcalidrawProps {
    32→  zenModeEnabled?: boolean
    33→  viewModeEnabled?: boolean
    34→  showExtendButton?: boolean
    35→  onChange?: (
    36→    elements: readonly ExcalidrawElement[],
    37→    appState: AppState,
    38→    files: BinaryFiles,
    39→  ) => void
    40→  className?: string
    41→  onReady?: (api: ExcalidrawImperativeAPI) => void
    42→
    43→  ////
    44→  data?: object
    45→  refUrl?: string
    46→  patchDiffDelta?: Delta
    47→}
    48→
    49→export interface ExcalidrawRefObject {
    50→  getRefData: () => ExcalidrawElement | null | undefined
    51→  getDiffDelta: () => Delta | null | undefined
    52→}
    53→export const Excalidraw = ({
    54→  ref,
    55→  ...props
    56→}: Omit<ExcalidrawProps, 'refUrl' | 'patchDiffDelta' | 'data'> & {
    57→  data: string
    58→} & { ref?: React.RefObject<ExcalidrawRefObject | null> }) => {
    59→  const { data, ...rest } = props
    60→  const transformedProps: {
    61→    data?: ExcalidrawElement
    62→    refUrl?: string
    63→    patchDiffDelta?: Delta
    64→  } = useMemo(() => {
    65→    if (!data) return {}
    66→    const tryParseJson = safeJsonParse(data)
    67→    if (!tryParseJson) {
    68→      // 1. data 是 string，取第一行判断
    69→      const splittedLines = data.split('\n')
    70→      const firstLine = splittedLines[0]
    71→      const otherLines = splittedLines.slice(1).join('\n')
    72→
    73→      const props = {} as any
    74→      // 第一行是地址
    75→      if (firstLine.startsWith('http')) {
    76→        props.refUrl = firstLine
    77→      }
    78→      // 第一行是 ref:file/:filename
    79→      // 命中后端文件
    80→      else if (firstLine.startsWith('ref:')) {
    81→        props.refUrl = `${API_URL}/objects/${firstLine.slice(4)}`
    82→      }
    83→
    84→      if (otherLines.trim().length > 0) {
    85→        // 识别为其他行是 delta diff
    86→
    87→        props.patchDiffDelta = safeJsonParse(otherLines)
    88→      }
    89→
    90→      return props
    91→    } else {
    92→      return {
    93→        data: tryParseJson as ExcalidrawElement,
    94→      }
    95→    }
    96→  }, [data])
    97→
    98→  const internalRef = useRef<InternelExcalidrawRefObject>(null)
    99→  const containerRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null)
   100→  const [hasEnteredView, setHasEnteredView] = useState(false)
   101→
   102→  useEffect(() => {
   103→    if (hasEnteredView) return
   104→    const target = containerRef.current
   105→    if (!target) return
   106→
   107→    const observer = new IntersectionObserver(
   108→      (entries) => {
   109→        const isIntersecting = entries.some((entry) => entry.isIntersecting)
   110→        if (isIntersecting) {
   111→          setHasEnteredView(true)
   112→          observer.disconnect()
   113→        }
   114→      },
   115→      {
   116→        rootMargin: '200px 0px',
   117→        threshold: 0.1,
   118→      },
   119→    )
   120→
   121→    observer.observe(target)
   122→
   123→    return () => observer.disconnect()
   124→  }, [hasEnteredView])
   125→
   126→  useImperativeHandle(ref, () => ({
   127→    getRefData() {
   128→      return internalRef.current?.getRemoteData()
   129→    },
   130→    getDiffDelta() {
   131→      return transformedProps.patchDiffDelta
   132→    },
   133→  }))
   134→
   135→  if (!hasEnteredView) {
   136→    return (
   137→      <div
   138→        ref={containerRef}
   139→        className={clsxm('relative h-[500px] w-full', props.className)}
   140→      >
   141→        <div className="center absolute inset-0 z-10 flex">
   142→          <div className="loading loading-spinner" />
   143→        </div>
   144→      </div>
   145→    )
   146→  }
   147→
   148→  return (
   149→    <div ref={containerRef}>
   150→      <ExcalidrawImpl ref={internalRef} {...rest} {...transformedProps} />
   151→    </div>
   152→  )
   153→}
   154→
   155→Excalidraw.displayName = 'Excalidraw'
   156→
   157→interface InternelExcalidrawRefObject {
   158→  getRemoteData: () => ExcalidrawElement | null | undefined
   159→}
   160→
   161→const ExcalidrawImpl = ({
   162→  ref,
   163→  data,
   164→  refUrl,
   165→  patchDiffDelta,
   166→  viewModeEnabled = true,
   167→  zenModeEnabled = true,
   168→  onChange,
   169→  className,
   170→  showExtendButton = true,
   171→  onReady,
   172→}: ExcalidrawProps & {
   173→  ref?: React.RefObject<InternelExcalidrawRefObject | null>
   174→}) => {
   175→  const excalidrawAPIRef = React.useRef<ExcalidrawImperativeAPI>(undefined)
   176→  const modal = useModalStack()
   177→  const isMobile = useIsMobile()
   178→
   179→  const { data: refData, isLoading } = useQuery({
   180→    queryKey: ['excalidraw', refUrl],
   181→    queryFn: async ({ queryKey }) => {
   182→      const [, queryRefUrl] = queryKey
   183→      if (typeof queryRefUrl !== 'string') {
   184→        throw new TypeError('Excalidraw ref url is invalid')
   185→      }
   186→      const res = await fetch(queryRefUrl)
   187→      return await res.json()
   188→    },
   189→    enabled: !!refUrl,
   190→  })
   191→
   192→  useImperativeHandle(ref, () => ({
   193→    getRemoteData() {
   194→      return refData
   195→    },
   196→  }))
   197→
   198→  const finalDataIfRefUrl = useMemo(() => {
   199→    if (!refData) return null
   200→
   201→    return patch(cloneDeep(refData), patchDiffDelta)
   202→  }, [refData, patchDiffDelta])
   203→
   204→  const isDarkMode = useIsDark()
   205→
   206→  const finalData = useMemo(() => {
   207→    const finalData = data || finalDataIfRefUrl
   208→    if (!finalData && !isLoading) {
   209→      console.error('Excalidraw: data not exist')
   210→    }
   211→    return finalData as ExcalidrawElement
   212→  }, [data, finalDataIfRefUrl, isLoading])
   213→
   214→  return (
   215→    <div
   216→      onKeyDown={stopPropagation}
   217→      onKeyUp={stopPropagation}
   218→      className={clsxm('relative h-[500px] w-full', className)}
   219→    >
   220→      {isLoading && (
   221→        <div className="center absolute inset-0 z-10 flex">
   222→          <div className="loading loading-spinner" />
   223→        </div>
   224→      )}
   225→      <>
   226→        <Board
   227→          key={
   228→            refUrl ? `excalidraw-refData-loading-${isLoading}` : 'excalidraw'
   229→          }
   230→          theme={isDarkMode ? 'dark' : 'light'}
   231→          initialData={finalData}
   232→          detectScroll={false}
   233→          zenModeEnabled={zenModeEnabled}
   234→          onChange={onChange}
   235→          viewModeEnabled={viewModeEnabled}
   236→          excalidrawAPI={(api) => {
   237→            excalidrawAPIRef.current = api
   238→
   239→            setTimeout(() => {
   240→              api.scrollToContent(undefined, {
   241→                fitToContent: true,
   242→              })
   243→            }, 300)
   244→
   245→            onReady?.(api)
   246→          }}
   247→        />
   248→
   249→        {viewModeEnabled && showExtendButton && (
   250→          <MotionButtonBase
   251→            onClick={() => {
   252→              if (!excalidrawAPIRef.current) {
   253→                toast.error('Excalidraw API not ready')
   254→                return
   255→              }
   256→
   257→              const elements = excalidrawAPIRef.current.getSceneElements()
   258→              if (isMobile) {
   259→                const win = window.open()
   260→                const blob = exportToBlob({
   261→                  elements,
   262→                  files: null,
   263→                })
   264→                blob.then((blob) => {
   265→                  win?.location.replace(URL.createObjectURL(blob))
   266→                })
   267→              } else {
   268→                modal.present({
   269→                  title: 'Preview',
   270→                  content: () => (
   271→                    <ExcalidrawImpl
   272→                      data={data}
   273→                      className="h-full"
   274→                      showExtendButton={false}
   275→                      refUrl={refUrl}
   276→                    />
   277→                  ),
   278→                  clickOutsideToDismiss: true,
   279→                  max: true,
   280→                })
   281→              }
   282→            }}
   283→            className={clsxm(
   284→              'center absolute bottom-2 right-2 z-10 box-content flex size-5 rounded-md border p-2',
   285→              'border-zinc-200 bg-base-100 text-zinc-600',
   286→              'dark:border-neutral-800 dark:text-zinc-500',
   287→            )}
   288→          >
   289→            <i className="i-mingcute-external-link-line" />
   290→          </MotionButtonBase>
   291→        )}
   292→      </>
   293→    </div>
   294→  )
   295→}
   296→ExcalidrawImpl.displayName = 'ExcalidrawImpl'
   297→

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-02 12:24:55Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now I see the key differences with excalidraw 0.17.0:

1. **Import types from sub-paths**: 0.17.0 uses `@excalidraw/excalidraw/types/element/types` and `@excalidraw/excalidraw/types/types` for types. 0.18.0 likely has different type paths.

2. **`Excalidraw` component**: Available in both versions as `Board` import.

3. **API**: The web app uses:
   - `Board` component (renamed from `Excalidraw`)

    17→  for (const entry of readdirSync(packagesDir)) {
    18→    if (entry.startsWith('rich-') && entry !== 'rich-editor-demo') {
    19→      const srcDir = resolve(packagesDir, entry, 'src')
    20→      if (existsSync(srcDir)) {
    21→        srcDirs.push(srcDir)
    22→      }
    23→    }
    24→  }
    25→
    26→  return {
    27→    name: 'watch-workspace-packages',
    28→    apply: 'serve',
    29→    configureServer(server) {
    30→      for (const dir of srcDirs) {
    31→        server.watcher.add(dir)
    32→      }
    33→      server.watcher.on('change', (file) => {
    34→        if (!srcDirs.some((dir) => file.startsWith(dir))) return
    35→        const mods = server.moduleGraph.getModulesByFile(file)
    36→        if (mods?.size) {
    37→          for (const mod of mods) server.moduleGraph.invalidateModule(mod)
    38→        }
    39→        server.hot.send({ type: 'full-reload' })
    40→      })
    41→    },
    42→  }
    43→}
    44→
    45→function workspaceCssPlugin(): Plugin {
    46→  const scopes = new Set(['@haklex', '@shiro'])
    47→  const EMPTY_PREFIX = '\0empty-css:'
    48→  const VE_PREFIX = '\0ve-css:'
    49→  const RENDERER_STYLE_PREFIX = '\0renderer-style:'
    50→
    51→  return {
    52→    name: 'workspace-css-dev',
    53→    apply: 'serve',
    54→    enforce: 'pre',
    55→    resolveId(id, importer) {
    56→      // style-renderer.css: plain CSS @import breaks HMR for upstream VE packages.
    57→      // Resolve to style-renderer.ts so each pkg/style.css gets proper VE resolution.
    58→      const rendererStyleMatch = id.match(
    59→        /^@([\w-]+)\/([\w-]+)\/style-renderer\.css$/,
    60→      )
    61→      if (rendererStyleMatch) {
    62→        const scope = `@${rendererStyleMatch[1]}`
    63→        const pkgDir = rendererStyleMatch[2]
    64→        if (scopes.has(scope)) {
    65→          const srcRendererTs = resolve(
    66→            __dirname,
    67→            `../${pkgDir}/src/style-renderer.ts`,
    68→          )
    69→          if (existsSync(srcRendererTs)) {
    70→            return `${RENDERER_STYLE_PREFIX}${srcRendererTs}`
    71→          }
    72→        }
    73→      }
    74→
    75→      const m = id.match(/^@([\w-]+)\/([\w-]+)\/style\.css$/)
    76→      if (!m) return null
    77→
    78→      const scope = `@${m[1]}`
    79→      if (!scopes.has(scope)) return null
    80→
    81→      const pkgDir = m[2]
    82→      const srcIndex = resolve(__dirname, `../${pkgDir}/src/index.ts`)
    83→      const srcCss = resolve(__dirname, `../${pkgDir}/src/style.css`)
    84→      const srcVeCss = resolve(__dirname, `../${pkgDir}/src/styles.css.ts`)
    85→
    86→      if (existsSync(srcIndex)) {
    87→        if (importer?.endsWith('.css')) {
    88→          // Keep CSS graph valid; JS graph will import source modules and emit VE CSS.
    89→          return existsSync(srcCss) ? srcCss : `${EMPTY_PREFIX}${id}`
    90→        }
    91→        return `${VE_PREFIX}${srcIndex}`
    92→      }
    93→
    94→      if (existsSync(srcCss)) {
    95→        return srcCss
    96→      }
    97→
    98→      if (!existsSync(srcVeCss)) {
    99→        return null
   100→      }
   101→
   102→      // For CSS @import, VE source cannot be parsed as CSS.
   103→      if (importer?.endsWith('.css')) {
   104→        return `${EMPTY_PREFIX}${id}`
   105→      }
   106→
   107→      // For JS imports, route style.css to VE source for real-time compilation.
   108→      return `${VE_PREFIX}${srcVeCss}`
   109→    },
   110→    load(id) {
   111→      if (id.startsWith(EMPTY_PREFIX)) return ''
   112→      if (id.startsWith(VE_PREFIX)) {
   113→        const sourcePath = id.slice(VE_PREFIX.length)
   114→        return `import ${JSON.stringify(sourcePath)}`
   115→      }
   116→      if (id.startsWith(RENDERER_STYLE_PREFIX)) {
   117→        const sourcePath = id.slice(RENDERER_STYLE_PREFIX.length)
   118→        return `import ${JSON.stringify(sourcePath)}`
   119→      }
   120→    },
   121→  }
   122→}
   123→
   124→export default defineConfig({
   125→  plugins: [
   126→    watchWorkspacePlugin(),
   127→    workspaceCssPlugin(),
   128→    vanillaExtractPlugin(),
   129→    codeInspectorPlugin({ bundler: 'vite', hotKeys: ['altKey'] }),
   130→    react(),
   131→  ],
   132→  server: {
   133→    port: 5188,
   134→    open: true,
   135→  },
   136→  optimizeDeps: {
   137→    include: ['react-intersection-observer', 'react-photo-view'],
   138→  },
   139→  resolve: {
   140→    dedupe: [
   141→      'lexical',
   142→      '@lexical/code',
   143→      '@lexical/link',
   144→      '@lexical/list',
   145→      '@lexical/markdown',
   146→      '@lexical/react',
   147→      '@lexical/rich-text',
   148→      '@lexical/table',
   149→      'react',
   150→      'react-dom',
   151→      'react/jsx-runtime',
   152→
   153→      'react-intersection-observer',
   154→      'react-photo-view',
   155→    ],
   156→  },
   157→})
   158→
```
</details></tool-use>
