```tsx
import '@haklex/rich-diff/style.css'

import { RichDiff } from '@haklex/rich-diff'
import type { RichEditorVariant } from '@haklex/rich-editor'
import { getVariantClass } from '@haklex/rich-editor'
import { ExcalidrawConfigProvider } from '@haklex/rich-renderers/excalidraw'
import { LinkCardRenderer } from '@haklex/rich-renderer-linkcard/static'
import { RichRenderer } from '@haklex/rich-static-renderer'
import { useCallback, useState } from 'react'

import { Panel } from '../components/Panel'
import { useTheme } from '../context/ThemeContext'
import { diffSamples } from '../fixtures/diff-samples'
import {
  customGithubRepoPlugin,
  extraLinkCardPlugins,
} from '../fixtures/extra-linkcard-plugins'

// ── Excalidraw Demo Data ───────────────────────────────────────

const excalidrawInlineSnapshot = JSON.stringify({
  elements: [
    {
      id: 'rect1',
      type: 'rectangle',
      x: 100,
      y: 100,
      width: 200,
      height: 120,
      strokeColor: '#1971c2',
      backgroundColor: '#a5d8ff',
      fillStyle: 'solid',
      strokeWidth: 2,
      roughness: 1,
      opacity: 100,
      seed: 1,
      version: 1,
      versionNonce: 1,
      isDeleted: false,
      boundElements: null,
      updated: 1,
      link: null,
      locked: false,
    },
    {
      id: 'ellipse1',
      type: 'ellipse',
      x: 350,
      y: 140,
      width: 160,
      height: 80,
      strokeColor: '#e8590c',
      backgroundColor: '#ffc078',
      fillStyle: 'solid',
      strokeWidth: 2,
      roughness: 1,
      opacity: 100,
      seed: 2,
      version: 1,
      versionNonce: 2,
      isDeleted: false,
      boundElements: null,
      updated: 1,
      link: null,
      locked: false,
    },
  ],
  appState: { viewBackgroundColor: '#ffffff' },
  files: {},
})

// ── Excalidraw Section ─────────────────────────────────────────

function ExcalidrawSection() {
  const theme = useTheme()
  const [blobStore] = useState<Map<string, string>>(() => new Map())

  const mockSaveSnapshot = useCallback(
    async (snapshot: object) => {
      const json = JSON.stringify(snapshot)
      const blob = new Blob([json], { type: 'application/json' })
      const url = URL.createObjectURL(blob)
      blobStore.set(url, json)
      return url
    },
    [blobStore],
  )

  const [remoteSnapshot, setRemoteSnapshot] = useState(() => {
    const blob = new Blob([excalidrawInlineSnapshot], {
      type: 'application/json',
    })
    return URL.createObjectURL(blob)
  })

  const [deltaSnapshot, setDeltaSnapshot] = useState(() => {
    const blob = new Blob([excalidrawInlineSnapshot], {
      type: 'application/json',
    })
    return URL.createObjectURL(blob)
  })

  // Lazy load the edit renderer
  const [ExcalidrawEditRenderer, setEditRenderer] = useState<any>(null)
  const [loaded, setLoaded] = useState(false)

  if (!loaded) {
    import('@haklex/rich-ext-excalidraw').then((mod) => {
      setEditRenderer(() => mod.ExcalidrawEditRenderer)
      setLoaded(true)
    })
  }

  if (!ExcalidrawEditRenderer) {
    return (
      <section className="showcase-section">
        <h3 className="showcase-section-title">Excalidraw Storage Modes</h3>
        <p style={{ color: 'var(--demo-text-muted)', fontSize: 14 }}>
          Loading excalidraw editor...
        </p>
      </section>
    )
  }

  return (
    <ExcalidrawConfigProvider saveSnapshot={mockSaveSnapshot}>
      <section className="showcase-section">
        <h3 className="showcase-section-title">Excalidraw Storage Modes</h3>
        <p
          style={{
            margin: '0 0 16px',
            color: 'var(--demo-text-muted)',
            fontSize: 14,
          }}
        >
          Three storage strategies: <strong>Remote</strong> (full snapshot
          uploaded as blob URL) and <strong>Delta</strong> (base ref + JSON diff
          patch). Toggle storage mode in the editor header.
        </p>
        <div className="biz-grid">
          <Panel title="Remote Mode" badge="excalidraw">
            <p className="node-description">
              Snapshot uploaded to a blob URL. The editor stores the URL as the
              snapshot value. On load, fetches the URL and renders.
            </p>
            <div
              style={{ height: 320, position: 'relative' }}
              data-theme={theme}
              data-color-scheme={theme}
            >
              <ExcalidrawEditRenderer
                key={`remote-${remoteSnapshot}`}
                snapshot={remoteSnapshot}
                onSnapshotChange={setRemoteSnapshot}
              />
            </div>
          </Panel>

          <Panel title="Delta Mode" badge="excalidraw">
            <p className="node-description">
              Base snapshot uploaded once. Subsequent saves store only the JSON
              diff (jsondiffpatch delta) against the base, drastically reducing
              payload size.
            </p>
            <div
              style={{ height: 320, position: 'relative' }}
              data-theme={theme}
              data-color-scheme={theme}
            >
              <ExcalidrawEditRenderer
                key={`delta-${deltaSnapshot}`}
                snapshot={deltaSnapshot}
                onSnapshotChange={setDeltaSnapshot}
              />
            </div>
          </Panel>
        </div>
      </section>
    </ExcalidrawConfigProvider>
  )
}

// ── LinkCard Section ───────────────────────────────────────────

interface DemoCard {
  key: string
  label: string
  url: string
  description: string
  title?: string
  useOverride?: boolean
}

const demoCards: DemoCard[] = [
  {
    key: 'douban',
    label: 'Douban Book (custom plugin)',
    url: 'https://book.douban.com/subject/1477390',
    description:
      'doubanBookPlugin 匹配 book.douban.com，与内置 11 个插件合并后生效',
  },
  {
    key: 'issue',
    label: 'Internal Issue (custom plugin)',
    url: 'https://issues.example.com/PROJ-1234',
    description:
      'internalIssuePlugin 匹配 issues.example.com，新增插件不影响内置',
  },
  {
    key: 'github',
    label: 'GitHub Repo (builtin)',
    url: 'https://github.com/facebook/react',
    description: '内置 gh-repo 插件自动匹配，无需额外配置',
  },
  {
    key: 'github-override',
    label: 'GitHub Repo (overridden)',
    url: 'https://github.com/vercel/next.js',
    description:
      '传入同名 "gh-repo" 插件覆盖内置实现，卡片标题带 (custom plugin) 标记',
    useOverride: true,
  },
  {
    key: 'static',
    label: 'Static fallback (no plugin match)',
    url: 'https://example.com/article',
    title: 'Example Article',
    description: '无插件匹配时降级为静态 props 渲染',
  },
]

function LinkCardSection() {
  const theme = useTheme()
  const [showOverride, setShowOverride] = useState(false)

  return (
    <section className="showcase-section">
      <h3 className="showcase-section-title">LinkCard Plugin Extension</h3>
      <p style={{ margin: '0 0 16px', color: 'var(--demo-text-muted)', fontSize: 14 }}>
        <code>plugins</code> prop 传入的插件会与内置 11
        个插件合并（同名覆盖，按 priority 排序）。
      </p>

      <div className="toolbar" style={{ marginBottom: 16 }}>
        <div className="toolbar-group">
          <span className="toolbar-label">Override</span>
          <button
            className={showOverride ? 'btn btn-active' : 'btn'}
            onClick={() => setShowOverride((v) => !v)}
          >
            Override gh-repo
          </button>
        </div>
      </div>

      <div className="biz-grid">
        {demoCards
          .filter((c) => !c.useOverride || showOverride)
          .map((card) => (
            <Panel key={card.key} title={card.label} badge="linkcard">
              <p className="node-description">{card.description}</p>
              <div
                className={`node-render ${getVariantClass('article')}`}
                data-theme={theme}
                style={{ marginTop: 8 }}
              >
                <LinkCardRenderer
                  url={card.url}
                  title={card.title}
                  plugins={
                    card.useOverride
                      ? [...extraLinkCardPlugins, customGithubRepoPlugin]
                      : extraLinkCardPlugins
                  }
                />
              </div>
            </Panel>
          ))}
      </div>
    </section>
  )
}

// ── Diff Section ───────────────────────────────────────────────

function DiffSection() {
  const theme = useTheme()
  const [variant, setVariant] = useState<RichEditorVariant>('comment')
  const [selectedKey, setSelectedKey] = useState(diffSamples[0].key)

  const selected =
    diffSamples.find((s) => s.key === selectedKey) || diffSamples[0]

  return (
    <section className="showcase-section">
      <h3 className="showcase-section-title">Rich Diff Viewer</h3>

      <div className="toolbar" style={{ marginBottom: 16 }}>
        <div className="toolbar-group">
          <span className="toolbar-label">Sample</span>
          {diffSamples.map((sample) => (
            <button
              key={sample.key}
              className={selectedKey === sample.key ? 'btn btn-active' : 'btn'}
              onClick={() => setSelectedKey(sample.key)}
              title={sample.description}
            >
              {sample.label}
            </button>
          ))}
        </div>
        <div className="toolbar-group">
          <span className="toolbar-label">Variant</span>
          <button
            className={variant === 'article' ? 'btn btn-active' : 'btn'}
            onClick={() => setVariant('article')}
          >
            Article
          </button>
          <button
            className={variant === 'comment' ? 'btn btn-active' : 'btn'}
            onClick={() => setVariant('comment')}
          >
            Comment
          </button>
        </div>
      </div>

      <Panel
        title="Side-by-Side Diff"
        badge={`${selected.label} · ${variant} · ${theme}`}
      >
        <RichDiff
          key={selected.key}
          oldValue={selected.oldValue}
          newValue={selected.newValue}
          variant={variant}
          theme={theme}
          className={theme === 'dark' ? 'rich-diff-dark' : ''}
        />
      </Panel>

      <div className="biz-grid" style={{ marginTop: 16 }}>
        <Panel title="Old">
          <RichRenderer
            key={`${selected.key}-old`}
            value={selected.oldValue}
            variant={variant}
            theme={theme}
          />
        </Panel>
        <Panel title="New">
          <RichRenderer
            key={`${selected.key}-new`}
            value={selected.newValue}
            variant={variant}
            theme={theme}
          />
        </Panel>
      </div>
    </section>
  )
}

// ── BizPage ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

export function BizPage() {
  return (
    <div className="page">
      <div className="showcase-intro">
        <h2>Business Scenarios</h2>
        <p>
          Integration demos: Excalidraw storage modes, LinkCard plugin
          extension, and Rich Diff viewer.
        </p>
      </div>

      <ExcalidrawSection />
      <LinkCardSection />
      <DiffSection />
    </div>
  )
}

```

Before providing your final summary, wrap your analysis in <analysis> tags to organize your thoughts and ensure you've covered all necessary points. In your analysis process:

Your summary should include the following sections:

1. Primary Request and Intent: Capture all of the user's explicit requests and intents in detail
2. Key Technical Concepts: List all important technical concepts, technologies, and frameworks discussed.
3. Files and Code Sections: Enumerate specific files and code sections examined, modified, or created. Pay special attention to the most recent messages and include full code snippets where applicable and include a summary of why this file read or edit is important.
4. Errors and fixes: List all errors that you ran into, and how you fixed them. Pay special attention to specific user feedback that you received, especially if the user told you to do something differently.
5. Problem Solving: Document problems solved and any ongoing troubleshooting efforts.
6. All user messages: List ALL user messages that are not tool results. These are critical for understanding the users' feedback and changing intent.
7. Pending Tasks: Outline any pending tasks that you have explicitly been asked to work on.

9. Optional Next Step: List the next step that you will take that is related to the most recent work you were doing. IMPORTANT: ensure that this step is DIRECTLY in line with the user's most recent explicit requests, and the task you were working on immediately before this summary request. If your last task was concluded, then only list next steps if they are explicitly in line with the users request. Do not start on tangential requests or really old requests that were already completed without confirming with the user first.

Let me chronologically analyze the conversation:

1. **Session Start (continued from previous)**: This session continues work on replacing tldraw with excalidraw across a Lexical rich editor monorepo. The previous session had completed most of the migration.

2. **First issue - Excalidraw 0.17.0 loading failure**: 
   - The user reported "Failed to load excalidraw" and specified version must be 0.17.0 (0.18 not usable)
   - Investigation found excalidraw 0.17.0 is a CJS/UMD webpack bundle (`main.js` → `module.exports`)
   - The demo's vite.config.ts didn't include `@excalidraw/excalidraw` in `optimizeDeps.include`
   - Added `@excalidraw/excalidraw` to `optimizeDeps.include` and `resolve.dedupe`
   - Added `mod.default?.Excalidraw` fallback for CJS interop in dynamic imports
   - User then reported actual error: `process is not defined` in main.js
   - Root cause: main.js checks `process.env.IS_PREACT` and `process.env.NODE_ENV`, but Vite 5+ no longer defines `process.env.NODE_ENV`
   - Fix: Added `define` in vite.config.ts for both `process.env.IS_PREACT` and `process.env.NODE_ENV`

3. **Readonly toolbar hiding**:
   - User showed screenshot of excalidraw's default hamburger menu visible in readonly mode
   - Added `UIOptions` prop with `readonlyUIOptions` constant to all `viewModeEnabled` instances
   - User also added CSS globalStyle rules in styles.css.ts to hide `.App-toolbar` and `.dropdown-menu-button`
   - Created `constants.ts` file (by user) exporting `readonlyUIOptions`

4. **Save not working issue**:
   - User reported: "在 edit excalidraw 之后无法保存，每次退出都会提示 Unsaved Changes modal"
   - Root causes identified:
     a. `onChange` fires on mount, immediately marking dirty
     b. `getSnapshot()` included full `appState` with transient data (cursor, scroll, zoom)
     c. Inline save error was silently swallowed
   - Fixes:
     a. Added `initializedRef` to skip first onChange (mount initialization)
     b. Stripped `getSnapshot()` appState to only persist `viewBackgroundColor` and `gridSize`
     c. Added `console.error` for inline save failures
     d. `handleChange` now receives `elements` parameter and checks `elements.length === 0`

5. **Preview not updating after save**:
   - User reported: "保存了之后 ExcalidrawEditRenderer 不更新呀"
   - Root cause: excalidraw's `initialData` only reads once on mount; preview instance already mounted with old data
   - Fix: Added `previewKey` state + `key={previewKey}` on preview component, `setPreviewKey(k => k + 1)` in `onClose`
   - But this still didn't work ("还是不行")
   - Deeper root cause: `onClose` was only called from `handleSaveAndClose`, NOT from `attemptClose` (non-dirty close) or ESC
   - Fix: Updated `attemptClose` to call `onClose(getSnapshot())` before `dismiss()`
   - ESC handler now delegates to `attemptClose()` instead of inline logic
   - Had to fix declaration order: `attemptClose` was used before definition in ESC useEffect

6. **BizPage creation**:
   - User requested: "remote 和 delta 两个 demo 的场景增加到 demo 中。新开一个页面承载。把现在的 demo 中的 LinkCard Diff 的都从 header 中移除，和这次的 demo 放到一起，属于业务场景。在 header 增加入口 Biz。Biz使用双列布局"

     - Uses `biz-grid` CSS class for two-column layout

     - Removed unused `useCallback` import and `ExcalidrawConfigProvider` import
   - Still in progress - need to add `.biz-grid` CSS class to demo.css

<summary>
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user is continuing a tldraw → excalidraw migration across a Lexical rich editor monorepo. In this session, the specific requests were:
   - Fix excalidraw 0.17.0 loading failure (version must be 0.17.0, 0.18 not usable)
   - Hide excalidraw's default toolbar/hamburger menu in readonly mode
   - Fix save not working (Unsaved Changes modal appearing every time, save not persisting)
   - Fix preview not updating after editing and saving
   - Create a new "Biz" page in the demo app combining: Excalidraw remote/delta storage demos, LinkCard plugin extension, and Diff viewer. Remove Diff and LinkCard from the header nav, add a "Biz" entry. Use two-column layout.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Excalidraw 0.17.0 is a CJS/UMD webpack bundle (`main.js` → `module.exports`), unlike 0.18.0 which is ESM
   - Vite 5+ no longer auto-defines `process.env.NODE_ENV` — must use `define` config
   - `optimizeDeps.include` for Vite pre-bundling of CJS deps used in dynamic imports
   - Excalidraw `onChange` fires on mount (initial render), not just user interaction
   - Excalidraw `initialData` only reads once on mount — need key-based remount to update
   - `UIOptions` prop controls visibility of excalidraw UI elements (canvasActions)
   - Three storage modes: inline (full JSON), remote (blob URL), delta (base ref + jsondiffpatch diff)
   - Hash-based routing in the demo app (no router library, `Page` type union + `window.location.hash`)

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **`haklex/rich-editor-demo/vite.config.ts`** — Vite config for the demo app
     - Added `@excalidraw/excalidraw` to `optimizeDeps.include` and `resolve.dedupe`
     - Added `define` to replace `process.env` references in excalidraw's CJS bundle
     ```typescript
     define: {
       'process.env.IS_PREACT': '"false"',
       'process.env.NODE_ENV': JSON.stringify('development'),
     },
     optimizeDeps: {
       include: ['react-intersection-observer', 'react-photo-view', '@excalidraw/excalidraw'],
     },
     ```

   - **`haklex/rich-ext-excalidraw/src/ExcalidrawDisplayRenderer.tsx`** — Static display renderer
     - Added CJS interop fallback for dynamic import (user later simplified to just `mod.Excalidraw` with console.log debug)
     - Added `readonlyUIOptions` constant and `UIOptions={readonlyUIOptions}` to all viewModeEnabled instances
     - User modified this file: changed import to use `readonlyUIOptions` from `./constants`, added console.log debug, changed error handler

   - **`haklex/rich-ext-excalidraw/src/ExcalidrawEditRenderer.tsx`** — Edit renderer (most complex changes)
     - User modified types: `ComponentType<ExcalidrawProps>` for ExcalidrawComponent, imports from `@excalidraw/excalidraw/types/types`
     - User added `readonlyUIOptions` import from `./constants` and `zenModeEnabled` to preview
     - Added `initializedRef` to skip mount onChange:
     ```typescript
     const initializedRef = useRef(false)
     const handleChange = useCallback(
       (elements: readonly any[]) => {
         if (!initializedRef.current) {
           initializedRef.current = true
           return
         }
         if (elements.length === 0) return
         if (!isDirtyRef.current) {
           isDirtyRef.current = true
           setIsDirty(true)
         }
         if (saveTimerRef.current) clearTimeout(saveTimerRef.current)
         saveTimerRef.current = setTimeout(() => performSave(), SAVE_DEBOUNCE_MS)
       },
       [performSave],
     )
     ```
     - Stripped `getSnapshot()` to only persist meaningful appState:
     ```typescript
     const getSnapshot = useCallback(() => {
       const api = apiRef.current
       if (!api) return undefined
       const appState = api.getAppState()
       return {
         elements: api.getSceneElements(),
         appState: {
           viewBackgroundColor: appState.viewBackgroundColor,
           gridSize: appState.gridSize,
         },
         files: api.getFiles(),
       }
     }, [])
     ```
     - Added `previewKey` state for forced remount:
     ```typescript
     const [previewKey, setPreviewKey] = useState(0)
     // In preview: key={previewKey}
     ```
     - Fixed `attemptClose` to call `onClose(getSnapshot())` before `dismiss()`:
     ```typescript
     const attemptClose = useCallback(() => {
       if (isDirtyRef.current) {
         showConfirmDialog()
       } else {
         onClose(getSnapshot())
         dismiss()
       }
     }, [dismiss, showConfirmDialog, getSnapshot, onClose])
     ```
     - ESC handler now delegates to `attemptClose()`:
     ```typescript
     useEffect(() => {
       const handleKeyDown = (e: KeyboardEvent) => {
         if (e.key === 'Escape') {
           e.preventDefault()
           e.stopImmediatePropagation()
           attemptClose()
         }
       }
       document.addEventListener('keydown', handleKeyDown, true)
       return () => document.removeEventListener('keydown', handleKeyDown, true)
     }, [attemptClose])
     ```
     - Updated `onClose` in `handleOpenEditor` to bump previewKey:
     ```typescript
     onClose={(doc) => {
       if (doc) {
         initialDataRef.current = doc
         setPreviewKey((k) => k + 1)
       }
     }}
     ```

   - **`haklex/rich-editor-demo/src/pages/BizPage.tsx`** — NEW file, business scenarios page
     - Three sections: ExcalidrawSection (remote + delta demos), LinkCardSection (from LinkCardPluginsPage), DiffSection (from DiffPage)
     - ExcalidrawSection uses local `ExcalidrawConfigProvider` with mock `saveSnapshot` (blob URL)
     - Lazy loads `ExcalidrawEditRenderer` via dynamic import from `@haklex/rich-ext-excalidraw`
     - Uses `biz-grid` CSS class for two-column layout
     - Remote demo: creates blob URL from inline snapshot, passes as `snapshot` prop
     - Delta demo: same approach, editor will detect storage mode from snapshot format

   - **`haklex/rich-editor-demo/src/App.tsx`** — Main app with routing
     - Changed `Page` type: removed `'diff' | 'linkcard'`, added `'biz'`
     - Removed `DiffPage` and `LinkCardPluginsPage` imports, added `BizPage` import
     - Replaced Diff/LinkCard nav buttons with single Biz button
     - Removed top-level `ExcalidrawConfigProvider`, `mockSaveSnapshot`, `useCallback` import
     - Updated hash validation and render conditions

   - **`haklex/rich-ext-excalidraw/src/styles.css.ts`** — User modified to add CSS hide rules:
     ```typescript
     globalStyle(`${excalidrawStaticContainer} .App-toolbar`, { display: 'none' })
     globalStyle(`.excalidraw--view-mode .dropdown-menu-button`, { display: 'none !important' })
     ```

   - **`haklex/rich-ext-excalidraw/src/constants.ts`** — User created, exports `readonlyUIOptions`

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **`process is not defined` at main.js:1:1**: Excalidraw 0.17.0's `main.js` checks `process.env.IS_PREACT` and `process.env.NODE_ENV`. Vite 5+ no longer defines these. Fixed with `define` in vite.config.ts.
   - **"Failed to load excalidraw"**: Dynamic import of CJS module might have `Excalidraw` on `mod.default?.Excalidraw`. Added fallback (user later simplified to just `mod.Excalidraw`).
   - **Unsaved Changes modal always appearing**: `onChange` fires on mount → marks dirty immediately. Fixed with `initializedRef` flag to skip first onChange. Also stripped transient `appState` from `getSnapshot()`.
   - **Preview not updating after save**: `initialData` only reads once. Fixed with `previewKey` state + `key={previewKey}` remount.
   - **Preview STILL not updating** ("还是不行"): `onClose` was only called in `handleSaveAndClose`, not in normal close path (`attemptClose` when not dirty). Fixed `attemptClose` to call `onClose(getSnapshot())` before `dismiss()`.
   - **`attemptClose` used before declaration**: ESC `useEffect` referenced `attemptClose` but was defined before it. Fixed by swapping the order (moved `attemptClose` definition before the useEffect).
   - **JSX closing tag mismatch in App.tsx**: When removing `ExcalidrawConfigProvider` wrapper, the closing tag was left mismatched. Fixed by removing the extra closing tag.

5. Problem Solving:
   - Excalidraw 0.17.0 CJS/UMD compatibility with Vite — solved with `define` + `optimizeDeps.include`
   - Excalidraw onChange mount behavior — solved with initialization guard
   - Preview update lifecycle — solved with key-based remount + ensuring all close paths call `onClose`
   - Demo restructure — in progress, creating BizPage with three sections

6. All user messages:
   - "readonly 下这个 excalidraw 默认的 toolbar 可以隐藏" (with screenshot showing hamburger menu in readonly mode)
   - "在 edit excalidraw 之后无法保存，每次退出都会提示 Unsaved Changes modal，然后退出之后 excalidraw 也没更新，再进去又会提示"
   - "还是不行" (preview still not updating after save fix)
   - "remote 和 delta 则两个 demo 的场景增加到 demo 中。新开一个页面承载。把现在的 demo 中的 LinkCard Diff 的都从 header 中移除，和 这次的 demo 放到一些， 属于业务场景。在 header 增加入口 Biz。Biz使用双列布局"

7. Pending Tasks:
   - Add `.biz-grid` CSS class to `haklex/rich-editor-demo/src/demo.css` for two-column layout
   - Optionally delete old `DiffPage.tsx` and `LinkCardPluginsPage.tsx` files (no longer imported)
   - Verify BizPage builds and works (the ExcalidrawSection lazy-loads the edit renderer)
   - The demo's `package.json` may need `@haklex/rich-ext-excalidraw` as a dependency if not already present

8. Current Work:
   Creating the BizPage and restructuring the demo app navigation. The App.tsx changes are complete:
   - Page type updated to include `'biz'` instead of `'diff' | 'linkcard'`
   - Nav buttons updated, render conditions updated
   - Top-level `ExcalidrawConfigProvider` removed (now inside BizPage)
   
   BizPage.tsx has been created with all three sections. The file uses a `biz-grid` CSS class that does NOT yet exist in `demo.css`. This CSS class needs to be added for the two-column layout to work.

9. Optional Next Step:
   Add the `.biz-grid` CSS class to `haklex/rich-editor-demo/src/demo.css` for the two-column layout. The user explicitly requested "Biz使用双列布局". The BizPage.tsx already references `className="biz-grid"` in multiple places but the CSS class doesn't exist yet. Something like:
   ```css
   .biz-grid {
     display: grid;
     grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr;
     gap: 16px;
   }
   ```
   Then verify the build works and optionally clean up the now-unused `DiffPage.tsx` and `LinkCardPluginsPage.tsx`.

     ```typescript
         onClose(getSnapshot())
         dismiss()
       }
     }, [dismiss, showConfirmDialog, getSnapshot, onClose])
     ```
     - ESC handler now delegates to `attemptClose()`:
     ```typescript
     useEffect(() => {
       const handleKeyDown = (e: KeyboardEvent) => {
         if (e.key === 'Escape') {
           e.preventDefault()
           e.stopImmediatePropagation()
           attemptClose()
         }
       }
       document.addEventListener('keydown', handleKeyDown, true)
       return () => document.removeEventListener('keydown', handleKeyDown, true)
     }, [attemptClose])
     ```
     - Updated `onClose` in `handleOpenEditor` to bump previewKey:
     ```typescript
     onClose={(doc) => {
       if (doc) {
         initialDataRef.current = doc
         setPreviewKey((k) => k + 1)
       }
     }}
     ```

   - **`haklex/rich-editor-demo/src/pages/BizPage.tsx`** — NEW file, business scenarios page
     - Three sections: ExcalidrawSection (remote + delta demos), LinkCardSection (from LinkCardPluginsPage), DiffSection (from DiffPage)
     - ExcalidrawSection uses local `ExcalidrawConfigProvider` with mock `saveSnapshot` (blob URL)
     - Lazy loads `ExcalidrawEditRenderer` via dynamic import from `@haklex/rich-ext-excalidraw`
     - Uses `biz-grid` CSS class for two-column layout
     - Remote demo: creates blob URL from inline snapshot, passes as `snapshot` prop
     - Delta demo: same approach, editor will detect storage mode from snapshot format

   - **`haklex/rich-editor-demo/src/App.tsx`** — Main app with routing
     - Changed `Page` type: removed `'diff' | 'linkcard'`, added `'biz'`
     - Removed `DiffPage` and `LinkCardPluginsPage` imports, added `BizPage` import
     - Replaced Diff/LinkCard nav buttons with single Biz button
     - Removed top-level `ExcalidrawConfigProvider`, `mockSaveSnapshot`, `useCallback` import
     - Updated hash validation and render conditions

   - **`haklex/rich-ext-excalidraw/src/styles.css.ts`** — User modified to add CSS hide rules:
     ```typescript
     globalStyle(`${excalidrawStaticContainer} .App-toolbar`, { display: 'none' })
     globalStyle(`.excalidraw--view-mode .dropdown-menu-button`, { display: 'none !important' })
     ```

   - **`haklex/rich-ext-excalidraw/src/constants.ts`** — User created, exports `readonlyUIOptions`

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **`process is not defined` at main.js:1:1**: Excalidraw 0.17.0's `main.js` checks `process.env.IS_PREACT` and `process.env.NODE_ENV`. Vite 5+ no longer defines these. Fixed with `define` in vite.config.ts.
   - **"Failed to load excalidraw"**: Dynamic import of CJS module might have `Excalidraw` on `mod.default?.Excalidraw`. Added fallback (user later simplified to just `mod.Excalidraw`).
   - **Unsaved Changes modal always appearing**: `onChange` fires on mount → marks dirty immediately. Fixed with `initializedRef` flag to skip first onChange. Also stripped transient `appState` from `getSnapshot()`.
   - **Preview not updating after save**: `initialData` only reads once. Fixed with `previewKey` state + `key={previewKey}` remount.
   - **Preview STILL not updating** ("还是不行"): `onClose` was only called in `handleSaveAndClose`, not in normal close path (`attemptClose` when not dirty). Fixed `attemptClose` to call `onClose(getSnapshot())` before `dismiss()`.
   - **`attemptClose` used before declaration**: ESC `useEffect` referenced `attemptClose` but was defined before it. Fixed by swapping the order (moved `attemptClose` definition before the useEffect).
   - **JSX closing tag mismatch in App.tsx**: When removing `ExcalidrawConfigProvider` wrapper, the closing tag was left mismatched. Fixed by removing the extra closing tag.

5. Problem Solving:
   - Excalidraw 0.17.0 CJS/UMD compatibility with Vite — solved with `define` + `optimizeDeps.include`
   - Excalidraw onChange mount behavior — solved with initialization guard
   - Preview update lifecycle — solved with key-based remount + ensuring all close paths call `onClose`
   - Demo restructure — in progress, creating BizPage with three sections

6. All user messages:
   - "readonly 下这个 excalidraw 默认的 toolbar 可以隐藏" (with screenshot showing hamburger menu in readonly mode)
   - "在 edit excalidraw 之后无法保存，每次退出都会提示 Unsaved Changes modal，然后退出之后 excalidraw 也没更新，再进去又会提示"
   - "还是不行" (preview still not updating after save fix)
   - "remote 和 delta 则两个 demo 的场景增加到 demo 中。新开一个页面承载。把现在的 demo 中的 LinkCard Diff 的都从 header 中移除，和 这次的 demo 放到一些， 属于业务场景。在 header 增加入口 Biz。Biz使用双列布局"

7. Pending Tasks:
   - Add `.biz-grid` CSS class to `haklex/rich-editor-demo/src/demo.css` for two-column layout
   - Optionally delete old `DiffPage.tsx` and `LinkCardPluginsPage.tsx` files (no longer imported)
   - Verify BizPage builds and works (the ExcalidrawSection lazy-loads the edit renderer)
   - The demo's `package.json` may need `@haklex/rich-ext-excalidraw` as a dependency if not already present

8. Current Work:
   Creating the BizPage and restructuring the demo app navigation. The App.tsx changes are complete:
   - Page type updated to include `'biz'` instead of `'diff' | 'linkcard'`
   - Nav buttons updated, render conditions updated
   - Top-level `ExcalidrawConfigProvider` removed (now inside BizPage)
   
   BizPage.tsx has been created with all three sections. The file uses a `biz-grid` CSS class that does NOT yet exist in `demo.css`. This CSS class needs to be added for the two-column layout to work.

9. Optional Next Step:
   Add the `.biz-grid` CSS class to `haklex/rich-editor-demo/src/demo.css` for the two-column layout. The user explicitly requested "Biz使用双列布局". The BizPage.tsx already references `className="biz-grid"` in multiple places but the CSS class doesn't exist yet. Something like:
   ```css

     display: grid;
   ```

```text
   506→  box-sizing: border-box;

   508→  font-family: 'SF Mono', SFMono-Regular, ui-monospace, monospace;
   509→  font-size: 12px;
   510→  line-height: 1.5;
   511→  border: 1px solid var(--demo-border);
   512→  border-radius: 6px;
   513→  resize: vertical;
   514→  background: var(--demo-surface-alt);
   515→  color: var(--demo-text);
   516→}
   517→
   518→.import-textarea:focus {
   519→  outline: none;
   520→  border-color: var(--demo-accent);
   521→  box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px var(--demo-accent-bg);
   522→}
   523→
   524→/* ── Comments Page ── */
   525→.comments-layout {
   526→  display: flex;
   527→  gap: 24px;
   528→  align-items: flex-start;
   529→}
   530→
   531→.comments-editor {
   532→  flex: 1;
   533→  position: relative;
   534→  min-width: 0;
   535→}
   536→
   537→.comments-sidebar {
   538→  width: 320px;
   539→  flex-shrink: 0;
   540→  position: sticky;
   541→  top: 88px;
   542→}
   543→
   544→/* ── Comment Block Button ── */
   545→.comment-block-btn-wrap {
   546→  position: absolute;
   547→  right: 8px;
   548→  display: flex;
   549→  align-items: center;
   550→  gap: 4px;
   551→  z-index: 5;
   552→  animation: comment-popup-in 0.1s ease-out;
   553→}
   554→
   555→.comment-gutter-count {
   556→  display: inline-flex;
   557→  align-items: center;
   558→  gap: 2px;
   559→  font-size: 11px;
   560→  color: var(--demo-accent);
   561→  background: var(--demo-accent-bg);
   562→  padding: 2px 6px;
   563→  border-radius: 999px;
   564→  font-weight: 600;
   565→  white-space: nowrap;
   566→}
   567→
   568→.comment-gutter-btn {
   569→  display: flex;
   570→  align-items: center;
   571→  justify-content: center;
   572→  width: 24px;
   573→  height: 24px;
   574→  border: 1px solid var(--demo-border);
   575→  border-radius: 6px;
   576→  background: var(--demo-surface);
   577→  color: var(--demo-text-muted);
   578→  cursor: pointer;
   579→  transition: all 0.15s;
   580→  padding: 0;
   581→  font-size: 0;
   582→}
   583→
   584→.comment-gutter-btn:hover {
   585→  background: var(--demo-accent-bg);
   586→  border-color: var(--demo-accent);
   587→  color: var(--demo-accent);
   588→}
   589→
   590→.comment-gutter-input {
   591→  display: flex;
   592→  gap: 4px;
   593→  align-items: center;
   594→  position: absolute;
   595→  right: 0;
   596→  white-space: nowrap;
   597→}
   598→
   599→/* ── Selection Popup ── */
   600→.comment-selection-popup {
   601→  position: fixed;
   602→  z-index: 100;
   603→  transform: translateX(-50%);
   604→  animation: comment-popup-in 0.12s ease-out;
   605→}
   606→
   607→@keyframes comment-popup-in {
   608→  from {
   609→    opacity: 0;
   610→    transform: translateX(-50%) translateY(4px);
   611→  }
   612→  to {
   613→    opacity: 1;
   614→    transform: translateX(-50%) translateY(0);
   615→  }
   616→}
   617→
   618→.comment-add-btn {
   619→  display: inline-flex;
   620→  align-items: center;
   621→  gap: 4px;
   622→  padding: 6px 12px;
   623→  font-size: 13px;
   624→  font-weight: 500;
   625→  border: 1px solid var(--demo-border);
   626→  border-radius: 8px;
   627→  background: var(--demo-surface);
   628→  color: var(--demo-text-secondary);
   629→  cursor: pointer;
   630→  box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.12);
   631→  transition: all 0.15s;
   632→  font-family: inherit;
   633→}
   634→
   635→.comment-add-btn:hover {
   636→  background: var(--demo-accent-bg);
   637→  border-color: var(--demo-accent);
   638→  color: var(--demo-accent);
   639→}
   640→
   641→/* ── Comment Input ── */
   642→.comment-inline-input {
   643→  display: flex;
   644→  gap: 4px;
   645→  align-items: center;
   646→  background: var(--demo-surface);
   647→  border: 1px solid var(--demo-border);
   648→  border-radius: 8px;
   649→  padding: 4px;
   650→  box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.12);
   651→}
   652→
   653→.comment-input {
   654→  padding: 4px 8px;
   655→  font-size: 13px;
   656→  border: 1px solid var(--demo-border);
   657→  border-radius: 4px;
   658→  background: var(--demo-surface-alt);
   659→  color: var(--demo-text);
   660→  outline: none;
   661→  font-family: inherit;
   662→  min-width: 160px;
   663→}
   664→
   665→.comment-input:focus {
   666→  border-color: var(--demo-accent);
   667→  box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px var(--demo-accent-bg);
   668→}
   669→
   670→.comment-input-sm {
   671→  min-width: 120px;
   672→  font-size: 12px;
   673→}
   674→
   675→/* ── Sidebar ── */
   676→.comment-sidebar-empty {
   677→  text-align: center;
   678→  padding: 24px 16px;
   679→  color: var(--demo-text-muted);
   680→  font-size: 13px;
   681→}
   682→
   683→.comment-sidebar-empty p {
   684→  margin: 0;
   685→}
   686→
   687→.comment-sidebar-hint {
   688→  margin-top: 8px !important;
   689→  font-size: 12px;
   690→  opacity: 0.7;
   691→}
   692→
   693→.comment-sidebar-list {
   694→  display: flex;
   695→  flex-direction: column;
   696→  gap: 8px;
   697→}
   698→
   699→.comment-card {
   700→  padding: 12px;
   701→  border: 1px solid var(--demo-border);
   702→  border-radius: 8px;
   703→  transition: all 0.15s;
   704→  cursor: default;
   705→}
   706→
   707→.comment-card:hover {
   708→  border-color: var(--demo-accent);
   709→  background: var(--demo-accent-bg);
   710→}
   711→
   712→.comment-card-active {
   713→  border-color: var(--demo-accent);
   714→  background: var(--demo-accent-bg);
   715→}
   716→
   717→.comment-card-header {
   718→  display: flex;
   719→  align-items: center;
   720→  gap: 6px;
   721→  margin-bottom: 6px;
   722→}
   723→
   724→.comment-badge {
   725→  font-size: 10px;
   726→  font-weight: 600;
   727→  padding: 1px 6px;
   728→  border-radius: 999px;
   729→  text-transform: uppercase;
   730→  letter-spacing: 0.5px;
   731→}
   732→
   733→.comment-badge-range {
   734→  background: rgba(51, 166, 184, 0.15);
   735→  color: #33a6b8;
   736→}
   737→
   738→[data-theme='dark'] .comment-badge-range {
   739→  background: rgba(245, 150, 170, 0.15);
   740→  color: #f596aa;
   741→}
   742→
   743→.comment-badge-block {
   744→  background: rgba(115, 115, 115, 0.15);
   745→  color: #737373;
   746→}
   747→
   748→.comment-card-time {
   749→  font-size: 11px;
   750→  color: var(--demo-text-muted);
   751→  flex: 1;
   752→}
   753→
   754→.comment-delete-btn {
   755→  display: flex;
   756→  align-items: center;
   757→  justify-content: center;
   758→  width: 20px;
   759→  height: 20px;
   760→  border: none;
   761→  border-radius: 4px;
   762→  background: none;
   763→  color: var(--demo-text-muted);
   764→  cursor: pointer;
   765→  opacity: 0;
   766→  transition: all 0.15s;
   767→  padding: 0;
   768→}
   769→
   770→.comment-card:hover .comment-delete-btn {
   771→  opacity: 1;
   772→}
   773→
   774→.comment-delete-btn:hover {
   775→  color: #ef4444;
   776→  background: rgba(239, 68, 68, 0.1);
   777→}
   778→
   779→.comment-quote {
   780→  font-style: italic;
   781→  color: var(--demo-text-muted);
   782→  font-size: 12px;
   783→  line-height: 1.4;
   784→  padding: 4px 8px;
   785→  margin-bottom: 6px;
   786→  border-left: 2px solid var(--demo-border);
   787→  white-space: nowrap;
   788→  overflow: hidden;
   789→  text-overflow: ellipsis;
   790→}
   791→
   792→.comment-card-text {
   793→  font-size: 13px;
   794→  line-height: 1.5;
   795→  color: var(--demo-text);
   796→}
   797→
   798→/* ── CSS Highlight API ── */
   799→::highlight(comment-highlight) {
   800→  background: rgba(51, 166, 184, 0.08);
   801→  text-decoration: underline;
   802→  text-decoration-color: rgba(51, 166, 184, 0.6);
   803→  text-decoration-thickness: 2px;
   804→  text-underline-offset: 2px;
   805→}
   806→

   811→  text-decoration-thickness: 2px;
   812→  text-underline-offset: 2px;
   813→}
   814→
   815→/* ── Responsive ── */
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./haklex/rich-editor-demo/src/App.tsx`

```text
     1→import 'modern-normalize/modern-normalize.css'
     2→import '@haklex/rich-kit-shiro/style.css'
     3→import '@haklex/rich-kit-shiro/style-renderer.css'
     4→import './demo.css'
     5→
     6→import type { ColorScheme } from '@haklex/rich-editor'
     7→import { DialogStackProvider } from '@haklex/rich-editor-ui'
     8→import { Monitor, Moon, Sun } from 'lucide-react'
     9→import { useEffect, useMemo, useState } from 'react'
    10→
    11→import { ThemeContext } from './context/ThemeContext'
    12→import { BizPage } from './pages/BizPage'
    13→import { CommentsPage } from './pages/CommentsPage'
    14→import { EditorPage } from './pages/EditorPage'
    15→import { NodeShowcase } from './pages/NodeShowcase'
    16→import { PresetsPage } from './pages/PresetsPage'
    17→
    18→type Page = 'editor' | 'comments' | 'nodes' | 'presets' | 'biz'
    19→type ThemeMode = 'system' | 'light' | 'dark'
    20→
    21→function useSystemColorScheme(): ColorScheme {
    22→  const [scheme, setScheme] = useState<ColorScheme>(() =>
    23→    window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)').matches
    24→      ? 'dark'
    25→      : 'light',
    26→  )
    27→
    28→  useEffect(() => {
    29→    const mq = window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)')
    30→    const handler = (e: MediaQueryListEvent) =>
    31→      setScheme(e.matches ? 'dark' : 'light')
    32→    mq.addEventListener('change', handler)
    33→    return () => mq.removeEventListener('change', handler)
    34→  }, [])
    35→
    36→  return scheme
    37→}
    38→
    39→export function App() {
    40→  const [currentPage, setCurrentPage] = useState<Page>(() => {
    41→    const hash = window.location.hash.slice(1) as Page
    42→    return hash === 'comments' ||
    43→      hash === 'nodes' ||
    44→      hash === 'presets' ||
    45→      hash === 'biz'
    46→      ? hash
    47→      : 'editor'
    48→  })
    49→  const [themeMode, setThemeMode] = useState<ThemeMode>('system')
    50→  const systemScheme = useSystemColorScheme()
    51→
    52→  const resolved: ColorScheme =
    53→    themeMode === 'system' ? systemScheme : themeMode
    54→
    55→  const dataTheme = useMemo(
    56→    () => (themeMode === 'system' ? systemScheme : themeMode),
    57→    [systemScheme, themeMode],
    58→  )
    59→
    60→  const cycleTheme = useCallback(() => {
    61→    setThemeMode((m) => {
    62→      if (m === 'system') return 'light'
    63→      if (m === 'light') return 'dark'
    64→      return 'system'
    65→    })
    66→  }, [])
    67→
    68→  const themeLabel =
    69→    themeMode === 'system' ? 'System' : themeMode === 'light' ? 'Light' : 'Dark'
    70→  const ThemeIcon =
    71→    themeMode === 'system' ? Monitor : themeMode === 'light' ? Sun : Moon
    72→
    73→  useEffect(() => {
    74→    const handleHashChange = () => {
    75→      const hash = window.location.hash.slice(1) as Page
    76→      const validPage =
    77→        hash === 'comments' ||
    78→        hash === 'nodes' ||
    79→        hash === 'presets' ||
    80→        hash === 'biz'
    81→          ? hash
    82→          : 'editor'
    83→      setCurrentPage(validPage)
    84→    }
    85→
    86→    window.addEventListener('hashchange', handleHashChange)
    87→    return () => window.removeEventListener('hashchange', handleHashChange)
    88→  }, [])
    89→
    90→  const navigate = (page: Page) => {
    91→    window.location.hash = page
    92→  }
    93→
    94→  return (
    95→    <ThemeContext value={resolved}>
    96→      <DialogStackProvider>
    97→          <div className="app" data-theme={dataTheme}>
    98→            <header className="app-header">
    99→              <div className="app-header-content">
   100→                <div>
   101→                  <h1 className="app-title">@haklex/rich-editor</h1>
   102→                  <p className="app-subtitle">
   103→                    Lexical-based rich text editor & renderer
   104→                  </p>
   105→                </div>
   106→                <nav className="app-nav">
   107→                  <button
   108→                    className={
   109→                      currentPage === 'editor'
   110→                        ? 'nav-tab nav-tab-active'
   111→                        : 'nav-tab'
   112→                    }
   113→                    onClick={() => navigate('editor')}
   114→                  >
   115→                    Editor
   116→                  </button>
   117→                  <button
   118→                    className={
   119→                      currentPage === 'comments'
   120→                        ? 'nav-tab nav-tab-active'
   121→                        : 'nav-tab'
   122→                    }
   123→                    onClick={() => navigate('comments')}
   124→                  >
   125→                    Comments
   126→                  </button>
   127→                  <button
   128→                    className={
   129→                      currentPage === 'nodes'
   130→                        ? 'nav-tab nav-tab-active'
   131→                        : 'nav-tab'
   132→                    }
   133→                    onClick={() => navigate('nodes')}
   134→                  >
   135→                    Node Showcase
   136→                  </button>
   137→                  <button
   138→                    className={
   139→                      currentPage === 'presets'
   140→                        ? 'nav-tab nav-tab-active'
   141→                        : 'nav-tab'
   142→                    }
   143→                    onClick={() => navigate('presets')}
   144→                  >
   145→                    Presets
   146→                  </button>
   147→                  <button
   148→                    className={
   149→                      currentPage === 'biz'
   150→                        ? 'nav-tab nav-tab-active'
   151→                        : 'nav-tab'
   152→                    }
   153→                    onClick={() => navigate('biz')}
   154→                  >
   155→                    Biz
   156→                  </button>
   157→                  <div className="nav-divider" />
   158→                  <button
   159→                    className="nav-theme-toggle"
   160→                    onClick={cycleTheme}
   161→                    title={themeLabel}
   162→                  >
   163→                    <ThemeIcon size={18} />
   164→                  </button>
   165→                </nav>
   166→              </div>
   167→            </header>
   168→
   169→            <main className="app-main">
   170→              {currentPage === 'editor' && <EditorPage />}
   171→              {currentPage === 'comments' && <CommentsPage />}
   172→              {currentPage === 'nodes' && <NodeShowcase />}
   173→              {currentPage === 'presets' && <PresetsPage />}
   174→              {currentPage === 'biz' && <BizPage />}
   175→            </main>
   176→          </div>
   177→        </DialogStackProvider>
   178→    </ThemeContext>
   179→  )
   180→}
   181→
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-02 14:22:05Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./haklex/rich-ext-excalidraw/src/useExcalidrawData.ts`

```text
     1→import { useEffect, useMemo, useState } from 'react'
     2→
     3→import { useExcalidrawConfig } from './ExcalidrawConfigContext'
     4→
     5→export interface ExcalidrawDataState {
     6→  snapshot: Record<string, any> | undefined
     7→  loading: boolean
     8→  error: string
     9→  /** The base reference line (URL or ref:file/xxx) when snapshot is remote or incremental */
    10→  baseRef?: string
    11→  /** The raw base data before delta patch (for computing new diffs in edit mode) */
    12→  baseData?: Record<string, any>
    13→}
    14→
    15→type ParsedData =
    16→  | { type: 'empty' }
    17→  | { type: 'inline'; snapshot: Record<string, any> }
    18→  | { type: 'remote'; fetchUrl: string; refLine: string }
    19→  | { type: 'incremental'; fetchUrl: string; refLine: string; delta: object }
    20→  | { type: 'error'; error: string }
    21→
    22→export function useExcalidrawData(data: string): ExcalidrawDataState {
    23→  const { apiUrl } = useExcalidrawConfig()
    24→
    25→  const parsed = useMemo((): ParsedData => {
    26→    if (!data) return { type: 'empty' }
    27→
    28→    try {
    29→      const json = JSON.parse(data)
    30→      if (json && typeof json === 'object') {
    31→        return { type: 'inline', snapshot: json as Record<string, any> }
    32→      }
    33→    } catch {
    34→      // not JSON — check for URL/ref
    35→    }
    36→
    37→    const lines = data.split('\n')
    38→    const firstLine = lines[0].trim()
    39→    let fetchUrl: string | undefined
    40→    const refLine = firstLine
    41→
    42→    if (firstLine.startsWith('http')) {
    43→      fetchUrl = firstLine
    44→    } else if (firstLine.startsWith('ref:') && apiUrl) {
    45→      fetchUrl = `${apiUrl}/objects/${firstLine.slice(4)}`
    46→    }
    47→
    48→    if (!fetchUrl) {
    49→      return {
    50→        type: 'error',
    51→        error: firstLine.startsWith('ref:')
    52→          ? 'Missing apiUrl for ref resolution'
    53→          : 'Unrecognized snapshot format',
    54→      }
    55→    }
    56→
    57→    // Check for delta diff in remaining lines
    58→    const remainingLines = lines.slice(1).join('\n').trim()
    59→    if (remainingLines) {
    60→      try {
    61→        const delta = JSON.parse(remainingLines)
    62→        if (delta && typeof delta === 'object') {
    63→          return { type: 'incremental', fetchUrl, refLine, delta }
    64→        }
    65→      } catch {
    66→        // not valid JSON delta — treat as plain remote
    67→      }
    68→    }
    69→
    70→    return { type: 'remote', fetchUrl, refLine }
    71→  }, [data, apiUrl])
    72→
    73→  const [remoteSnapshot, setRemoteSnapshot] = useState<
    74→    Record<string, any> | undefined
    75→  >()
    76→  const [baseData, setBaseData] = useState<Record<string, any> | undefined>()
    77→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(
    78→    parsed.type === 'remote' || parsed.type === 'incremental',
    79→  )
    80→  const [error, setError] = useState('')
    81→
    82→  useEffect(() => {
    83→    if (parsed.type !== 'remote' && parsed.type !== 'incremental') return
    84→
    85→    const { fetchUrl } = parsed
    86→    const delta = parsed.type === 'incremental' ? parsed.delta : undefined
    87→
    88→    let cancelled = false
    89→    setLoading(true)
    90→    setError('')
    91→
    92→    fetch(fetchUrl)
    93→      .then((res) => {
    94→        if (!res.ok) throw new Error(`HTTP ${res.status}`)
    95→        return res.json()
    96→      })
    97→      .then(async (json) => {
    98→        if (cancelled) return
    99→        setBaseData(json)
   100→
   101→        if (delta) {
   102→          const { patch } = await import('jsondiffpatch')
   103→          if (cancelled) return
   104→          const patched = patch(structuredClone(json), delta as any)
   105→          setRemoteSnapshot(patched as Record<string, any>)
   106→        } else {
   107→          setRemoteSnapshot(json)
   108→        }
   109→        setLoading(false)
   110→      })
   111→      .catch((err) => {
   112→        if (!cancelled) {
   113→          setError(err instanceof Error ? err.message : 'Failed to load')
   114→          setLoading(false)
   115→        }
   116→      })
   117→
   118→    return () => {
   119→      cancelled = true
   120→    }
   121→  }, [parsed])
   122→
   123→  if (parsed.type === 'inline') {
   124→    return { snapshot: parsed.snapshot, loading: false, error: '' }
   125→  }
   126→
   127→  if (parsed.type === 'remote' || parsed.type === 'incremental') {
   128→    return {
   129→      snapshot: remoteSnapshot,
   130→      loading,
   131→      error,
   132→      baseRef: parsed.refLine,
   133→      baseData,
   134→    }
   135→  }
   136→
   137→  if (parsed.type === 'error') {
   138→    return { snapshot: undefined, loading: false, error: parsed.error }
   139→  }
   140→
   141→  return { snapshot: undefined, loading: false, error: '' }
   142→}
   143→
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-02 14:22:16Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The issue is clear. In `BizPage.tsx`, the snapshot is created as a blob URL:

```typescript
const [remoteSnapshot, setRemoteSnapshot] = useState(() => {
    const blob = new Blob([excalidrawInlineSnapshot], {
      type: 'application/json',
    })
    return URL.createObjectURL(blob)
  })
```

This creates a `blob:http://...` URL. In `useExcalidrawData`, the code checks:
- Is it JSON? No, it's a blob URL string.
- Does it start with `http`? No, it starts with `blob:`.


- Does it start with `ref:`? No.

So it falls through to "Unrecognized snapshot format".

The fix is straightforward: I should either pass the inline JSON directly instead of wrapping it in a blob URL, or update the handler to recognize `blob:` URLs. Option 1 is cleaner for a demo—just pass `excalidrawInlineSnapshot` directly since it's already valid JSON.

The `ExcalidrawEditRenderer` accepts a snapshot string and forwards it to `useExcalidrawData`, which will parse it as the inline type. I'll update BizPage to pass the JSON string directly instead of creating blob URLs.
</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-02 14:22:16Z)**_

问题在 `useExcalidrawData`：blob URL 以 `blob:` 开头，非 `http` 亦非 `ref:`，故判为 "Unrecognized"。BizPage 应直接传 JSON string 而非 blob URL。

```text
    72→// ── Excalidraw Section ─────────────────────────────────────────
    73→
    74→function ExcalidrawSection() {
    75→  const theme = useTheme()
    76→  const [blobStore] = useState<Map<string, string>>(() => new Map())
    77→
    78→  const mockSaveSnapshot = useCallback(
    79→    async (snapshot: object) => {
    80→      const json = JSON.stringify(snapshot)
    81→      const blob = new Blob([json], { type: 'application/json' })
    82→      const url = URL.createObjectURL(blob)
    83→      blobStore.set(url, json)
    84→      return url
    85→    },
    86→    [blobStore],
    87→  )
    88→
    89→  const [remoteSnapshot, setRemoteSnapshot] = useState(() => {

    96→  const [deltaSnapshot, setDeltaSnapshot] = useState(() => {

   101→  })

   132→            color: 'var(--demo-text-muted)',
   133→            fontSize: 14,
   134→          }}
   135→        >
   136→          Three storage strategies: <strong>Remote</strong> (full snapshot

   153→                snapshot={remoteSnapshot}
   154→                onSnapshotChange={setRemoteSnapshot}
   155→              />
   156→            </div>
   157→          </Panel>
   158→
   159→          <Panel title="Delta Mode" badge="excalidraw">
   160→            <p className="node-description">
   161→              Base snapshot uploaded once. Subsequent saves store only the JSON
   162→              diff (jsondiffpatch delta) against the base, drastically reducing
   163→              payload size.
   164→            </p>
   165→            <div
   166→              style={{ height: 320, position: 'relative' }}
   167→              data-theme={theme}
   168→              data-color-scheme={theme}

   174→              />

   183→// ── LinkCard Section ───────────────────────────────────────────
   184→
   185→interface DemoCard {
   186→  key: string
   187→  label: string
   188→  url: string
   189→  description: string
   190→  title?: string

   194→const demoCards: DemoCard[] = [

   196→    key: 'douban',
   197→    label: 'Douban Book (custom plugin)',
   198→    url: 'https://book.douban.com/subject/1477390',

   202→  {
   203→    key: 'issue',
   204→    label: 'Internal Issue (custom plugin)',
   205→    url: 'https://issues.example.com/PROJ-1234',
   206→    description:
   207→      'internalIssuePlugin 匹配 issues.example.com，新增插件不影响内置',
   208→  },
   209→  {
   210→    key: 'github',
   211→    label: 'GitHub Repo (builtin)',
   212→    url: 'https://github.com/facebook/react',
   213→    description: '内置 gh-repo 插件自动匹配，无需额外配置',
   214→  },
   215→  {
   216→    key: 'github-override',
   217→    label: 'GitHub Repo (overridden)',
   218→    url: 'https://github.com/vercel/next.js',
   219→    description:
   220→      '传入同名 "gh-repo" 插件覆盖内置实现，卡片标题带 (custom plugin) 标记',
   221→    useOverride: true,
   222→  },
   223→  {
   224→    key: 'static',
   225→    label: 'Static fallback (no plugin match)',
   226→    url: 'https://example.com/article',
   227→    title: 'Example Article',
   228→    description: '无插件匹配时降级为静态 props 渲染',
   229→  },
   230→]
   231→
   232→function LinkCardSection() {
   233→  const theme = useTheme()
   234→  const [showOverride, setShowOverride] = useState(false)
   235→
   236→  return (
   237→    <section className="showcase-section">
   238→      <h3 className="showcase-section-title">LinkCard Plugin Extension</h3>
   239→      <p style={{ margin: '0 0 16px', color: 'var(--demo-text-muted)', fontSize: 14 }}>
   240→        <code>plugins</code> prop 传入的插件会与内置 11
   241→        个插件合并（同名覆盖，按 priority 排序）。
   242→      </p>
   243→
   244→      <div className="toolbar" style={{ marginBottom: 16 }}>
   245→        <div className="toolbar-group">
   246→          <span className="toolbar-label">Override</span>
   247→          <button
   248→            className={showOverride ? 'btn btn-active' : 'btn'}
   249→            onClick={() => setShowOverride((v) => !v)}
   250→          >
   251→            Override gh-repo
   252→          </button>
   253→        </div>
   254→      </div>
   255→
   256→      <div className="biz-grid">
   257→        {demoCards
   258→          .filter((c) => !c.useOverride || showOverride)
   259→          .map((card) => (
   260→            <Panel key={card.key} title={card.label} badge="linkcard">
   261→              <p className="node-description">{card.description}</p>
   262→              <div
   263→                className={`node-render ${getVariantClass('article')}`}
   264→                data-theme={theme}
   265→                style={{ marginTop: 8 }}
   266→              >
   267→                <LinkCardRenderer
   268→                  url={card.url}
   269→                  title={card.title}
   270→                  plugins={
   271→                    card.useOverride
   272→                      ? [...extraLinkCardPlugins, customGithubRepoPlugin]
   273→                      : extraLinkCardPlugins
   274→                  }
   275→                />
   276→              </div>
   277→            </Panel>
   278→          ))}
   279→      </div>
   280→    </section>
   281→  )
   282→}
   283→
   284→// ── Diff Section ───────────────────────────────────────────────
   285→
   286→function DiffSection() {
   287→  const theme = useTheme()
   288→  const [variant, setVariant] = useState<RichEditorVariant>('comment')
   289→  const [selectedKey, setSelectedKey] = useState(diffSamples[0].key)
   290→
   291→  const selected =
   292→    diffSamples.find((s) => s.key === selectedKey) || diffSamples[0]
   293→
   294→  return (
   295→    <section className="showcase-section">
   296→      <h3 className="showcase-section-title">Rich Diff Viewer</h3>
   297→
   298→      <div className="toolbar" style={{ marginBottom: 16 }}>
   299→        <div className="toolbar-group">
   300→          <span className="toolbar-label">Sample</span>
   301→          {diffSamples.map((sample) => (
   302→            <button
   303→              key={sample.key}
   304→              className={selectedKey === sample.key ? 'btn btn-active' : 'btn'}
   305→              onClick={() => setSelectedKey(sample.key)}
   306→              title={sample.description}
   307→            >
   308→              {sample.label}
   309→            </button>
   310→          ))}
   311→        </div>
   312→        <div className="toolbar-group">
   313→          <span className="toolbar-label">Variant</span>
   314→          <button
   315→            className={variant === 'article' ? 'btn btn-active' : 'btn'}
   316→            onClick={() => setVariant('article')}
   317→          >
   318→            Article
   319→          </button>
   320→          <button
   321→            className={variant === 'comment' ? 'btn btn-active' : 'btn'}
   322→            onClick={() => setVariant('comment')}
   323→          >
   324→            Comment
   325→          </button>
   326→        </div>
   327→      </div>
   328→
   329→      <Panel
   330→        title="Side-by-Side Diff"
   331→        badge={`${selected.label} · ${variant} · ${theme}`}
   332→      >
   333→        <RichDiff
   334→          key={selected.key}
   335→          oldValue={selected.oldValue}
   336→          newValue={selected.newValue}
   337→          variant={variant}
   338→          theme={theme}
   339→          className={theme === 'dark' ? 'rich-diff-dark' : ''}
   340→        />
   341→      </Panel>
   342→
   343→      <div className="biz-grid" style={{ marginTop: 16 }}>
   344→        <Panel title="Old">
   345→          <RichRenderer
   346→            key={`${selected.key}-old`}
   347→            value={selected.oldValue}
   348→            variant={variant}
   349→            theme={theme}
   350→          />
   351→        </Panel>
   352→        <Panel title="New">
   353→          <RichRenderer
   354→            key={`${selected.key}-new`}
   355→            value={selected.newValue}
   356→            variant={variant}
   357→            theme={theme}
   358→          />
   359→        </Panel>
   360→      </div>
   361→    </section>
   362→  )
   363→}
   364→
   365→// ── BizPage ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   366→
   367→export function BizPage() {
   368→  return (
   369→    <div className="page">
   370→      <div className="showcase-intro">
   371→        <h2>Business Scenarios</h2>
   372→        <p>
   373→          Integration demos: Excalidraw storage modes, LinkCard plugin
   374→          extension, and Rich Diff viewer.
   375→        </p>
   376→      </div>
   377→
   378→      <ExcalidrawSection />
   379→      <LinkCardSection />
   380→      <DiffSection />
   381→    </div>
   382→  )
   383→}
   384→
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-02 14:22:30Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Two issues:
1. The snapshot is a blob URL (`blob:http://...`) which `useExcalidrawData` doesn't recognize. The initial state should just be the inline JSON string directly, since `useExcalidrawData` handles `inline` type (JSON parse succeeds).
2. Need to add anchor navigation at the top of BizPage.

For the snapshot fix: instead of creating blob URLs for initial state, just pass `excalidrawInlineSnapshot` directly. The `useExcalidrawData` will parse it as inline JSON. When `mockSaveSnapshot` returns a blob URL, the `onSnapshotChange` will update the state to that blob URL. 

I realize `useExcalidrawData` also needs to handle `blob:` URLs, so I'll add blob URL recognition to the fetch logic alongside HTTP URLs. This way the initial load works with inline JSON, and after saving in remote mode when a blob URL is returned, the hook can properly fetch that data.
</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Two problems:

1. **Storage mode defaults to 'inline'**: The initial storage mode detection logic checks: if `JSON.parse(initialSnapshot)` succeeds → 'inline'. Since BizPage now passes `excalidrawInlineSnapshot` (a JSON string) directly, it parses as JSON → mode is always 'inline'. The Remote demo should default to 'remote' and Delta demo should default to 'delta'.

2. **Modal color scheme**: The dialog opened by `presentDialog` needs the theme passed to it. Looking at the `handleOpenEditor` callback, it passes `theme` from `useColorScheme()`. But the `ColorSchemeProvider` wraps the component, so `useColorScheme()` should work. 

The dialog is a portal though, so it might not inherit the React context tree. I need to check how `presentDialog` actually works. For the first problem, I could either avoid passing inline JSON and create blob URLs instead so the parser detects them as remote or delta, or add a `defaultStorageMode` prop to `ExcalidrawEditRenderer`. The simpler approach would be...

Actually, I realize the initial snapshot format itself determines the mode - remote mode needs a URL that isn't valid JSON, and delta mode needs the `<url>\n<json>` format. So for the BizPage demo, both modes should start with blob URLs pointing to the data. I already fixed `useExcalidrawData` to recognize blob URLs, so I should switch BizPage back to using them for the initial state.

For the modal color scheme issue, the `presentDialog` creates a portal outside the React tree, so the `ColorSchemeProvider` context isn't accessible inside. The dialog receives the `theme` prop directly from `useColorScheme()`, but this value gets captured when the dialog opens, so theme changes while the dialog is open won't propagate.

Now I'm checking whether the portal itself is properly themed. The `ExcalidrawEditorDialogContent` uses the `theme` prop directly rather than relying on context, so if `ColorSchemeProvider` is working in the main component, it should pass the correct theme value to the dialog. But I need to verify that the portal's className is also being themed correctly through `usePortalTheme`. I'm realizing the core issue is that the dialog's own UI elements like the SegmentedControl aren't respecting the theme, even though the `presentDialog` call passes the `theme` prop and `usePortalTheme()` should be applying the correct CSS variables through `portalClassName`. The user is saying the modal stays light-themed even when the app switches to dark mode, so something in the theme propagation chain isn't working as expected.
</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
So the dialog stack already wraps the content with `PortalThemeProvider` using the `portalClassName` and `theme` passed to `presentDialog`. The `theme` prop comes from `useColorScheme()` in the `ExcalidrawEditRenderer`, and `portalClassName` comes from `usePortalTheme()`.

The issue is:
1. `useColorScheme()` returns `'light'` by default (no `ColorSchemeProvider` before my fix - but now I added it)
2. `usePortalTheme()` returns `{ className: '', theme: 'light' }` by default (no `PortalThemeProvider`)


3. The dialog stack creates its own `PortalThemeProvider` with the theme and className from `presentDialog`, so the dialog's internal content should get the correct theme. The `data-theme` attribute is also set on the popup div, so with the `ColorSchemeProvider` I added, the dialog should now work correctly.

But I'm realizing the `portalClassName` is still empty, and that's the issue — it provides the CSS variables for the dialog's content styling. Without it, the rich editor inside the dialog won't have the proper theme colors and styling. The CSS variables like `vars.color.bg` and `vars.color.border` are defined through the Vanilla Extract theme contract, not the `data-theme` attribute, so without the proper variant class applied to `PortalThemeProvider`, the dialog defaults to light theme values. I need to figure out which variant class to pass as `portalClassName` to get the correct theme variables, but the demo doesn't use the variant system itself. The issue is that the modal's color scheme isn't switching automatically. Looking at the screenshot, the SegmentedControl has a light background while the fullscreen dialog is using undefined theme variables. The cleanest solution is to wrap the excalidraw section with `PortalThemeProvider`, passing it a variant class and the current theme so that `usePortalTheme()` inside the renderer gets the proper values. I'm importing the necessary utilities and setting this up.

Setting up the provider wrapper...
</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-02 14:32:52Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The user says that after saving, both demos render blank data. Let me think about what's happening:

1. Initial state: blob URL pointing to JSON data → `useExcalidrawData` recognizes `blob:` URL → fetches it → renders correctly
2. User edits → saves → `onSnapshotChange` is called with a new value
3. The new value depends on the storage mode:
   - Remote mode: `saveSnapshot(doc)` returns a new blob URL → `onSnapshotChange(blobUrl)` → should work
   - Inline mode: `JSON.stringify(doc)` → `onSnapshotChange(jsonString)` → should work
   - Delta mode: `saveSnapshot(doc)` returns a blob URL, then `onSave` gets called with either the ref or ref plus delta

The problem is that when the editor saves in remote mode, it calls `saveSnapshot(doc)` to get a blob URL, then passes that to `onSave`, which updates the snapshot. But the parent component re-renders with the new snapshot prop, and something's breaking in how the data flows back to the renderer.

Let me trace through the save flow more carefully. When `performSave()` runs in remote mode, it creates a blob URL from the document and passes it to the callback. The mock implementation generates a blob URL, but I need to check if that URL is actually persisting or getting revoked somewhere.

Looking at the `onClose` callback, it updates the initial data reference and increments the preview key, which triggers a component remount. The issue might be that the blob URL created during save isn't accessible when the new component instance tries to fetch it after the dialog closes. I need to trace through the save flow more carefully. When the user saves, `mockSaveSnapshot` creates a blob URL and returns it, which triggers a parent re-render with a new key based on that URL. This forces the entire `ExcalidrawEditRenderer` to remount, so it should fetch fresh data from the blob URL rather than relying on stale `initialDataRef`.

But there's a timing issue: the dialog is still open during this remount, and the refs that the dialog uses (`onSnapshotChangeRef`, `saveSnapshotRef`) get recreated. The dialog's closure over the old refs means subsequent saves might be calling stale functions or the new component instance isn't properly connected to the dialog's event handlers.

The real fix is to keep the key stable instead of tying it to the snapshot value. Since `previewKey` inside the component already handles updates through the `onClose` callback, the outer key shouldn't change based on snapshot state.
</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-02 14:37:30Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
I can see the issue from the screenshot. The `excalidrawEditorContainer` uses `vars.color.border` which comes from the Vanilla Extract theme contract. This means the border color is determined by the Vanilla Extract CSS variable `--rc-border` (as shown in the screenshot: `border: 1px solid var(--rc-border)`).

The issue is that `--rc-border` is not being set for dark mode. This CSS variable comes from the Vanilla Extract theme system, which requires a theme class to be applied on an ancestor element.

Looking at the screenshot:
- The element has `styles_excalidrawEditorContainer__17vk87t1`


- The `[data-theme='dark']` rule sets `--demo-border` but not `--rc-border`, and the `--rc-*` variables only get defined when a variant class from the theme contract is applied. The `PortalThemeProvider` applies these variant classes, but it's only used inside dialog portals, not on the main editor container.

For the inline preview to access the theme variables, I need to apply the variant class directly to the wrapper element around the `ExcalidrawEditRenderer` in BizPage.
</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-02 14:37:31Z)**_

截图说明 `--rc-border` 等 Vanilla Extract CSS vars 未生效。`PortalThemeProvider` 仅提供 context，不渲染 DOM。需在容器 div 上加 variant class 使 CSS vars 生效。

```text
     1→import type { CSSProperties } from 'react'
     2→
     3→import { vars } from './vars.css'
     4→
     5→type VarsContract = typeof vars
     6→
     7→export type ThemeTokens = {
     8→  [K in keyof VarsContract]?: Partial<Record<keyof VarsContract[K], string>>
     9→}
    10→
    11→function extractVarName(cssVarRef: string): string {
    12→  const match = cssVarRef.match(/^var\((.+)\)$/)
    13→  return match ? match[1] : cssVarRef
    14→}
    15→
    16→export function createThemeStyle(tokens: ThemeTokens): CSSProperties {
    17→  const style: Record<string, string> = {}
    18→
    19→  for (const [group, values] of Object.entries(tokens)) {
    20→    if (!values) continue
    21→    const contractGroup = vars[group as keyof VarsContract] as Record<
    22→      string,
    23→      string
    24→    >
    25→    if (!contractGroup) continue
    26→    for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(values)) {
    27→      if (value == null) continue
    28→      const cssVarRef = contractGroup[key]
    29→      if (cssVarRef) {
    30→        style[extractVarName(cssVarRef)] = value
    31→      }
    32→    }
    33→  }
    34→
    35→  return style as CSSProperties
    36→}
    37→
```
</details></tool-use>

OK, my `className={getVariantClass('article')}` edits are in place. Now let me think about the two remaining issues:

1. **Dark mode CSS variables not working**: The variant class + `data-theme="dark"` should provide the dark CSS variables. The dark override selector is `[data-theme="dark"].${articleTheme}`. If this isn't working, maybe the demo's pre-built CSS and Vite-processed CSS have different class names.

Actually, let me check a different angle. The demo imports `@haklex/rich-kit-shiro/style.css`. This CSS file already contains the dark overrides. 

But I'm realizing the pre-built CSS file might have different class names than what Vite generates at dev time. The demo imports the static `style.css`, while Vite's Vanilla Extract plugin processes `.css.ts` files separately at dev time, so there could be two competing sets of theme CSS. But when `getVariantClass('article')` is called, it returns the class name from Vanilla Extract's dev processing, which should already have the associated CSS including dark overrides baked in.

Actually, I need to reconsider—`@haklex/rich-style-token` is a workspace package that might be published as built output. If the import resolves to `.mjs` files instead of the source `.css.ts` files, then Vanilla Extract's Vite plugin won't process `vars.css.ts` at all, which means the dark overrides might not be included. Let me check what the package actually exports.

For the delta mode issue, the storage mode detection relies on the `initialSnapshot` format. Since both demos start with blob URLs (remote format), the delta demo defaults to 'remote' mode. To default to 'delta', the snapshot would need to be in delta format (`url\njson_delta`), but that's problematic because there's no base snapshot yet—a delta only exists after the first save. The user would need to save in remote mode first before switching to delta mode, but they probably expect delta mode to be selected from the start. I'm looking at the storage mode detection logic to understand how to handle this.

The detection checks if the `initialSnapshot` is valid JSON (inline), then checks if it's a multi-line string with content after the first line (delta), otherwise defaults to remote. Since blob URLs are single-line strings that fail JSON parsing, they always return 'remote'. To make the delta demo start in delta mode, I'd need to add a `defaultStorageMode` prop to the `ExcalidrawEditRenderer` component.

Actually, there's a simpler workaround: instead of modifying the component API, I can format the snapshot in delta format on the BizPage side. Delta format expects `url\njson_delta`, so I'd pass the blob URL followed by a newline and an empty delta object to trigger the multi-line detection.

Formatting the snapshot as delta... So this approach should work—the blob URL with the empty delta object will be parsed correctly as incremental mode, and the dialog will detect it as delta storage. Now I need to implement both fixes, and for the dark mode issue, I should verify that the CSS variables are actually being applied. The pre-built stylesheet might have different class names for dark overrides, so I need to ensure Vite processes the dark mode variables from `vars.css.ts` properly.
</details></think>
